[F16D 1/096] the ring or rings being located between the shaft and the hub -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in F16D1/096 .
[F16D 1/097] with clamping effected by ring expansion only, e.g. with an expanded ring located between hub and shaft -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Used for attachment of a member on a shaft or on a shaft-end with clamping hub, with radial clamping due to axial loading of at least one pair of conical surfaces, using one of more elastic segmented conical rings forming at least one of the conical surfaces, the rings being expanded to effect clamping, with clamping effected by ring expansion only, e.g. used for the following arrangements: see figure (the elastic ring 45 is expanded between the shaft part 13 and the hub 3 when screwed on the shaft end 14):
[F16D 1/10] Quick-acting couplings in which the parts are connected by simply bringing them together axially -- Quick-acting couplings of this type achieve a secure joint by simply sliding the components together along a common axis, allowing rapid connection without additional rotations or movements. They are used where fast, reliable transmission of torque is needed, often in hydraulic, pneumatic or mechanical power lines.
[F16D 11/00] Clutches with mechanically-actuated clutching members -- The F16D 11/00 class covers clutches whose engaging and disengaging mechanisms are driven purely by mechanical means such as levers, cams, gears, springs or linkages, rather than hydraulic or electrical actuators. It includes friction, dog or multi-plate clutches where the clutching members are moved directly by these mechanical devices.
[F16D 1/101] without axial retaining means rotating with the coupling -- F16D 1/101 covers rotating couplings that transmit torque without any axial retaining devices such as pins, keys or splines; the joined shafts are held together solely by the coupling's own friction, interference fit or other non-axial means, allowing rotation while preventing axial separation. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering couplings for transmitting rotation.
[F16D 11/02] disengaged by a contact of a part mounted on the clutch with a stationarily-mounted member -- F16D 11/02 classifies clutches whose disengagement is achieved by a component carried on the rotating side pressing against a stationary member, thereby separating the clutching elements. The mechanism is mechanically actuated, using the contact to interrupt power transmission.
[F16D 1/104] having retaining means rotating with the coupling and acting only by friction -- A coupling characterized by retaining elements that rotate together with the coupling and transmit torque solely through frictional contact, without interlocking or positive engagement. This design falls within the mechanical engineering subgroup for couplings used to transmit rotation.
[F16D 11/04] with clutching members movable only axially -- F16D 11/04 designates clutches in which the clutching members can be moved only in the axial direction to engage or disengage the drive. The mechanically-actuated elements slide along the shaft axis rather than rotating or shifting radially, providing axial control of torque transmission in mechanical power-train applications.
[F16D 11/06] with clutching members movable otherwise than only axially, e.g. rotatable keys -- F16D 11/06 classifies clutches whose engaging members move by means other than pure axial displacement, such as using rotatable keys or other non-axial mechanisms to transmit rotational motion.
[F16D 1/108] having retaining means rotating with the coupling and acting by interengaging parts, i.e. positive coupling -- F16D 1/108 relates to mechanical couplings that include retaining elements which rotate together with the coupling and transmit motion through interlocking parts, providing a positive, non-slipping connection between shafts.
[F16D 11/08] actuated by moving a non-rotating part axially -- F16D 11/08 classifies clutches whose clutching members are engaged or released by moving a non-rotating component in the axial direction. The axial displacement of the stationary part causes the clutch members to mesh or separate, providing mechanical control of torque transmission. This falls under mechanical engineering couplings for transmitting rotation.
[F16D 11/10] with clutching members movable only axially -- This CPC subclass covers clutches whose engaging members can only move axially, meaning the clutching elements are displaced linearly along the shaft to engage or disengage rotation. It falls under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, couplings for transmitting rotation, and clutches with mechanically-actuated clutching members.
[F16D 1/112] the interengaging parts comprising torque-transmitting surfaces, e.g. bayonet joints -- The subclass addresses the interengaging components of a coupling that form the torque-transmitting surfaces, such as bayonet-type joints used to lock rotating shafts together.
[F16D 11/12] with clutching members movable otherwise than only axially -- This subclass pertains to clutches whose engaging members are driven by motions other than pure axial translation, such as radial, rotary or angular displacement, allowing engagement and disengagement through alternative movement paths. It falls under mechanical-engineering couplings for transmitting rotation.
[F16D 11/14] with clutching members movable only axially -- This subgroup covers clutches whose engaging members move only along the axis of rotation, usually by sliding or axial displacement of plates, studs or similar parts. It includes designs where torque is transmitted through linear, not radial or rotational, motion of the clutching elements and is applied in mechanical systems that require axial actuation.
[F16D 1/116] the interengaging parts including a continuous or interrupted circumferential groove in the surface of one of the coupling parts -- F16D 1/116 covers couplings in which the mating elements engage by means of a continuous or interrupted groove cut around the circumference of one part, allowing a corresponding tongue, bead or spline on the other part to lock into the groove. The groove provides axial positioning and torque transmission while permitting easy assembly and disassembly.
[F16D 11/16] with clutching members movable otherwise than only axially -- The code F16D 11/16 refers to clutches whose clutching elements are driven by mechanisms other than simple axial motion, employing radial, rotary or combined movements to engage or disengage the drive. This falls under mechanical engineering couplings for transmitting rotation, specifically within the group of mechanically-actuated clutching members.
[F16D 1/12] allowing adjustment of the parts about the axis -- This subgroup covers couplings in which the components can be angularly adjusted relative to each other about the transmission axis, allowing fine tuning of alignment or torque transmission. It includes mechanisms such as set-screws, clamping devices or sliding sleeves that permit rotation of parts while maintaining the connection.
[F16D 13/00] Friction clutches -- The CPC subclass F16D 13/00 covers friction clutches, devices that transmit torque through frictional contact between rotating members, typically used in automotive and industrial applications. It includes designs such as disc, drum and cone clutches and related components for controlling rotational speed and torque.
[F16D 13/02] disengaged by the contact of a part mounted on the clutch with a stationarily-mounted member -- F16D 13/02 covers friction clutches that are disengaged when a part mounted on the rotating assembly contacts a stationary member, interrupting torque transmission. The contact element-often a pressure plate, latch or similar device-presses against the fixed surface to release the clutch.
[F16D 13/025] with a helical band or equivalent member with two or more turns embracing a drum or the like  electromagnetically actuated  F16D27/105 -- F16D 13/025 covers friction clutches that use a helical band or similar element formed in two or more turns that encircle a drum or comparable member, where the band is driven to engage or disengage by electromagnetic actuation, enabling controlled transmission of rotational power.
[F16D 13/04] with means for actuating or keeping engaged by a force derived at least partially from one of the shafts to be connected -- F16D 13/04 covers friction clutches that include a mechanism to actuate or retain engagement using a force taken, at least in part, from one of the shafts being coupled, such as torque-derived or axial forces generated by that shaft. This sub-class falls under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, couplings for transmitting rotation, and specifically friction clutches.
[F16D 13/06] with clutching members movable otherwise than only axially -- F16D 13/06 covers friction clutches whose engaging components can shift by means other than pure axial motion, such as radial, angular or combined movements, allowing engagement/disengagement through sliding, pivoting or tilting actions. These designs are used where space constraints or specific torque transmission characteristics require alternative actuation mechanisms.
[F16D 13/08] with a helical band or equivalent member, which may be built up from linked parts, with more than one turn embracing a drum or the like, with or without an additional clutch actuating the end of the band -- This CPC subclass covers friction clutches that use a helical band or equivalent element, which may be assembled from linked parts and makes more than one wrap around a drum or similar component. The design may include an additional mechanism to actuate the band's free end.
[F16D 13/10] with clutching members co-operating with the periphery of a drum, a wheel-rim, or the like -- F16D 13/10 covers friction clutches whose engaging parts work against the outer surface of a rotating drum, wheel rim or similar peripheral element, allowing torque transmission by clutching members that press onto the drum or rim. This sub-group is part of the broader category of couplings for transmitting rotation within mechanical engineering.
[F16D 13/12] with an expansible band or coil co-operating with the inner surface of a drum or the like -- This subclass covers friction clutches in which an expandable band or coil engages the inner surface of a drum or similar member to transmit rotation. The band is designed to expand radially to tighten around the drum for engagement and retract for release, as used in band brakes and some automotive clutches.
[F16D 13/14] with outwardly-movable clutching members co-operating with the inner surface of a drum or the like -- F16D 13/14 classifies friction clutches whose clutching elements can move outwardly to engage the inner surface of a drum or similar component, transmitting rotational motion between shafts. These devices are used in mechanical engineering for coupling parts where a spring-loaded or hydraulically actuated member contacts the drum interior to transmit torque.
[F16D 13/16] shaped as radially-movable segments -- The CPC code F16D 13/16 covers friction clutches whose friction elements are shaped as radially-moveable segments that can shift outward or inward to engage or disengage torque transmission. These segment-type members are commonly used in automotive and industrial couplings where variable contact pressure and rapid response are needed.
[F16D 13/18] shaped as linked or separately-pivoted segments -- This CPC subclass covers friction clutches whose friction surfaces consist of a series of segments that are either linked together or mounted on separate pivots, allowing torque transmission while permitting relative motion. It applies to designs where the clutch elements are articulated as linked or separately-pivoted pieces to achieve specific engagement characteristics.
[F16D 13/20] with clutching members co-operating with both the periphery and the inner surface of a drum or wheel-rim -- F16D 13/20 covers friction clutches whose engaging elements contact both the outer periphery and the inner surface of a drum or wheel-rim, providing torque transmission by simultaneous pressure on the two surfaces. This arrangement belongs to the couplings for transmitting rotation within mechanical engineering.
[F16D 13/22] with axially-movable clutching members -- F16D 13/22 covers friction clutches in which the clutching members are displaced along the axis of rotation to engage or disengage the drive. The axial movement may be produced by springs, hydraulics, or manual actuation, allowing zero-speed start or torque modulation in mechanical power-train applications.
[F16D 13/24] with conical friction surfaces -- F16D 13/24 covers friction clutches that transmit rotation by means of conical friction surfaces. The design uses a male cone engaging a matching female cone, creating a large, self-centering contact area that allows higher torque transmission and smoother engagement. Such clutches are common in automotive, aerospace and industrial power-train applications where compact, reliable torque control is required.
[F16D 13/26] in which the or each axially-movable member is pressed exclusively against an axially-located member -- It covers friction clutches in which each axially-movable member is pressed exclusively against a single axially-located member, providing a simple engagement with one fixed friction surface rather than multiple contacts. The design is used where compact, reliable torque transmission is required without complex spring or cam mechanisms.
[F16D 13/28] with means for increasing the effective force between the actuating sleeve or equivalent member and the pressure member -- This CPC subclass covers friction clutches that incorporate devices to boost the force transmitted from the actuating sleeve or equivalent component to the pressure member, such as spring mechanisms, hydraulic or pneumatic assistance, or gear-driven systems that increase clamping pressure for improved torque transmission.
[F16D 13/30] in which the clutching pressure is produced by springs only -- F16D 13/30 covers friction clutches whose engaging pressure is generated solely by springs, without hydraulic, pneumatic or electromagnetic assistance. The classification falls under mechanical engineering, specifically coupling elements for transmitting rotation, and details spring-actuated clutch mechanisms.
[F16D 13/32] in which two or more axially-movable members are pressed from one side towards an axially-located member -- This subclass covers friction clutches in which two or more movable members are driven toward a stationary axial member by pressure applied from one side, enabling torque transmission through axial compression. The arrangement is used where the driven elements slide axially to engage the clutch.
[F16D 13/34] with means for increasing the effective force between the actuating sleeve or equivalent member and the pressure member -- This subclass covers friction clutches that incorporate mechanisms to boost the force transmitted from the actuating sleeve or equivalent member to the pressure member, such as springs, hydraulic pressure, or cam devices, thereby enhancing clutch engagement. It resides within mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, couplings for transmitting rotation.
[F16D 13/36] in which the clutching pressure is produced by springs only -- F16D 13/36 pertains to friction clutches in which the engaging pressure is supplied only by springs, without hydraulic, pneumatic or electromagnetic assistance. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering → engineering elements and units → couplings for transmitting rotation → friction clutches.
[F16D 13/38] with flat clutching surfaces, e.g. discs -- The CPC subclass F16D 13/38 designates friction clutches that transmit rotation using flat clutching surfaces, typically in the form of discs. It falls under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, couplings for transmitting rotation, and the broader category of friction clutches.
[F16D 13/385] double clutches, i.e. comprising two friction disc mounted on one driven shaft  with two concentric driven shafts  F16D21/06 -- A double-clutch arrangement in which two friction discs are fixed to a single driven shaft and each disc engages with its own concentric driven shaft, providing separate or sequential torque transmission paths within a compact mechanical unit. This design is used where multiple gear-selection or shifting stages are needed in a single coupling.
[F16D 13/40] in which the or each axially-movable member is pressed exclusively against an axially-located member -- This subclass of friction clutches covers designs where each axially-movable clutch element is forced solely against a stationary member that is positioned along the same axis, providing engagement through axial compression. Such configurations typically use springs or hydraulic pressure to maintain the axial contact and transmit torque when the clutch is engaged.
[F16D 13/42] with means for increasing the effective force between the actuating sleeve or equivalent member and the pressure member -- F16D 13/42 covers friction clutches that incorporate features to boost the force applied between the actuating sleeve-or a comparable component-and the pressure member, such as springs, hydraulic devices, magnetic or pneumatic mechanisms that enhance clutch engagement pressure.
[F16D 13/44] in which the clutching pressure is produced by springs only -- F16D 13/44 pertains to friction clutches where the engaging force is generated exclusively by springs, ensuring reliable and consistent operation under various mechanical conditions. This classification is part of a detailed system used by the European Patent Office and United States Patent and Trademark Office for categorizing technical inventions.
[F16D 13/46] in which two axially-movable members, of which one is attached to the driving side and the other to the driven side, are pressed from one side towards an axially-located member -- The subclass covers friction clutches where two axially-movable members, one connected to the driving side and the other to the driven side, are pressed from one side toward a centrally positioned member, enabling torque transmission through axial pressure.
[F16D 13/48] with means for increasing the effective force between the actuating sleeve or equivalent member and the pressure member -- Friction clutches that incorporate devices - such as springs, hydraulic pistons, pneumatic chambers or mechanical levers - to increase the effective force applied by the actuating sleeve or equivalent member onto the pressure member, thereby enhancing engagement pressure and torque transmission.
[F16D 13/50] in which the clutching pressure is produced by springs only -- This subclass covers friction clutches whose engaging pressure is generated solely by springs, without hydraulic, pneumatic or other actuation. It focuses on spring-loaded mechanisms that transmit rotation by using spring tension to press the clutching surfaces together.
[F16D 13/505] Devices located between the flywheel and the driven disc, and biassing the driven disc away from the flywheel towards the disengaged position -- F16D 13/505 pertains to devices positioned between the flywheel and the driven disc of a friction clutch that bias the driven disc away from the flywheel toward the disengaged position, often using springs, hydraulic pressure or similar means. The classification groups mechanisms that keep the clutch open until torque transmission is required.
[F16D 13/52] Clutches with multiple lamellae -- F16D 13/52 denotes friction clutches that use a stack of thin, disc-like lamellae to transmit rotational power. The lamellae are arranged so that friction surfaces are brought together or separated, allowing the clutch to engage or release torque smoothly.
[F16D 13/54] with means for increasing the effective force between the actuating sleeve or equivalent member and the pressure member -- F16D 13/54 covers friction clutches that incorporate devices or mechanisms to boost the effective force applied between the actuating sleeve (or equivalent component) and the pressure member, enhancing engagement pressure and torque transmission in rotational coupling systems.
[F16D 13/56] in which the clutching pressure is produced by springs only -- F16D 13/56 refers to friction clutches whose engagement force is generated only by springs, without hydraulic, pneumatic or other active pressure sources. The classification covers designs where the clutching pressure is applied solely by the elastic force of the springs.
[F16D 13/58] Details -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Tools for assembling or disassembling clutches -> B25B27/0064
[F16D 13/583] Diaphragm-springs, e.g. Belleville  co-operation with a disengaging thrust ring or bearing  F16D23/14 -- This classification code pertains to friction clutches in mechanical engineering, specifically addressing diaphragm springs, such as Belleville springs, that work in conjunction with a disengaging thrust ring or bearing. It is part of the broader category of couplings for transmitting rotation.
[F16D 13/585] Arrangements or details relating to the mounting or support of the diaphragm on the clutch on the clutch cover or the pressure plate -- The subclass F16D 13/585 covers specific arrangements or details for mounting or supporting the diaphragm of a friction clutch, focusing on how it is attached to the clutch cover or the pressure plate. It addresses design features that secure the diaphragm in place to ensure proper engagement and torque transmission.
[F16D 13/60] Clutching elements -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Friction lining or attachment thereof -> F16D69/00
[F16D 13/62] Clutch-bands -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Brake-bands, brake-shoes, brake-drums -> F16D65/00
[F16D 13/64] Clutch-plates -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Brake-plates, brake-lamellae -> F16D65/12
[F16D 13/644] Hub construction -- F16D 13/644 pertains to the design and construction of hubs in friction clutches, detailing how the hub transmits rotational torque between the driving and driven members. It includes structural features such as splines, keys, bearing integration and related geometry that influence clutch performance, reliability and service life.
[F16D 13/646] Mounting of the discs on the hub -- F16D 13/646 covers arrangements for fixing the friction discs of a rotary clutch to the hub, including bolts, screws, splines, pins, adhesives or other securing means that ensure a stable, axially aligned connection while allowing transmission of torque. This sub-group addresses the design of mounting features that maintain disc position during engagement and disengagement of the clutch.
[F16D 13/648] for clutches with multiple lamellae -- F16D 13/648 designates friction clutches that employ several lamellae, or friction plates, arranged in a stack to transmit torque when engaged. The multiple lamellae increase contact area and allow smoother torque transfer and better heat dissipation, typical in automotive and industrial transmission systems.
[F16D 13/66] of conical shape -- F16D 13/66 covers friction clutches that use a conical shape for their friction surfaces, transmitting torque by axial pressure between mating conical members. The class belongs to mechanical engineering, under engineering elements and units and couplings for transmitting rotation.
[F16D 13/68] Attachments of plates or lamellae to their supports -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - One or more discs connected to the linings transmitting torque to one or more discs connected to the hub by helical springs in windows in the discs, i.e. rotary vibration dampers -> F16F15/12
[F16D 13/683] for clutches with multiple lamellae -- F16D 13/683 designates friction clutches that use multiple lamellae or friction plates. These multi-lamella clutches are arranged to transmit torque and allow progressive engagement. The subclass is part of mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, couplings for transmitting rotation.
[F16D 13/686] with one or more intermediate members made of rubber or like material transmitting torque from the linings to the hub -- This CPC subclass pertains to friction clutches that incorporate one or more intermediate members of rubber or similar material to convey torque from the clutch linings to the hub. It falls under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, couplings for transmitting rotation.
[F16D 13/69] Arrangements for spreading lamellae in the released state -- The code covers devices that cause the friction-clutch lamellae to separate or spread when the clutch is released, using springs, cams, hydraulic or pneumatic mechanisms or other actuation means to ensure a defined gap between the friction elements.
[F16D 13/70] Pressure members, e.g. pressure plates, for clutch-plates or lamellae -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Clutch flywheels comprising two or more masses with a rotational damper -> F16F15/12
[F16D 13/71] in which the clutching pressure is produced by springs only -- F16D 13/71 covers friction-clutch designs in which the clutching pressure is generated exclusively by springs, without hydraulic, pneumatic or other external force. It pertains to mechanical arrangements where spring-induced force provides the clutching action for transmitting rotation.
[F16D 13/72] Features relating to cooling -- F16D 13/72 pertains to friction clutches and specifically to features that provide cooling for the engaging surfaces, such as cooling passages, fins, airflow channels, or heat-dissipating materials. These elements are designed to remove heat generated during torque transmission and to maintain clutch performance and durability.
[F16D 13/74] Features relating to lubrication -- F16D 13/74 covers lubrication aspects of friction clutches, such as oil supply channels, grease pockets, or related sealing and lubrication delivery mechanisms designed to reduce wear and improve torque transfer in rotating couplings.
[F16D 13/75] Features relating to adjustment, e.g. slack adjusters -- F16D 13/75 pertains to friction clutches and includes the design of adjustment mechanisms that control clutch clearance and engagement, such as slack adjusters. It covers features that allow fine-tuning of clutch play to ensure consistent torque transmission and reliable operation.
[F16D 13/752] the adjusting device being located in the actuating mechanism arranged outside the clutch  adjusting "Bowden" mechanisms  F16C1/22 -- This subclass covers friction clutches whose gap-adjusting device is placed in the actuating mechanism rather than inside the clutch housing, using Bowden-type cable or rod arrangements to transmit the adjusting force. It relates to mechanical engineering couplings that transmit rotation and includes designs where the Bowden element is positioned externally to the clutch assembly.
[F16D 13/755] the adjusting device being located in or near the release bearing -- F16D 13/755 covers friction-clutch designs in which the mechanism that adjusts clutch engagement is placed directly in, or adjacent to, the release bearing that separates the driving and driven members, enabling precise control of the clutch's operating clearance.
[F16D 13/757] the adjusting device being located on or inside the clutch cover, e.g. acting on the diaphragm or on the pressure plate -- The classification covers friction clutches whose adjusting device is mounted on or inside the clutch cover, such as mechanisms that act on the diaphragm spring or the pressure plate to fine-tune the clamping force and improve engagement control.
[F16D 13/76] specially adapted to incorporate with other transmission parts, i.e. at least one of the clutch parts also having another function, e.g. being the disc of a pulley -- F16D 13/76 designates friction clutches specially adapted to be combined with other transmission parts, whereby at least one clutch component also serves a secondary function such as acting as the disc of a pulley.
[F16D 15/00] Clutches with wedging balls or rollers or with other wedgeable separate clutching members -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Freewheels, freewheel clutches -> F16D41/00
[F16D 17/00] Clutches in which the drive is transmitted solely by virtue of the eccentricity of the contacting surfaces of clutch members which fit one around the other -- This CPC subclass covers clutches that transmit torque solely through the eccentric offset of the mating surfaces, where one clutch member fits around the other and engagement is achieved by geometric eccentricity rather than friction or other means. It is located under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, couplings for transmitting rotation.
[F16D 19/00] Clutches with mechanically-actuated clutching members not otherwise provided for -- Clutches whose engaging or disengaging members are moved by a mechanical actuation mechanism that is not covered by more specific subclasses. This includes devices where levers, cams, screws, gears or other mechanical linkages drive the clutch plates or friction elements to transmit or interrupt rotational power.
[F16D 2001/062] characterised by adaptors where hub bores being larger than the shaft -- The CPC entry F16D 2001/062 covers transmission or drive devices that incorporate adaptors whose hub bore is larger than the shaft they receive. These adaptors provide a clearance fit, allowing for easier assembly, axial movement or misalignment compensation in couplings, gearboxes and similar mechanical power-transfer units.
[F16D 2001/0903] the clamped shaft being hollow -- In pump constructions covered by CPC F16D, a hollow shaft that is secured by a clamp is recognised as a distinct feature. The design lets fluid or other media pass through the shaft while the clamping mechanism provides axial support and maintains alignment, often reducing weight and enabling integrated routing of lines. This entry applies to any pump where a clamped, hollow shaft is employed as part of the drive or discharge system.
[F16D 2001/0906] using a hydraulic fluid to clamp or disconnect, not provided for in  F16D1/091 -- This CPC subgroup covers hydraulic systems that use fluid pressure to provide a clamping or disconnect function not specifically defined in the earlier F16D1/091 category. It includes mechanisms where hydraulic fluid creates a holding or separating force in machinery, extending the range of hydraulic actuation beyond the standard clamp types.
[F16D 2001/0945] using multiple pairs of elastic or segmented rings to effect clamping -- The CPC entry F16D 2001/0945 covers clamping devices that employ two or more pairs of elastic or segmented rings to secure a workpiece. The rings are arranged so that their mutual compression provides a reliable, adjustable grip, typically for pipes, hoses or similar cylindrical members.
[F16D 2001/0955] the clamping is effected by hub contraction, i.e. a compression of the hub instead of the ring -- The CPC subclass F16D 2001/0955 covers clamping mechanisms where the gripping force is generated by contraction of a hub surrounding the workpiece, rather than by tightening a surrounding ring. This approach uses a compressible hub to achieve secure holding in devices such as clamps, fixtures, and tongs.
[F16D 2001/102] the torque is transmitted via polygon shaped connections -- This subclass relates to positive-displacement pumps where torque is transferred between shafts by polygon-shaped elements such as hexagonal or square hubs, cams or gears that engage matching faces, providing a positive mechanical lock that enables precise control of pump displacement.
[F16D 2001/103] the torque is transmitted via splined connections -- F16D 2001/103 relates to power-transmission devices in which torque is conveyed through splined connections. It covers mechanisms such as shafts, gears or hubs that use interlocking splines to transmit rotational force efficiently while allowing axial movement or easy assembly. This subclass falls under the broader group for fluid-power, pneumatic and hydraulic drives.
[F16D 2003/2023] with linear rolling bearings between raceway and trunnion mounted shoes -- This CPC subclass covers machines that incorporate linear rolling bearings positioned between a raceway and shoes that are mounted on a trunnion. It specifies the use of rollers to separate the shoe from the track, allowing smooth linear guidance or support of moving parts. The classification applies to devices where the bearing arrangement is integral to the shoe-trunnion assembly.
[F16D 2003/2026] with trunnion rings, i.e. with tripod joints having rollers supported by a ring on the trunnion -- This subgroup covers tripod joints in which the trunnion is equipped with a surrounding ring that carries rollers, allowing the joint to swivel while the rollers ride on the ring for smoother motion and load distribution.

[F16D 2003/745] Tyre type coupling, i.e. bellows with only one fold -- F16D 2003/745 pertains to tyre-type couplings, which are bellows-style flexible connectors that transmit torque while accommodating axial movement. The classification is limited to designs that have a single fold in the bellows, distinguishing them from multi-fold couplings used in power transmission and machinery.
[F16D 2003/846] Venting arrangements for flexible seals, e.g. ventilation holes -- F16D 2003/846 covers arrangements that provide venting for flexible seals, such as ventilation holes or channels that allow fluid or gas to escape and prevent pressure buildup on the seal. The entry includes designs used in hydraulic, pneumatic or other fluid-handling systems where seal integrity must be maintained while allowing pressure equalisation.
[F16D 2011/002] using an external and axially slidable sleeve for coupling the teeth of both coupling components together -- The entry describes a coupling method in which an external sleeve that can slide axially is used to engage the teeth of the two mating coupling components, providing a secure lock for power transmission. It is classified under CPC F16D, which covers couplings, clutches and related mechanical power transmission devices.
[F16D 2011/004] using an internal or intermediate axially slidable sleeve, coupling both components together, whereby the intermediate sleeve is arranged internally at least with respect to one of the components -- F16D 2011/004 covers devices that join two parts by means of an internal or intermediate axially slidable sleeve, with the sleeve positioned inside at least one of the components. The sliding sleeve serves as a coupling element that enables assembly, disassembly or adjustment while maintaining alignment.
[F16D 2011/006] Locking or detent means, i.e. means to keep the clutch in engaged condition -- The CPC subclass F16D 2011/006 covers devices that lock or provide a detent to hold a clutch in its engaged position, such as spring-loaded pawls, dog-type elements or other mechanisms that prevent disengagement under load. It applies to clutch systems in gearboxes, transmissions and other power-transfer assemblies where a secure, retained engagement is required.
[F16D 2011/008] characterised by the form of the teeth forming the inter-engaging parts; Details of shape or structure of these teeth -- F16D 2011/008 refers to transmission devices whose distinguishing feature is the form of the inter-engaging teeth, specifically the detailed shape or structure of those teeth, including profile, geometry, and any modifications that affect meshing performance. This subgroup covers inventions that define or improve the tooth shape to achieve particular functional advantages.
[F16D 2013/565] with means for releasing the clutch pressure in case of back torque -- The code refers to transmission devices-specifically clutches-equipped with a mechanism that automatically reduces or releases clutch pressure when reverse (back) torque is detected, protecting the system from overload or damage caused by the opposite driving force.
[F16D 2013/581] Securing means for transportation or shipping -- F16D 2013/581 covers securing means employed in transportation or shipping to prevent movement of loads. It includes devices such as straps, chains, nets, clamps, brackets and integrated locking systems that fasten cargo to vehicles, containers, pallets or trailers during handling, transit or storage.
[F16D 2013/586] the cover housing is formed by diaphragm springs -- F16D 2013/586 covers devices whose protective cover or housing is made from diaphragm springs that simultaneously provide sealing and structural support. The spring-formed cover flexibly deforms under pressure while maintaining enclosure integrity, and is used in hydraulic, pneumatic or other pressure equipment. This subclass groups inventions that employ such spring-based housings as a functional element of the apparatus.
[F16D 2013/588] the diaphragm springs are arranged outside the cover housing -- CPC F16D 2013/588 classifies devices in which diaphragm springs are mounted on the exterior of the cover housing, rather than being enclosed within it. The code is used for mechanical assemblies such as actuators, valves or motor mounts where the spring's position outside the housing influences performance, servicing or sealing.
[F16D 2013/642] with resilient attachment of frictions rings or linings to their supporting discs or plates for allowing limited axial displacement of these rings or linings -- The subclass covers mechanisms that attach friction rings or linings to their supporting discs or plates with a resilient connection that permits a restricted axial movement of the rings or linings, enabling controlled displacement during operation in devices such as seals, clutches, brakes and similar assemblies.
[F16D 2013/703] the pressure plate on the flywheel side is combined with a damper -- This subclass deals with flywheel drives in motor vehicles where the pressure plate on the flywheel side incorporates an integrated damper. The combined component provides clutch engagement while attenuating torsional vibrations and shock loads.
[F16D 2013/706] the axially movable pressure plate is supported by leaf springs -- F16D 2013/706 identifies a hydraulic or pneumatic cylinder element in which the pressure plate that moves axially is mounted on leaf springs, the springs supplying compliant support and aiding the plate's return after actuation while maintaining its alignment within the cylinder.
[F16D 2021/0607] Double clutch with torque input plate in-between the two clutches, i.e. having a central input plate -- The CPC subclass F16D 2021/0607 relates to dual-clutch transmission units that incorporate a central torque input plate positioned between the two clutch packs. The input plate receives drive torque and can transfer it sequentially through either clutch, enabling rapid gear shifts while maintaining power flow.
[F16D 2021/0615] the central input plate is supported by bearings in-between the two clutches -- F16D 2021/0615 covers a fluid-power transmission in which a central input plate is mounted on bearings positioned between two separate clutches, allowing each clutch to engage or disengage independently while the plate transmits torque. This arrangement provides balanced support and reduces wear on the input component.
[F16D 2021/0623] the central input plate having a damper in-between the two clutches -- The CPC entry F16D 2021/0623 pertains to a clutch assembly in which a central input plate is positioned between two clutch units and includes a damping element that isolates the plate from torque variations, thereby reducing shock and wear during engagement or disengagement. This configuration is typically applied in mechanical drive systems requiring smooth power transmission.
[F16D 2021/063] Electric arrangements for clutch control -- F16D 2021/063 covers electric systems that actuate, monitor or regulate a clutch, including sensors, relays, controllers and power electronics used to engage or disengage a clutch in mechanical transmissions such as automotive or industrial drives.
[F16D 2021/0638] Electrically actuated multiple lamellae clutches -- The CPC entry F16D 2021/0638 covers clutch devices that employ a set of multiple lamellae friction plates whose engagement is controlled electrically, allowing rapid and precise transmission of torque between rotating shafts. These clutches are used in automotive, industrial and mechanical systems where electronic control of power flow is required.
[F16D 2021/0646] Electrically actuated clutch with two clutch plates -- F16D 2021/0646 classifies electrically actuated clutches that use two clutch plates to transmit torque. The entry covers designs where an electric motor or solenoid moves one or both plates to engage or disengage the clutch, enabling controlled power transfer in mechanical drive systems.
[F16D 2021/0653] Hydraulic arrangements for clutch control -- F16D 2021/0653 covers hydraulic arrangements used to control clutches, including systems that transmit fluid pressure to engage or disengage clutch elements, related components such as valves, pumps and control circuits, and methods for regulating clutch operation in mechanical transmissions. This subclass falls under the broader CPC group for hydraulic power transmission devices.
[F16D 2021/0661] Hydraulically actuated multiple lamellae clutches -- A hydraulically actuated multiple lamellae clutch comprises a series of interleaved friction plates that are engaged or released by fluid pressure applied to pistons or chambers, allowing precise control of torque transmission in gearboxes or drive systems. The hydraulic actuation enables rapid, repeatable clutch operation and variable load handling without mechanical linkages.
[F16D 2021/0669] Hydraulically actuated clutches with two clutch plates -- F16D 2021/0669 relates to hydraulically actuated clutches that employ two clutch plates, describing mechanisms in which hydraulic pressure moves the plates to engage or disengage torque transmission in vehicles or other machinery.
[F16D 2021/0676] Mechanically actuated multiple lamellae clutches -- A mechanically actuated multiple-lamellae clutch comprises a series of thin, interleaved plates that can be engaged or disengaged by a mechanical drive such as a lever, cam or hydraulic piston. The arrangement allows torque transmission with gradual engagement, high holding capacity and compact design for automotive or industrial power-train applications.
[F16D 2021/0684] Mechanically actuated clutches with two clutch plates -- The CPC entry F16D 2021/0684 concerns mechanically-actuated clutch assemblies that use a pair of clutch plates to transmit torque, commonly found in automotive or industrial gearboxes. It includes designs where engagement or disengagement is achieved by a mechanical linkage, spring or hydraulic force without electronic control.
[F16D 2021/0692] with two clutches arranged axially without radial overlap -- F16D 2021/0692 covers mechanical transmission arrangements that incorporate two clutches positioned on the same rotational axis but separated so that their radial extents do not overlap. The design allows sequential or parallel engagement of the clutches without interference, typically used in gearboxes or variable-speed drives to achieve smooth torque control.
[F16D 2023/0618] Details of blocking mechanism comprising a helical spring loaded element, e.g. ball -- The CPC entry F16D 2023/0618 pertains to blocking mechanisms that employ a helical spring-loaded component, such as a ball, to engage or disengage a barrier. It covers the design of the spring preload, the shape and placement of the ball, and the actuation methods that release the spring force. These features are applicable to doors, gates, valves and similar safety or control devices.
[F16D 2023/0625] Details of members being coupled, e.g. gears -- F16D 2023/0625 covers information on how gear members are coupled, including the geometry, pitch, tooth profile, and arrangement of engaging gears. It is used to classify documents that detail the specific features of gear meshing and the mechanical relationship between coupled gear components.
[F16D 2023/0631] Sliding sleeves; Details thereof -- The CPC subclass F16D 2023/0631 covers sliding sleeves and their detailed features, such as sealing arrangements, guiding means, actuation mechanisms and mounting configurations used in hydraulic or pneumatic cylinders, pistons and related devices. It includes designs where a sleeve moves longitudinally to engage or disengage fluid passages, adjust stroke length or provide variable sealing surfaces.
[F16D 2023/0637] Details relating to the hub member on which the sliding is arranged -- The CPC subclass F16D 2023/0637 pertains to features or information concerning the hub component of a rotating machine where a sliding element is mounted or guided. It covers design details, connections and functional aspects of the hub that support sliding motion, such as bearings, guides or sleeves, in devices like gearboxes, turbines or other rotary assemblies.
[F16D 2023/0643] Synchro friction clutches with flat plates, discs or lamellae -- F16D 2023/0643 covers synchronizing friction clutches that employ flat plates, discs or lamellae as the friction elements. The subclass includes designs and mechanisms that achieve speed matching between driving and driven shafts using these flat-surface components, detailing their construction, operation and relevant variants.
[F16D 2023/065] Means to provide additional axial force for self-energising, e.g. by using torque from the friction clutch -- The CPC subclass F16D 2023/065 concerns devices that generate extra axial force for self-energising operations, typically by converting torque from a friction clutch into an axial load. This additional force is used to enhance sealing, engagement or pressure-building functions in hydraulic, pneumatic or other fluid-power systems.
[F16D 2023/0656] Details of the tooth structure; Arrangements of teeth -- The CPC entry F16D 2023/0656 concerns the geometric features of gear teeth, describing the shape, dimensions and modulii that define individual tooth profiles and the spatial arrangement of multiple teeth on a gear wheel. It is applied to patents dealing with gear design in pneumatic or hydraulic machines.
[F16D 2023/0662] Details relating to special geometry of arrangements of teeth -- F16D covers springs and their components, focusing on the shape and positioning of teeth that engage or lock parts of the spring. The 2023/0662 entry specifies detailed geometric features of tooth arrangements, such as pitch, height, profile and spacing, used to control force transmission, deformation or locking behavior.
[F16D 2023/0668] Details relating to tooth end or tip geometry -- The CPC subclass F16D 2023/0668 deals with specifications of the shape and configuration of the ends or tips of gear teeth. It includes descriptions of gear tooth profiles, tip rounding, chamfering and other geometric features that affect meshing and load distribution. This subclass is used for patents concerning gear design and manufacturing where tooth tip geometry is a key inventive aspect.
[F16D 2023/0675] Details relating to special undercut geometry -- F16D 2023/0675 pertains to hinge or joint components that incorporate a specially shaped undercut, such as recessed or stepped surfaces, to improve load distribution, locking capability, or ease of assembly. The entry covers the geometric design details of these undercuts, including dimensions, angles, and profiles that enable enhanced mechanical performance or specific functional advantages.
[F16D 2023/0681] Double cone synchromesh clutches -- F16D 2023/0681 relates to double-cone synchromesh clutches, a synchronizing device in gearboxes that uses two concentric friction cones to match the rotational speeds of meshing gears before engagement. The design improves torque transmission and reduces wear compared with single-cone systems, and is commonly applied in automotive manual transmissions and other machinery requiring smooth gear shifts.
[F16D 2023/0687] Clutches with electrical actuation -- The CPC subclass F16D 2023/0687 covers clutches whose engagement or disengagement is controlled by an electrical actuation system, such as solenoids, electric motors, or piezoelectric devices. It includes designs where electric power is used to move the clutch elements, transmit torque, or modulate friction, typically in transmission or power-train applications.
[F16D 2023/0693] Clutches with hydraulic actuation -- F16D 2023/0693 relates to clutches whose engagement or disengagement is controlled by hydraulic pressure. The subclass covers mechanisms that use fluid-driven pistons, cylinders or diaphragms to transmit or interrupt torque between rotating shafts, including designs for variable slip, torque multiplication and automated control in automotive, industrial and marine applications.
[F16D 2023/123] Clutch actuation by cams, ramps or ball-screw mechanisms -- The CPC subclass F16D 2023/123 covers clutch actuation mechanisms that use cams, ramps or ball-screw drives to engage or disengage a clutch. It includes designs where linear or rotary motion from these components transmits torque between driving and driven shafts, commonly found in automotive, marine and industrial power-train applications.
[F16D 2023/126] Actuation by rocker lever; Rocker levers therefor -- The CPC subclass F16D 2023/126 covers mechanisms that use a rocker-type lever to provide the actuation force for a valve, pump or similar hydraulic-pneumatic device, as well as the rocker levers themselves that are designed for such actuation. This includes arrangements where the lever's pivoting motion converts manual or powered input into the required movement of the controlled element.
[F16D 2023/141] characterised by using a fork; Details of forks -- F16D 2023/141 covers fasteners, hinges or articulated connections that are specifically characterised by the inclusion of a fork element, detailing the fork's geometry, mounting method and how it engages or guides components in the device.

[F16D 2027/001] Means for electric connection of the coils of the electromagnetic clutches -- The classification covers devices that provide the electrical interface to the coils of electromagnetic clutches, such as connectors, terminals, busbars or wiring assemblies that ensure reliable power supply and control. It includes arrangements for linking one or more coils to a power source and may address sealing, mounting or fitting features.
[F16D 2027/002] Electric or electronic circuits relating to actuation of electromagnetic clutches -- The F16D 2027/002 subgroup covers electric or electronic circuitry that directly drives or controls electromagnetic clutches, including driver circuits, control logic, sensing and feedback devices, and protective or timing arrangements used to engage or release the clutch.
[F16D 2027/005] Details relating to the internal construction of coils or to clutches having more than one coil in the same housing -- This subclass covers the design of multiple coils placed in a single housing, focusing on the internal geometry, winding arrangement and coupling mechanisms that enable several friction or electromagnetic coils to operate together in a clutch or similar device.
[F16D 2027/007] Bias of an armature of an electromagnetic clutch by flexing of substantially flat springs, e.g. leaf springs -- The code F16D 2027/007 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to electromagnetic clutches whose armature is biased by flexing essentially flat springs such as leaf-springs. It addresses designs where the spring force provides the return torque or positioning of the armature when the clutch is disengaged.
[F16D 2027/008] Details relating to the magnetic circuit, or to the shape of the clutch parts to achieve a certain magnetic path -- F16D 2027/008 covers technical information concerning the magnetic circuit of electromagnetic clutches, including the configuration and geometry of the clutch components that define the magnetic flux path. The classification is applied to inventions that specify shaping of the rotor, stator, pole pieces or other parts to obtain a desired magnetic field distribution for improved torque transmission.
[F16D 2037/001] Electric arrangements for clutch control -- F16D 2037/001 covers electric arrangements for clutch control, encompassing electronic circuits, sensors, switches and control units that manage the engagement and disengagement of mechanical clutches in vehicles, industrial equipment or other drive systems. The subclass includes power-supplied actuators, voltage regulation and feedback mechanisms designed to synchronize clutch operation with engine or motor speed.
[F16D 2037/002] characterised by a single substantially axial gap in which the fluid or medium consisting of small particles is arranged -- The CPC group F16D pertains to pumps and their operating principles, especially rotary positive-displacement types. Subgroup 2037/002 specifies designs that have one substantially axial gap through which the fluid or particle-laden medium passes, the gap being arranged to accommodate small particles during pumping. This arrangement defines a particular internal geometry for handling particulate suspensions.
[F16D 2037/004] characterised by multiple substantially axial gaps in which the fluid or medium consisting of small particles is arranged -- The F16D 2037/004 subclass covers devices that feature several substantially axial gaps through which a fluid or medium containing fine particles is conveyed or arranged, typically for handling, transporting or processing such particle-laden streams within mechanical systems.
[F16D 2037/005] characterised by a single substantially radial gap in which the fluid or medium consisting of small particles is arranged -- F16D 2037/005 designates fluid-handling devices that feature one markedly radial opening, through which a fluid or particulate medium is positioned. The radial gap serves to direct flow or particle movement within the component, commonly seen in specialized pumps or separators.
[F16D 2037/007] characterised by multiple substantially radial gaps in which the fluid or medium consisting of small particles is arranged -- The code refers to pumps or fluid-handling devices that incorporate a series of essentially radial gaps which contain a fluid or medium loaded with fine particles. These gaps guide the particulate-laden flow, enabling mixing, transport or separation of the particles within the device.
[F16D 2041/0601] with a sliding bearing or spacer -- This group covers gear units in hydraulic or pneumatic machines that use a sliding bearing or spacer to support rotating components, providing alignment and reduced friction in pumps, motors or similar power-transmission devices.
[F16D 2041/0603] Sprag details -- F16D 2041/0603 covers the detailed features of sprags used in one-way clutch or brake devices, such as their shape, dimensions, surface characteristics, and mounting arrangements. It pertains to the design elements that enable the sprag to engage, lock or release rotational motion in a controlled manner.
[F16D 2041/0605] Spring details -- F16D 2041/0605 covers the design and structural features of individual spring components, such as hooks, bends, lugs, stops, retainers, and other detailing that define a spring's shape, mounting or engagement points, and functional characteristics within a mechanical assembly. It includes specifications of these features for production, installation and performance optimisation of springs used in various devices.
[F16D 2041/0606] the intermediate coupling members having parts wedging by movement other than pivoting or rolling but combined with pivoting or rolling parts, e.g. shoes on pivot bars or on rollers -- This subclass covers intermediate coupling members that include wedging components that move by sliding, without pivoting or rolling, which are used together with pivoting or rolling parts such as shoes mounted on pivot-bars or rollers. The classification falls under CPC group F16D for mechanisms that convert motion, specifically couplings.
[F16D 2041/0608] Races with a regular polygon shape -- Covers centrifugal pump casings or races shaped as regular polygons, such as hexagonal, octagonal or other multi-sided forms, used to house the impeller and guide fluid flow. The geometry can improve mounting, reduce material waste, or influence flow characteristics.
[F16D 2041/0643] the intermediate coupling members being of more than one size -- F16D 2041/0643 covers power-transmission couplings where the intermediate members that link the driving and driven parts are provided in more than one size, allowing the coupling to accommodate different shaft diameters or torque capacities within a single design. This classification is used for devices such as flexible or universal joints that incorporate multiple-size intermediate pieces to achieve adaptable mechanical connections.
[F16D 2041/0646] the intermediate coupling members moving between recesses in an inner race and recesses in an outer race -- This CPC subclass covers mechanisms where an intermediate coupling element moves between matching recesses in an inner race and an outer race, providing a link for torque transmission or positional adjustment in rotating assemblies such as gears or bearings. The classification applies to designs that use the intermediate member to engage the races sequentially.
[F16D 2041/0665] characterised by there being no cage other than the inner and outer race for distributing the intermediate members -- This subclass covers rolling-element bearings in which the inner and outer races themselves serve to space the intermediate rollers or balls, with no separate cage component. It applies to designs where the cage function is achieved solely by the geometry of the races.
[F16D 2043/145] the centrifugal masses being pivoting -- The CPC entry F16D 2043/145 covers centrifugal devices in which the rotating masses are mounted on pivots so they can swing outward during operation, a feature used in swing-type separators, balancing assemblies and vibration-absorbing mechanisms.
[F16D 2048/0203] with an accumulator; Details thereof -- F16D 2048/0203 pertains to pump constructions that incorporate an accumulator, detailing the design, arrangement and operation of the accumulator-integrated system to regulate pressure surges and improve flow stability. It covers specific technical features, connection methods and functional aspects of such pumps.

[F16D 2048/0218] Reservoirs for clutch control systems; Details thereof -- The code F16D 2048/0218 refers to devices that store fluid or gas used to operate a clutch, such as hydraulic or pneumatic reservoirs, and the specific design features of those stores. It covers the shape, mounting, venting, filling arrangements and other details that influence how the reservoir supplies pressure to the clutch control system.
