[F16D 2048/045] Vacuum boosters therefor -- F16D 2048/045 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to vacuum boosters used to assist the operation of valves or other actuating devices. The entry covers designs, mechanisms and applications that employ a vacuum source to increase pressure differentials and improve actuation performance.
[F16D 2051/001] Parts or details of drum brakes -- The CPC subclass F16D 2051/001 covers individual components and specific details of drum brake systems, including drums, brake shoes, adjusters, springs, and related hardware used to assemble or service the braking mechanism of vehicles.
[F16D 2051/003] Brake supports -- F16D 2051/003 covers the structural elements that hold or mount braking components in vehicles or machinery. It includes brackets, carriers, and other support members designed to secure drums, discs, shoes or calipers to a chassis, axle or frame, ensuring proper alignment and load transfer during operation.
[F16D 2051/005] Protective covers -- F16D 2051/005 covers protective covers designed to shield mechanical or plant equipment, such as machine housings, gearboxes, or moving parts, from environmental influences, dust, moisture, or accidental contact. The covers may be removable, hinged, or fitted with sealing features to facilitate maintenance while maintaining protection during operation. This subclass falls under the broader category of protective devices for machines and plant in the CPC system.
[F16D 2051/006] Braking members arranged axially spaced, e.g. side by side -- This CPC subgroup covers brake components that are positioned at different axial locations along a shaft, such as two or more brake pads or shoes mounted side by side. It includes arrangements where the braking members are spaced longitudinally to provide staged or balanced braking forces.
[F16D 2051/008] Brakes with only one substantially rigid braking member -- F16D 2051/008 refers to braking devices that use a single substantially rigid braking member as the primary friction element. The classification covers designs where a solid pad, shoe or block contacts a moving component to create braking force, often in hydraulic or pneumatic systems.
[F16D 2055/0004] Parts or details of disc brakes -- The code belongs to the F16D main group for braking devices and specifically to sub-group 2055/0004, which covers parts or details of disc brakes such as calipers, rotors, pads, mounting brackets and related hardware. It is applied to inventions that deal with the design, manufacture or improvement of these components for automotive, rail or industrial braking systems.
[F16D 2055/0008] Brake supports -- F16D 2055/0008 covers devices that provide structural support for brake systems, such as brackets, frames, or mounting elements that hold brake cylinders, drums, pads or other components in place. The classification includes arrangements that secure brakes to vehicles, machinery or equipment, ensuring proper alignment and load transfer during operation.
[F16D 2055/0012] integral with vehicle suspension -- F16D 2055/0012 pertains to components that are built into or form part of a vehicle's suspension system, such as springs, dampers, linkages or other elements that are integral rather than added as separate parts. The code is applied to patents where the suspension function is combined with other structural features of the vehicle.
[F16D 2055/0016] Brake calipers -- F16D 2055/0016 classifies brake calipers, the housings that contain pistons and apply force to brake pads to generate friction and slow a vehicle. The code sits within the cooperative patent classification for fluid-actuated braking systems and related components.
[F16D 2055/002] assembled from a plurality of parts -- The subclass concerns gates, valves, doors or similar pipework closures that are constructed by joining several separate components. It includes designs where the functional unit results from assembling a plurality of parts rather than being a single monolithic piece. The classification is used to capture inventions that emphasize the multi-part construction of such devices.
[F16D 2055/0025] comprising a flat frame member -- F16D 2055/0025 covers tanks, containers or similar structures that incorporate a flat frame member as part of their supporting framework. The flat frame is used to provide rigidity, support the walls or serve as a mounting base for additional components. This subclass is applied to designs where the planar frame is an integral element of the container's construction.
[F16D 2055/0029] Retraction devices -- F16D 2055/0029 covers retraction devices, which are mechanisms designed to withdraw, pull back or retract a tool, drill head, cutter or other working element relative to a workpiece or support structure. The classification applies to hydraulic, pneumatic, mechanical or combined systems used in drilling, mining, construction and related fields.
[F16D 2055/0033] Fully-enclosing housings -- F16D 2055/0033 classifies valve assemblies whose housings completely surround the internal components, providing a sealed, protective enclosure that isolates the valve mechanisms from the external environment and often includes integrated mounting or connection features. This subclass covers designs where the housing fully encloses the valve body, passages and actuating parts to ensure containment of fluids, pressure, or contaminants.
[F16D 2055/0037] Protective covers -- The CPC class F16D 2055/0037 covers protective covers designed for pump assemblies and related components, intended to shield moving parts from contaminants, debris, or accidental contact. It includes removable or fixed housings, guards and enclosures that maintain proper operation and safety of the pump system.
[F16D 2055/0041] Resilient elements interposed directly between the actuating member and the brake support, e.g. anti-rattle springs -- The subclass F16D 2055/0041 covers brake arrangements that insert a resilient element, such as an anti-rattle spring, directly between the actuating member (lever, piston or push-rod) and the brake support or backing plate. The component absorbs shock, prevents vibration and maintains consistent brake engagement.
[F16D 2055/0045] Braking members arranged non-symmetrically with respect to the brake disc -- This subgroup covers brake assemblies in which the friction elements such as pads or shoes are positioned asymmetrically around the rotating disc rather than being evenly spaced, influencing heat distribution, wear or performance.
[F16D 2055/005] Brakes straddling an annular brake disc radially internally -- This subclass covers brake devices where the brake assembly encircles a circular disc from the inside and applies a radial braking force. The disc is annular and the brake members press inwardly against its inner surface, commonly used in internal-disc or drum-like brakes for rotating shafts or wheels.
[F16D 2055/0054] Brakes located in the radial gap between two coplanarly arranged annular brake discs -- A brake arrangement in which two annular brake discs are positioned coplanar, creating a radial gap between them, and the braking mechanism is mounted within that gap to apply frictional force. This configuration belongs to the CPC subgroup F16D 2055/0054, covering brake devices that use a radial gap between concentric discs.
[F16D 2055/0058] Fully lined, i.e. braking surface extending over the entire disc circumference -- The code designates disc brake rotors whose friction material is applied around the whole periphery, forming a continuous braking surface. Such fully-lined discs provide uniform grip and heat dissipation, improving brake performance and wear consistency in automotive and industrial applications.
[F16D 2055/0062] Partly lined, i.e. braking surface extending over only a part of the disc circumference -- The entry covers brake discs whose friction material is applied to only a segment of the disc rim instead of the full 360°. Such partly lined discs provide braking on a limited arc of the circumference, typical for specialized or lightweight braking systems.
[F16D 2055/0066] Brakes having more than one actuator on the same side of the disc -- This subclass covers braking systems that employ two or more actuators mounted on the same side of a rotating disc, such as dual-piston calipers or combined hydraulic-and-electric mechanisms that apply force to the same disc surface, focusing on their arrangement and coordinated operation.
[F16D 2055/007] Pins holding the braking members -- This CPC group relates to hydraulic or mechanical braking systems where pins are used to secure the brake shoes, pads or other braking members to the supporting structure. It covers designs, configurations and attachment methods for such pins, including retaining, adjusting or releasing mechanisms.
[F16D 2055/0075] Constructional features of axially engaged brakes -- This CPC subclass defines the constructional features of brakes that are engaged by axial movement, covering the arrangement of components, actuation mechanisms, and interfacing parts that transmit force along the brake's axis to achieve stopping or holding functions.
[F16D 2055/0079] with braking members arranged non-symmetrically with respect to the rotor -- F16D 2055/0079 covers rotor or turbine designs where the braking members are placed asymmetrically with respect to the rotor, creating non-uniform or directional braking forces. The classification is applied to machines such as pumps, compressors, turbines or other rotating equipment that use this uneven brake arrangement.
[F16D 2055/0083] with brake actuator located radially inside of an annular rotor -- F16D 2055/0083 covers braking devices in which the actuator is placed radially inside an annular rotor rather than on its exterior. The classification applies to rotary brakes that integrate the actuation mechanism within the rotating ring, providing a compact arrangement and protection from external contaminants.
[F16D 2055/0087] with brake actuator located between two coplanar annular rotors -- A brake arrangement in which the actuation mechanism is placed between two coplanar annular (ring-shaped) rotors, allowing the actuator to apply force directly to the discs from the intermediate position. This configuration is used in fluid-pressure or mechanical brake systems to achieve compact, balanced braking performance.
[F16D 2055/0091] Plural actuators arranged side by side on the same side of the rotor -- The CPC subclass F16D 2055/0091 covers rotary-actuator designs that employ two or more actuators placed side by side on the same side of a rotor. It relates to arrangements where the actuators work together to transmit torque or motion, often to balance forces, increase output, or simplify the drive mechanism in hydraulic, pneumatic or electric rotary systems.
[F16D 2055/0095] Plural rotors with different properties, e.g. to influence working conditions like wear or temperature -- F16D 2055/0095 covers machines that use multiple rotors deliberately designed with different characteristics such as material, geometry or coating in order to influence operating conditions like wear rates, temperature distribution or load handling.
[F16D 2065/022] Rollers -- The CPC entry F16D 2065/022 covers rollers employed in pump constructions, particularly those that guide or support moving elements such as screws, racks, or shafts within a pump housing. It includes rollers that transmit motion, reduce friction, or maintain alignment of the pump's internal components.
[F16D 2065/024] the braking surface being inclined with respect to the rotor's axis of rotation at an angle other than 90 degrees, e.g. comprising a conical rotor -- F16D 2065/024 covers brake assemblies where the friction or braking surface is mounted at an angle other than 90 degrees to the rotor's axis of rotation, such as designs that use a conical rotor to provide an inclined braking face. This classification addresses the geometry of the braking surface rather than the specific materials or actuation mechanisms.
[F16D 2065/026] characterised by a particular outline shape of the braking member, e.g. footprint of friction lining -- F16D 2065/026 relates to brake systems in which the braking component is defined by a specific outline shape, such as the footprint of the friction lining. The classification covers designs where the geometry of the brake member's contact surface is a distinguishing feature of the invention.
[F16D 2065/13] Parts or details of discs or drums -- F16D 2065/13 covers the various components and accessories that form or are attached to rotating discs or drums, such as hubs, flanges, end plates, brackets and similar fittings. The subclass is applied to patents for mechanical devices like pumps, compressors, fans and other rotary equipment where the disc or drum functions as a key element of the machine.
[F16D 2065/1304] Structure -- The entry covers the structural features of devices used for handling parts or materials, such as frames, supports, guides, housings or other mechanical elements that form the body of conveyors, feeders, and related equipment for moving, supporting or positioning workpieces.
[F16D 2065/1308] one-part -- F16D 2065/1308 designates pumps that are built as a single integral unit, with the housing, impeller and related elements formed from one piece of material rather than assembled from separate parts. This one-part construction simplifies manufacture, enhances reliability and reduces the risk of leakage.
[F16D 2065/1312] circumferentially segmented -- F16D 2065/1312 covers gear constructions that are built from separate pieces arranged around the gear's circumference, providing a segmented design that can be assembled, replaced or reconfigured easily. Such circumferential segmentation is used to adjust gear geometry or facilitate maintenance.
[F16D 2065/1316] radially segmented -- The CPC entry F16D 2065/1316 covers rotary power-transmission elements that are divided into separate radial sections, each section forming a portion of a tooth or gear rack. It applies to designs where the gear or sprocket is built from radially segmented components to enable modular assembly, adjustable engagement or variable transmission characteristics. This falls within the broader class of gear wheels and other power-transmission devices.
[F16D 2065/132] layered -- The CPC subclass F16D 2065/132 covers gear bodies or components that are constructed as layered structures, in which multiple material layers are combined to form a single gear element. These designs use differing material properties to improve strength, wear resistance or damping.
[F16D 2065/1324] carrying friction elements -- The CPC subclass F16D 2065/1324 covers devices in which friction elements are used to transmit or carry loads, typically in hydraulic or pneumatic cylinders, actuators, brakes, clutches and similar mechanisms. It includes designs where friction pads, shoes, plates or linings are integral to the motion-transfer system, ensuring controlled resistance or stopping power.
[F16D 2065/1328] internal cavities, e.g. cooling channels -- F16D 2065/1328 relates to fluid-handling devices that incorporate internal cavities, such as cooling channels, designed to remove heat from the system. It covers the structure, arrangement and manufacturing of these internal passages within hydraulic, pneumatic or similar components.
[F16D 2065/1332] external ribs, e.g. for cooling or reinforcement -- The code F16D 2065/1332 covers external ribs applied to the outside of fluid-handling members such as pump housings, casings, or conduits, where the ribs serve purposes like improving heat dissipation or providing structural reinforcement. This includes any protruding rib features designed to enhance cooling efficiency or mechanical strength of the component.
[F16D 2065/1336] integral part of vehicle wheel -- F16D 2065/1336 classifies components that form a permanent, structural part of a vehicle wheel, such as rims, spokes, hubs, disc brakes, or integrated suspension elements, which are not removable without destroying the wheel. The code is used for patents describing designs where the part is built into the wheel rather than attached as an accessory.
[F16D 2065/134] Connection -- F16D 2065/134 covers connections used in hydraulic, pneumatic or fluid-power systems, such as couplings, fittings, joints, quick-disconnects and other devices that join pipes, hoses or components to a pump or actuator. It relates to the means of securing and sealing fluid-conducting lines within fluid-power machinery.
[F16D 2065/1344] permanent, e.g. by casting -- This subclass relates to doors, gates, flaps, hatches or similar openings that are integral to the structure, being made permanent-often by casting-rather than removable. It applies to such fixed access points in aircraft or other vehicles.
[F16D 2065/1348] resilient -- The CPC subclass F16D 2065/1348 belongs to positive displacement pumps and covers designs that use resilient components such as flexible diaphragms, membranes or elastomeric seals to absorb pressure changes, reduce wear and improve sealing performance.
[F16D 2065/1352] articulated -- F16D 2065/1352 classifies mechanical drive arrangements that incorporate articulated joints or linkages, allowing pivoting or flexible movement between shafts, gears, or other rotating elements. It covers designs where the articulation enables transmission of torque while accommodating angular displacement, typical in complex machinery, robotics, or vehicle steering systems.
[F16D 2065/1356] interlocking -- This CPC subclass covers interlocking mechanisms used in fluid-control devices such as valves, where moving parts engage each other to lock positions and prevent unwanted motion, often employing lugs, teeth or dovetail shapes to secure the valve stem or closure member during operation.
[F16D 2065/136] with relative movement radially -- The CPC entry F16D 2065/136 relates to valves or gates that achieve flow regulation by moving radially with respect to a fixed housing, such as sliding or rotating throttling elements that shift outward or inward. It specifically covers mechanisms that control liquid or gas flow through radial displacement rather than axial motion.
[F16D 2065/1364] with relative movement axially -- This classification covers hydraulic or pneumatic cylinders in which a piston, rod or similar moving member travels relative to the cylinder housing along the axis, providing linear actuation. It includes designs where the sliding element moves axially within a sealed chamber to generate or transmit force.
[F16D 2065/1368] with relative movement both radially and axially -- F16D 2065/1368 covers shafts, spindles or similar rotating members that are designed to allow a relative motion of the member both radially outward or inward and axially forward or backward with respect to a supporting component. The classification is used for mechanisms such as splined shafts, flexible couplings or bearing arrangements that permit combined radial and axial displacement.
[F16D 2065/1372] outer circumference -- The CPC subclass F16D 2065/1372 covers inventions whose design or functional aspects are defined by the outer circumference of a component, such as the external profile of rotating shafts, drums, or other cylindrical elements. It includes features that affect strength, balance, sealing, or interaction with surrounding parts based on that outer boundary.
[F16D 2065/1376] inner circumference -- F16D 2065/1376 covers inventions that define or modify the inner circumference of a gear or gear-like component, including its geometry, dimensions, profile and related features that affect meshing, strength or performance. It is used for patents focusing on the inner circular boundary of rotating transmission elements.
[F16D 2065/138] to wheel -- F16D 2065/138 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to specific aspects of clutches or couplings adapted for use on wheels, detailing mechanisms or features that facilitate their application or function in this context.
[F16D 2065/1384] to wheel hub -- F16D 2065/1384 classifies inventions that relate to devices or components mounted on, integrated with, or acting on a wheel hub, such as bearings, brakes, sensors, lighting or other functional elements that are attached to or form part of the hub structure. It covers arrangements where the hub performs a purpose beyond merely supporting the wheel, including mechanisms for power transmission, control or signal generation.
[F16D 2065/1388] to shaft or axle -- F16D 2065/1388 covers arrangements that transmit fluid-actuated power to a shaft or axle, such as hydraulic or pneumatic motors, torque converters and other drive mechanisms that convert fluid pressure into rotational motion. It includes the construction, mounting and coupling of these power-transfer units to the rotating element.
[F16D 2065/1392] Connection elements -- F16D 2065/1392 covers the various connection elements used in hydraulic or pneumatic cylinders, such as couplings, flanges, sleeves, clamps, bushings and fastening devices that join the piston rod to the cylinder body or to other parts. These components transmit force while permitting assembly, alignment or disassembly of the cylinder assembly.
[F16D 2065/1396] Ancillary resilient elements, e.g. anti-rattle or retraction springs -- F16D 2065/1396 relates to auxiliary resilient components used in hydraulic or pneumatic systems, specifically devices such as anti-rattle or retraction springs that absorb shock, maintain position or return moving parts to a rest position.
[F16D 2065/383] for adjusting the spring force in spring-applied brakes -- The code covers devices or methods that adjust the spring force in spring-applied braking systems, such as mechanisms that vary tension, preload or compression of the spring to control activation pressure or release characteristics of the brake. It includes adjustable springs, tensioning devices and related control elements used in mechanical brakes.
[F16D 2065/386] driven electrically -- F16D 2065/386 classifies pumps that are powered by an electric motor or other electrical drive, covering designs in which the pump is driven electrically rather than mechanically, hydraulically or pneumatically.
[F16D 2065/781] involving phase change of material -- CPC class F16D 2065/781 covers devices in which a material changes phase-solid to liquid, liquid to gas, or vice-versa-to achieve a mechanical or functional effect. The entry includes heating, cooling or heat-transfer arrangements that exploit the latent-heat transition for actuation, damping, thermal storage or similar applications.
[F16D 2065/782] the brake-actuating fluid being used as a coolant -- This subclass concerns hydraulic brake systems in which the brake-actuating fluid is also employed to remove heat from the brakes or related components. It includes arrangements where the fluid circulates through heat-exchangers, passages or cooling fins, allowing the same medium to convey braking force and act as a coolant during repeated or prolonged braking.
[F16D 2065/783] cooling control or adjustment -- The Cooperative Patent Classification code F16D 2065/783, used by the European and United States patent offices, pertains to methods and devices for controlling or adjusting the cooling of mechanical components, particularly in friction clutches or brakes. This classification covers various aspects of thermal management to ensure optimal performance and longevity of these parts.
[F16D 2065/784] the coolant not being in direct contact with the braking surface -- The code covers brake assemblies that employ a cooling medium separated from the friction surfaces, such that the coolant does not directly contact the brake pad or disc. It includes designs using indirect heat removal via chambers, ducts or heat-conductive links that transport thermal energy away from the braking interface.
[F16D 2065/785] Heat insulation or reflection -- The CPC class F16D 2065/785 relates to pipe or valve assemblies that incorporate heat-insulating or reflective measures, such as insulating layers, reflective coatings or thermal barriers, to minimise heat loss or shield the component from external temperatures. It includes designs where these thermal-control features are integrated into the structure of the fitting.
[F16D 2065/786] Fluid spray devices -- F16D 2065/786 covers devices that transform a liquid into a spray or mist by forcing it through a nozzle or atomizer, including hand-held, pneumatic or electric sprayers used for coating, cleaning, cooling or dispensing. The class includes designs that meter the fluid, generate droplets of defined size and may incorporate pumps, horns or ultrasonic transducers.
[F16D 2065/787] Pumps -- The CPC subclass F16D 2065/787 covers patents dealing with pumps, which are devices that impart kinetic energy to fluids to create flow or pressure. It includes various types such as centrifugal, axial, or positive-displacement pumps and their structural features, drive arrangements, and control methods.
[F16D 2065/788] Internal cooling channels -- F16D 2065/788 relates to internal cooling channels integrated within the walls of fluid conduits, ducts or similar components to remove heat from the conveyed fluid, providing enhanced thermal management through passages built into the structure.
[F16D 2065/789] External cooling ribs -- F16D 2065/789 relates to external cooling ribs that are mounted on the surface of a component, such as a shaft, housing or bearing, to enhance heat removal. The ribs extend outward from the part, increasing the area exposed to air or a coolant flow and thereby improving thermal dissipation. The classification covers various shapes, arrangements and attachment methods for such ribs.
[F16D 2066/001] Temperature -- F16D 2066/001 pertains to temperature-related features of shafts, spindles, bearings, couplings and similar rotating elements. It includes devices for heating, cooling, temperature sensing or control that are integrated with or applied to these components to protect them, maintain operating conditions or adjust performance.
[F16D 2066/003] Position, angle or speed -- F16D 20/66/003 classifies devices or methods that control or detect the position, angular orientation or speed of moving parts in reciprocating pumps, such as pistons, shafts or cams. It includes sensors, feedback mechanisms and actuators used to adjust pump operation.
[F16D 2066/005] Force, torque, stress or strain -- The CPC entry F16D 2066/005 falls within the fluid-power engineering subclass and covers aspects of force, torque, stress or strain in hydraulic and pneumatic systems, including the measurement, monitoring, transmission and control of mechanical loads and deformations.
[F16D 2066/006] without direct measurement of the quantity monitored, e.g. wear or temperature calculated form force and duration of braking -- This CPC subclass deals with indirect determination of wear or temperature in braking systems, using measured braking force and the duration of braking rather than direct sensors. It covers methods that calculate the monitored quantity from force and time data.
[F16D 2066/008] of clutches -- F16D 2066/008 covers mechanical clutches that are incorporated in pumps, compressors or similar rotating machines; it pertains to devices that transmit or interrupt torque between a drive and a driven shaft, including friction, hydraulic or other engagement mechanisms used to control start-up, speed or load handling.
[F16D 2069/001] Material of friction lining and support element of same or similar composition -- CPC F16D 2069/001 classifies inventions in which the friction-lining material and the backing element are formed from the same or a closely related composition, such as homogeneous or composite structures used in brakes, clutches or other friction devices. It covers designs where the lining and its support share material properties to improve performance or manufacturing efficiency.
[F16D 2069/002] Combination of different friction materials -- F16D 2069/002 covers devices in which two or more friction materials are combined on a single operating surface to obtain specific wear, heat-dissipation or coefficient-of-friction characteristics, such as layered, interleaved or mixed-material brake pads, clutch plates or similar components. The classification includes arrangements where different composites, metals, ceramics or organic compounds are integrated to enhance performance or durability.
[F16D 2069/003] Selection of coacting friction materials -- The code identifies technology for choosing interacting friction material pairings used in brakes, clutches or similar devices where two surfaces slide to transmit torque or force. It addresses criteria, methods and guidelines for selecting compatible friction materials to meet performance, wear and temperature requirements.
[F16D 2069/004] Profiled friction surfaces, e.g. grooves, dimples -- The subclass F16D 2069/004 covers friction surfaces that are deliberately profiled-such as with grooves, dimples or other texture-to control lubrication, reduce wear, or improve sealing in hydraulic, pneumatic or other fluid-power components. It includes designs where the surface profile is an integral part of the device's functional performance.
[F16D 2069/005] having a layered structure -- F16D 2069/005 covers rotary flow machines, such as water turbines, whose components are built with a layered structure, including laminates, composite layers or coatings, to obtain particular mechanical or hydraulic characteristics. The code is applied to patents that disclose layered designs in blades, housings or other turbine parts.
[F16D 2069/006] comprising a heat-insulating layer -- F16D 2069/006 covers valves, pipe fittings or similar devices that incorporate a heat-insulating layer as part of their construction. The layer is applied to reduce heat transfer between the fluid and surrounding parts, improve energy efficiency, or protect temperature-sensitive components. It includes any arrangement where the insulating material is integral to the sealing or structural elements of the device.
[F16D 2069/007] comprising a resilient layer -- F16D 2069/007 relates to reciprocating piston fluid-power pumps that incorporate a resilient layer-typically an elastomeric or rubber coating-on the piston or a piston component to provide sealing, cushioning, or wear reduction.
[F16D 2069/008] Layers of fibrous materials -- F16D 2069/008 relates to the construction of protective or structural components consisting of multiple fibrous layers, such as fabrics, composites, or mats, often used in sealing, filtering, or reinforcement applications. The subclass addresses the arrangement, bonding, and integration of these layers to achieve desired mechanical, thermal, or barrier properties.
[F16D 2069/009] Linings attached to both sides of a central support element, e.g. a carrier plate -- This subclass covers pump or fluid-moving devices that employ a central support element, such as a carrier plate, with protective linings bonded to both of its faces, providing wear resistance, sealing or corrosion protection on each side.
[F16D 2069/0425] Attachment methods or devices -- F16D 2069/0425 refers to attachment methods or devices, encompassing fasteners, brackets, clamps and other mechanisms that connect or secure component parts in mechanical constructions. It includes designs for joining structures such as pipes, frames or panels. The entry is used in patent classification for inventions that improve how parts are attached.
[F16D 2069/0433] Connecting elements not integral with the braking member, e.g. bolts, rivets -- The CPC subclass F16D 2069/0433 covers fastening devices that are not built into a brake component but are used to attach it, such as bolts, rivets and similar connectors. It applies to any brake system where the braking member is secured by separate, removable hardware.
[F16D 2069/0441] Mechanical interlocking, e.g. roughened lining carrier, mating profiles on friction material and lining carrier -- This subclass covers friction devices in which the lining and its carrier are joined by mechanical interlocking, such as a roughened carrier surface, complementary mating profiles on the friction material, or other geometry that locks the parts together to prevent slip. It is applied to brakes, clutches and similar assemblies where secure attachment of the friction element is required.
[F16D 2069/045] Bonding -- F16D 2069/045 refers to bonding techniques used to join components in sealing, gasket and joint arrangements. It covers adhesive bonding, welding, brazing, soldering and other methods that create a permanent or semi-permanent seal between parts, particularly for fluid-tight or pressure-resistant applications.
[F16D 2069/0458] metallurgic, e.g. welding, brazing, sintering -- The subclass F16D 2069/0458 covers metallurgical processes applied to the surfaces of structures, such as welding, brazing and sintering, used to improve strength, corrosion resistance or to join components. It pertains to surface-level treatments rather than bulk material modification.
[F16D 2069/0466] chemical, e.g. using adhesives, vulcanising -- The CPC subgroup F16D 2069/0466 addresses devices or processes for joining or sealing members by chemical means, for example through the use of adhesives or vulcanising agents. It includes methods that rely on chemical bonding rather than mechanical interlocking to achieve a reliable connection.
[F16D 2069/0475] comprising thermal treatment -- The CPC subclass F16D 2069/0475 covers pumps, compressors, fans, blowers or similar fluid-moving devices that incorporate a thermal-treatment stage, such as heating, cooling or conditioning the conveyed medium as part of the machine's function.
[F16D 2069/0483] Lining or lining carrier material shaped  in situ -- This subgroup relates to fluid-power components whose protective or functional lining, together with its carrier, is formed directly at the installation site, for example by spraying, casting, or curing the material in place. It covers processes and apparatuses that shape the lining material on-site to conform to the interior surfaces of cylinders, valves, or pipelines.
[F16D 2069/0491] Tools, machines, processes -- F16D 2069/0491 designates tools, machines and related processes used for the manufacture, assembly, repair or maintenance of engineering components, covering special-purpose devices and the methods by which they operate.
[F16D 21/00] Systems comprising a plurality of actuated clutches -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - For synchronisation -> F16D23/04
[F16D 21/02] for interconnecting three or more shafts or other transmission members in different ways -- The F16D 21/02 subclass covers devices that interconnect three or more shafts or other transmission members, providing multiple coupling arrangements for complex power distribution. It includes mechanisms such as gear, chain, belt or hybrid systems that enable varying linkage configurations among the shafts.
[F16D 21/04] with a shaft carrying a number of rotatable transmission members, e.g. gears, each of which can be connected to the shaft by a clutching member or members between the shaft and the hub of the transmission member -- This subclass covers arrangements where a single shaft supports multiple rotatable transmission members such as gears, each of which can be selectively connected to the shaft through one or more clutching elements placed between the shaft and the hub of the transmission member.
[F16D 21/06] at least two driving shafts or two driven shafts being concentric -- **Glossary:** - Mechanically actuated clutches: include also clutches other than mechanically actuated, e.g. electrically or hydraulically actuated
[F16D 21/08] Serially-arranged clutches interconnecting two shafts only when all the clutches are engaged -- F16D 21/08 covers mechanisms where multiple clutches are positioned in series between two shafts and the shafts are linked together only when every clutch in the series is engaged, ensuring full transmission of rotation only after all clutches are simultaneously activated.
[F16D 2121/00] Type of actuator operation force -- **Definition:** This place covers:  This code indicates which operation force drives the brake and is combined with a classification characterising the general structure of the brake or the actuator itself (e.g. drum / disc brake).  If different sources are present, each is classified separately, and in addition, a classification in group F16D2123/00 is assigned.
[F16D 2121/005] unspecified force for releasing a normally applied brake -- The CPC code F16D 2121/005 covers actuator forces that are not defined in detail and are used to release a brake that is normally-applied. It falls under mechanical engineering couplings for transmitting rotation and pertains to the type of force used in the release operation.
[F16D 2121/02] Fluid pressure -- The Cooperative Patent Classification entry F16D 2121/02, under the category of Mechanical Engineering and specifically for couplings transmitting rotation, pertains to actuators operated by fluid pressure. This classification is used for inventions involving hydraulic or pneumatic systems that facilitate rotational coupling in mechanical devices.
[F16D 2121/04] acting on a piston-type actuator, e.g. for liquid pressure -- F16D 2121/04 covers couplings that convert rotational motion into a force acting on a piston-type actuator, typically to generate or control liquid pressure. It relates to mechanical engineering elements that translate rotary input into linear piston movement for hydraulic or pneumatic systems.
[F16D 2121/06] for releasing a normally applied brake -- F16D 2121/06 classifies couplings that transmit rotation and incorporate an actuator force for releasing a brake that is normally applied. The entry covers mechanisms where the coupling's operation overcomes a pre-engaged braking device, allowing the driven shaft to rotate.
[F16D 2121/08] acting on a membrane-type actuator, e.g. for gas pressure -- F16D 2121/08 covers devices where a rotational coupling exerts force on a membrane-type actuator, such as those driven by gas pressure. The classification falls under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, couplings for transmitting rotation, and type of actuator operation force.
[F16D 2121/10] for releasing a normally applied brake -- F16D 2121/10 classifies rotational couplings that incorporate a force-actuated mechanism whose purpose is to disengage a brake that is normally applied. The entry falls under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, and couplings for transmitting rotation, focusing on actuator operation force types used for brake release.
[F16D 2121/12] for releasing a normally applied brake, the type of actuator being irrelevant or not provided for in groups -- F16D 2121/12 classifies couplings used to transmit rotation that incorporate a mechanism for releasing a brake that is normally applied, without specifying or requiring a particular type of actuator. It falls under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, and couplings for transmitting rotation.
[F16D 2121/14] Mechanical -- F16D 2121/14 is a CPC subclass that covers mechanical couplings used to transmit rotational motion when the driving force is supplied by a mechanical actuator. It resides in the hierarchy Mechanical Engineering → Engineering Elements and Units → Couplings for Transmitting Rotation → Type of Actuator Operation Force, and deals specifically with purely mechanical means of operating the coupling.
[F16D 2121/16] for releasing a normally applied brake -- F16D 2121/16 pertains to devices that release a brake that is normally applied, using an actuator force to disengage the braking action in a rotational coupling. The subclass includes mechanisms where the brake is held by default and is released by a control input.
[F16D 2121/18] Electric or magnetic -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Brakes comprising a medium with electrically or magnetically controlled internal friction -> F16D57/002 - Electrodynamic brake systems for vehicles -> B60L7/00 - Dynamo-electric brakes; eddy current brakes -> H02K49/00
[F16D 2121/20] using electromagnets -- F16D 2121/20 relates to mechanical couplings that transmit rotary motion and use electromagnets as the actuation force, employing magnetic attraction or repulsion to engage, lock, or release the coupling.
[F16D 2121/22] for releasing a normally applied brake -- F16D 2121/22 pertains to couplings whose actuator force is employed to release a brake that is normally applied, describing mechanisms that disengage a holding or safety brake by applying a torque or linear force.
[F16D 2121/24] using motors -- F16D 2121/24 covers rotational couplings whose driving force is provided by electric or otherwise powered motors, describing how motor actuation is used to engage, adjust or transmit torque between shafts. This sub-class falls under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, and specifically couplings for transmitting rotation.
[F16D 2121/26] for releasing a normally applied brake -- F16D 2121/26 covers devices that disengage a brake that is normally applied, such as spring-applied or safety brakes, by using an auxiliary force or mechanism to release the braking action. These actuation systems are part of coupling arrangements that transmit rotation and are used in mechanical engineering to provide controlled brake release.
[F16D 2121/28] using electrostrictive or magnetostrictive elements, e.g. piezoelectric elements -- The CPC subclass F16D 2121/28 relates to rotational couplings that use electrostrictive, magnetostrictive or piezoelectric elements to create the actuation force. It covers devices where an electric or magnetic field-induced strain generates torque transmission between shafts.
[F16D 2121/30] for releasing a normally applied brake -- A coupling classified under F16D 2121/30 is a mechanical device that releases a brake that is normally applied, using an actuator to overcome the braking force and permit rotation. It belongs to the family of engineering elements for transmitting rotation and specifically addresses brake-release mechanisms.
[F16D 2121/32] using shape memory elements -- This subgroup addresses rotational couplings whose actuation force is generated by shape-memory elements that alter their shape in response to a stimulus such as heat, allowing the coupling to engage, disengage or adjust without external mechanical input. It falls under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, and specifically couplings for transmitting rotation.
[F16D 2121/34] for releasing a normally applied brake -- The CPC entry F16D 2121/34 refers to coupling devices that incorporate an actuator for releasing a brake that is normally applied, enabling the transmission of rotation only when the brake is disengaged. Such mechanisms are used in rotating machinery where a safety brake must be held in the engaged position until a controlled release is required.
[F16D 2123/00] Multiple operation forces -- **Definition:** This place covers:  This code indicates the presence of multiple independently operating actuators. Not used for plural actuators which are connected to the same power source and can only work in concert, like interconnected hydraulic cylinders.  When this code is assigned, each operation force is indexed additionally.
[F16D 2125/00] Components of actuators -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The code is used to describe the mechanical construction of actuators; a symbol is assigned for each individual component.
[F16D 2125/02] Fluid-pressure mechanisms -- F16D 2125/02 covers fluid-pressure mechanisms that generate or transmit rotational motion, such as hydraulic or pneumatic cylinders, motors and similar devices used in actuators. The subclass includes arrangements where fluid pressure acts on a piston, vane, gear or other element to produce or control rotary output.
[F16D 2125/023] Pumps -- F16D 2125/023 designates pumps that serve as components of actuators within mechanical transmission systems. It covers rotary, gear and similar pump types that are integrated to convey fluid power in devices that transmit rotational motion.
[F16D 2125/026] Pressure-to-pressure converters, e.g. hydropneumatic -- This subclass covers devices that convert fluid pressure from one level to another, typically using a hydropneumatic principle to transmit force within actuator systems. Such converters adjust pressure ratios to match the requirements of coupled mechanical components.
[F16D 2125/04] Cylinders -- F16D 2125/04 covers cylinders that form part of actuator systems used to transmit rotational motion. The subclass includes hydraulic, pneumatic or other fluid-driven cylinders designed to convert fluid pressure into linear or rotary output within mechanical couplings. It is situated under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, and couplings for transmitting rotation.
[F16D 2125/06] Pistons -- This CPC subclass F16D 2125/06 defines pistons used as actuator components that transmit rotational motion, covering their design, construction and operation within mechanical coupling systems. It is located under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, and couplings for transmitting rotation.
[F16D 2125/08] Seals, e.g. piston seals -- F16D 2125/08 relates to sealing devices used in mechanical couplings that transmit rotation, specifically piston-type seals and similar elements that prevent fluid or gas leakage while allowing linear or rotary motion within actuator assemblies.
[F16D 2125/10] Plural pistons interacting by fluid pressure, e.g. hydraulic force amplifiers using different sized pistons -- This subclass includes devices in which two or more pistons are arranged so that fluid pressure transmitted between them produces a mechanical advantage, for example hydraulic force amplifiers that employ pistons of different diameters to increase output force.
[F16D 2125/12] Membrane or diaphragm types -- F16D 2125/12 designates membrane or diaphragm type couplings that transmit rotary motion as components of actuators. These couplings use a flexible membrane or diaphragm to convey torque while permitting misalignment, shock absorption or axial movement, and are classified under mechanical engineering → engineering elements and units → couplings for transmitting rotation.
[F16D 2125/14] Fluid-filled flexible members, e.g. enclosed air bladders -- F16D 2125/14 covers fluid-filled flexible members such as enclosed air bladders that are used as components of actuators to transmit rotational motion. The classification includes devices where a sealed cavity is filled with gas or liquid, allowing expansion or contraction to convert pressure changes into controlled mechanical movement.
[F16D 2125/16] Devices for bleeding or filling -- F16D 2125/16 covers devices that provide a means to bleed or fill hydraulic, pneumatic or other pressurised fluid systems associated with actuators, allowing removal of air or introduction of fluid for maintenance or operation. Such mechanisms are typically integrated into coupling assemblies that transmit rotation.
[F16D 2125/18] Mechanical mechanisms -- F16D 2125/18 designates mechanical mechanisms that form part of actuators, specifically couplings that transmit rotational motion between shafts or other rotating elements. It includes devices such as gear trains, cam drives and other moving linkages used to convert or control motion in mechanical systems.
[F16D 2125/20] converting rotation to linear movement or -- The class F16D 2125/20 includes devices that convert rotary motion into linear movement, such as screw drives, cam-oriented mechanisms, gear-to-lead conversions and other actuator components that transform rotation into straight-line displacement. It covers designs where the primary function is to achieve linear travel from a rotating input.
[F16D 2125/22] acting transversely to the axis of rotation -- The CPC entry F16D 2125/22 covers components of actuator couplings that act transversely to the axis of rotation, meaning they apply or transmit force perpendicular to the rotating shaft. Typical examples include split-type couplings, transverse locking devices and torque-transmitting elements that engage across the shaft rather than along it.
[F16D 2125/24] Rack-and-pinion -- F16D 2125/24 defines rack-and-pinion couplings, where a circular gear (pinion) meshes with a linear gear (rack) to convert rotary motion into linear movement or vice versa. This arrangement is used in actuators and other mechanical systems to transmit rotation while providing precise linear displacement.
[F16D 2125/26] Cranks -- F16D 2125/26 classifies cranks that form part of actuator components used in couplings for transmitting rotational motion, situated within the mechanical-engineering hierarchy of engineering elements and units. This code covers design and functional aspects of such cranks.
[F16D 2125/28] Cams -- Cams are rotating or reciprocating components that convert continuous rotation into prescribed linear, oscillating or intermittent motion, forming part of actuator mechanisms. In the CPC they fall under F16D 2125/28, a subcategory of mechanical engineering dealing with couplings for transmitting rotation and other actuator elements.
[F16D 2125/30] acting on two or more cam followers, e.g. S-cams -- F16D 2125/30 covers coupling or actuation arrangements that simultaneously drive two or more cam followers, such as S-cam mechanisms, within mechanical engineering components for transmitting rotation. These devices are used in actuator systems to provide coordinated motion of multiple followers.
[F16D 2125/32] acting on one cam follower -- The F16D 2125/32 subclass covers couplings that transmit rotary motion by acting on a single cam follower. It includes devices where a cam profile engages one follower element to convert or control movement, typical in mechanical actuators.
[F16D 2125/34] acting in the direction of the axis of rotation -- F16D 2125/34 designates couplings that transmit rotation while applying force parallel to the rotational axis, commonly used in actuator mechanisms to convey axial thrust or torque without lateral displacement.
[F16D 2125/36] Helical cams, Ball-rotating ramps -- F16D 2125/36 covers helical cam mechanisms that employ ball-rotating ramps to transmit rotary motion while generating controlled linear or oscillatory displacement. These devices are used as coupling elements in actuators, converting rotational input into precise motion through the interaction of a helical cam surface and ball-guided ramps.
[F16D 2125/38] with plural cam or ball-ramp mechanisms arranged concentrically with the brake rotor axis -- The F16D 2125/38 subclass covers actuator components that use multiple cam or ball-ramp mechanisms positioned concentrically around the axis of a brake rotor, enabling the transmission of rotational motion for braking or torque-control functions. It falls under mechanical engineering couplings for transmitting rotation.
[F16D 2125/40] Screw-and-nut -- The F16D 2125/40 class defines screw-and-nut arrangements that serve as couplings for transmitting rotation, especially as components of actuators. It includes designs where a threaded screw engages a matching nut to transfer torque while permitting axial movement or adjustment.
[F16D 2125/405] with differential thread -- This subclass covers couplings that transmit rotation and incorporate a differential thread, a screw mechanism where threads of differing pitch or lead provide relative motion between parts, often used to adjust torque or speed in actuators.
[F16D 2125/42] Rack-and-worm gears -- F16D 2125/42 designates rack-and-worm gear assemblies, a type of coupling that converts rotary motion into linear displacement within actuator mechanisms. These gears combine a worm screw with a toothed rack to achieve high reduction ratios and compact power transmission.
[F16D 2125/44] transmitting rotation -- F16D 2125/44 covers actuator components that convey rotational motion between parts, such as shafts or gears. It falls under couplings for transmitting rotation within mechanical engineering and addresses devices that transmit torque while allowing for alignment or misalignment.
[F16D 2125/46] Rotating members in mutual engagement -- F16D 2125/46 relates to rotating members that are engaged with each other to transmit motion, such as intermeshing gears, splines or other mutually engaged surfaces used in couplings for actuators. It covers the design of those parts that mesh to transfer rotational power between shafts.
[F16D 2125/48] with parallel stationary axes, e.g. spur gears -- The subclass F16D 2125/48 covers gear couplings where the driving and driven shafts stay parallel and one or more axes are stationary, as in spur-gear arrangements used as actuator components to transmit rotation.
[F16D 2125/50] with parallel non-stationary axes, e.g. planetary gearing -- A coupling that transmits rotation between shafts whose parallel axes move relative to each other, such as in planetary-type gearing, is covered by this class. It enables torque transfer while allowing the driven member to orbit the driver without altering the parallel alignment of the axes.
[F16D 2125/52] with non-parallel stationary axes, e.g. worm or bevel gears -- The CPC entry F16D 2125/52 falls under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, and couplings for transmitting rotation, specifically covering components of actuators that join shafts with non-parallel stationary axes such as worm or bevel gear arrangements. This classification targets devices that transmit power between intersecting or skewed shafts.
[F16D 2125/54] with non-parallel non-stationary axes -- This subclass deals with couplings that join shafts whose axes are neither parallel nor fixed, allowing the axes to move relative to each other while transmitting rotational power. It includes devices such as flexible or articulated couplings that accommodate angular misalignment and dynamic motion.
[F16D 2125/56] Shafts for transmitting torque directly -- F16D 2125/56 covers shafts that directly transmit torque between rotating components, typically used as part of actuator assemblies and coupling systems. These shafts are designed to handle torque loads without intermediate gears or belts, providing a simple mechanical link in machines and equipment.
[F16D 2125/565] flexible -- F16D 2125/565 classifies flexible couplings used as components of actuators to transmit rotation, allowing torque transmission while accommodating angular, axial or radial misalignment. The entry covers elastomeric, spring or other compliant elements that connect rotating shafts within mechanical systems.
[F16D 2125/58] transmitting linear movement -- The subclass F16D 2125/58 covers devices that convert rotational motion into linear motion for the purpose of transmitting movement, such as lead screws, ball screws, rack-and-pinion gear sets and other linear actuators. It is situated under couplings for transmitting rotation within the broader mechanical-engineering hierarchy.
[F16D 2125/582] Flexible element, e.g. spring, other than the main force generating element -- This subclass covers flexible components such as springs that are incorporated in rotational couplings but are not the primary force-producing element. They are used to accommodate misalignment, damp vibrations, or provide compliance within actuator assemblies.
[F16D 2125/585] arranged in parallel with a force-applying member -- F16D 2125/585 designates actuator couplings that are positioned in parallel with a force-applying member, allowing the torque transmission to be shared or coordinated between the coupling and the member. This arrangement is used in mechanical systems where parallel placement improves load distribution or control.
[F16D 2125/587] Articulation, e.g. ball-socket -- This subclass covers couplings that incorporate an articulating joint such as a ball-socket mechanism, allowing angular misalignment while transmitting rotational motion. It is applied to actuator components where a spherical bearing permits pivoting of the driven part relative to the driver.
[F16D 2125/60] Cables or chains, e.g. Bowden cables -- The Cooperative Patent Classification code F16D 2125/60 pertains to components of actuators, specifically addressing cables or chains, including Bowden cables, used for transmitting rotational force in mechanical engineering. This classification is part of a detailed system maintained by the European Patent Office and the United States Patent and Trademark Office.
[F16D 2125/62] Fixing arrangements therefor, e.g. cable end attachments -- F16D 2125/62 covers fixing arrangements for couplings that transmit rotation, specifically devices that attach cable ends to rotating shafts in actuators. Typical examples include clamps, terminals, or crimp fittings that secure the cable while allowing torque transfer.
[F16D 2125/64] Levers -- This CPC subclass (F16D2125/64) covers levers employed as components of actuators within mechanical couplings that transmit rotational motion. It includes lever mechanisms that convert or transmit torque or force in devices such as gearboxes, clutches or other transmission units.
[F16D 2125/645] with variable leverage, e.g. movable fulcrum -- F16D 2125/645 covers couplings that provide variable mechanical advantage by using a movable fulcrum, allowing the leverage ratio between input and output shafts to be adjusted. These devices are used in actuator assemblies to modify torque and speed characteristics as needed.
[F16D 2125/66] Wedges -- This class covers wedge-shaped elements employed in mechanical couplings that transmit rotary motion, particularly as components of actuators. It includes designs where a wedge engages a mating part to lock or adjust torque and the associated variations and configurations.
[F16D 2125/68] Lever-link mechanisms, e.g. toggles with change of force ratio -- F16D 2125/68 covers lever-link mechanisms, such as toggle linkages, that change the force ratio between input and output and are used as actuator components for transmitting rotation.
[F16D 2125/70] Rods -- F16D 2125/70 - Rods covers rod-like components that form part of actuators used in rotational coupling mechanisms, such as connecting rods, push rods and transmission shafts that transfer motion or force between moving parts. These elements are classified under mechanical engineering, specifically engineering elements and units for couplings that transmit rotation.
[F16D 2127/00] Auxiliary mechanisms -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Additional control mechanisms having a specific function; does not include slack adjusters (classified in group F16D65/38 ) or simple mechanical elements (indexed F16D2125/00 ).
[F16D 2127/001] for automatic or self-acting brake operation -- F16D 2127/001 covers couplings designed with auxiliary mechanisms that automatically or self-actuate brake functions to control rotation transmission. These devices integrate a braking system that engages without external control, improving safety and reliability in mechanical power transmission.
[F16D 2127/002] speed-responsive -- A speed-responsive coupling includes an auxiliary mechanism that automatically varies its operation according to the rotational speed of the driven shaft. Such devices may change engagement pressure, torque transmission capacity or disengage to protect equipment when speed exceeds set limits, providing dynamic control within a rotational transmission system.
[F16D 2127/004] direction-responsive -- F16D 2127/004 designates auxiliary coupling mechanisms that respond to the direction of rotation, altering their engagement or transmission characteristics when the input turns clockwise versus counter-clockwise. These direction-responsive devices, such as one-way clutches or torque limiters, are used to control power flow in shafts and gear trains.
[F16D 2127/005] force- or torque-responsive -- F16D 2127/005 covers couplings that modify their engagement or transmission characteristics in response to applied force or torque, such as torque limiters, overload clutches, and safety devices that disconnect or slip when a load exceeds a preset value.
[F16D 2127/007] for non-linear operation -- F16D 2127/007 covers auxiliary mechanisms in rotational couplings that produce non-linear motion, such as devices that convert constant rotary input into varying speed or displacement. It includes designs where the transmitted rotation follows a non-linear relationship with the driver, enabling applications like variable-speed drives or motion-profile shaping.
[F16D 2127/008] Trigger mechanisms -- F16D 2127/008 pertains to trigger mechanisms, auxiliary devices incorporated in rotational couplings to initiate or release power transmission. These mechanisms are used in mechanical engineering to control the start-stop action of a coupling that transmits rotation.
[F16D 2127/02] Release mechanisms -- F16D 2127/02 covers release mechanisms that are part of auxiliary devices for couplings used to transmit rotation. These mechanisms allow a driven shaft to be disengaged automatically or manually, for example in overload protection, safety disengagement or controlled decoupling, ensuring that the rotational power can be stopped or redirected without damaging the main coupling.
[F16D 2127/04] for manual operation -- F16D 2127/04 covers auxiliary mechanisms for mechanical couplings that transmit rotation and are intended to be operated manually. These devices assist in engaging, disengaging or adjusting the coupling without power assistance, fitting within the broader categories of mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, and couplings for transmitting rotation.
[F16D 2127/06] Locking mechanisms, e.g. acting on actuators, on release mechanisms or on force transmission mechanisms -- The F16D 2127/06 subclass deals with auxiliary locking devices used in rotational couplings, covering mechanisms that secure or release actuators, release systems or force-transmission elements. It is part of the broader group on couplings for transmitting rotation within mechanical engineering.
[F16D 2127/08] Self-amplifying or de-amplifying mechanisms -- F16D 2127/08 covers self-amplifying or de-amplifying mechanisms used in couplings that transmit rotation. These mechanisms modify the input torque or speed, increasing or decreasing it through gear, lever or hydraulic means, typically as auxiliary components in rotating drive systems.
[F16D 2127/085] having additional fluid pressure elements -- F16D 2127/85 relates to rotational couplings that include extra fluid-pressure components, such as hydraulic pistons or chambers, to augment the gripping or torque-transmitting force. These auxiliary mechanisms use fluid power to improve engagement, disengagement or load capacity of the coupling.
[F16D 2127/10] having wedging elements -- F16D 2127/10 designates auxiliary mechanisms for rotating couplings that employ wedging elements, such as wedge-shaped components that lock, clamp or transmit torque between shafts. These elements engage by sliding or expanding to secure the connection and may be released when the wedge is withdrawn.
[F16D 2127/12] having additional frictional elements -- A coupling that incorporates extra friction components, such as friction plates, pads or surfaces, to supplement the primary mechanical link for transmitting rotation. The additional friction elements improve torque transfer, provide damping or prevent slippage under load. This subclass addresses auxiliary mechanisms that rely on friction in conjunction with the main coupling.
[F16D 2129/00] Type of operation source for auxiliary mechanisms -- This subclass classifies the type of power or control source used to operate auxiliary mechanisms associated with rotational couplings, such as hydraulic, pneumatic, electric, manual or thermal drives. It distinguishes devices based on whether the auxiliary system is driven by a motor, engine, external source or self-acting means.
[F16D 2129/02] Fluid-pressure -- F16D 2129/02 designates couplings that use fluid pressure as the source of operation for auxiliary mechanisms, such as hydraulic or pneumatic devices that assist in transmitting rotational motion. The class includes systems where fluid-pressure actuation controls engagement, disengagement or torque modulation within mechanical transmission assemblies.
[F16D 2129/04] Mechanical -- F16D 2129/04 relates to mechanical couplings that transmit rotation when the auxiliary mechanism is driven by a specific type of operation source, such as hydraulic, pneumatic, electric or other secondary power. It is classified under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, and couplings for transmitting rotation.
[F16D 2129/043] Weights -- F16D 2129/043 covers weights employed in couplings that transmit rotation, acting as auxiliary mechanisms that supply a source of operation. These weights are used to adjust torque, balance, or damping within mechanical coupling assemblies.
[F16D 2129/046] Flywheels -- Flywheels are rotating masses that store kinetic energy and release it to smooth out power fluctuations, providing an auxiliary source of rotational energy in coupling mechanisms. They are used to maintain constant speed or supply torque during brief interruptions in the primary drive.
[F16D 2129/06] Electric or magnetic -- F16D 2129/06 classifies couplings that transmit rotation by using electric or magnetic forces. It covers electromagnetic couplings, electric-motor-driven couplings and similar devices where torque is generated by an electric or magnetic field rather than direct mechanical contact, serving as auxiliary mechanisms in mechanical engineering.
[F16D 2129/065] Permanent magnets -- F16D 2129/065 denotes couplings for transmitting rotation that employ permanent magnets as the source of operation for auxiliary mechanisms. It covers mechanical engineering elements where magnetic fields provide the driving or locking force, eliminating the need for external power or pneumatic actuation. This classification groups such magnet-driven coupling designs within the broader category of engineering elements and units.
[F16D 2129/08] Electromagnets -- F16D 2129/08 covers the use of electromagnets as the power source for auxiliary mechanisms that control or assist rotational couplings. It relates to mechanical engineering applications where magnetic fields generate the motion needed to engage, disengage, or adjust coupling elements, providing an alternative to hydraulic or pneumatic actuation. This subclass falls under the broader categories of engineering elements, rotation-transmitting couplings, and operation-source types.
[F16D 2129/10] Motors -- F16D 2129/10 covers motors used as the power source for auxiliary mechanisms in couplings that transmit rotation. It pertains to the type of operation source for such devices, including electric, hydraulic, pneumatic or other motor types that drive coupling accessories.
[F16D 2129/12] Electrostrictive or magnetostrictive elements, e.g. piezoelectric -- F16D 2129/12 classifies couplings that employ electro-strictive, magneto-strictive or piezoelectric elements to generate or modulate torque for auxiliary mechanisms in rotation-transmission systems. The subclass covers devices in which the driving force originates from changes in material strain under electric or magnetic fields, providing an alternative energy source for mechanical coupling operation.
[F16D 2129/14] Shape memory elements -- F16D 2129/14 classifies shape-memory elements that serve as auxiliary actuation sources in rotational couplings. These components exploit reversible shape changes, typically triggered by temperature, to transmit or modify torque within mechanical transmission systems.
[F16D 2131/00] Overall arrangement of the actuators or their elements, e.g. modular construction -- The subclass F16D 2131/00 covers the overall layout of actuators and their constituent parts within couplings that transmit rotation, emphasizing how the elements are arranged or assembled, especially in modular or interchangeable configurations. It includes design concepts that organize the actuator system as a complete unit rather than detailing individual components.
[F16D 2131/02] of the actuator controllers -- F16D 2131/02 pertains to the specific design and integration of controllers for actuators used in couplings that transmit rotational motion, focusing on modular construction and overall arrangement. This classification is part of a detailed system covering mechanical engineering elements and units, specifically addressing sophisticated control mechanisms in coupling systems.
[F16D 2200/00] Materials -- F16D 2200/00 covers the materials used to make rotary couplings that transmit rotation. It includes metals, polymers, composites and other substances selected for strength, wear resistance, corrosion protection or thermal performance in mechanical-engineering coupling applications.
[F16D 2200/0004] metallic -- F16D 2200/0004 designates metal couplings used to transmit rotational motion between shafts or other rotating components. The classification applies to couplings whose principal material is metallic, encompassing various designs such as jaw, sleeve, and gear couplings.
[F16D 2200/0008] Ferro -- F16D 2200/0008 covers couplings for transmitting rotation that are made from ferro-based materials, meaning ferrous or magnetic alloys such as iron, steel or similar composites. [F16D 2200/0013] Cast iron -- F16D 2200/0013 designates the use of cast iron as the material for couplings that transmit rotational motion, classified under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, and couplings for transmitting rotation.

It includes alloys, coatings, surface treatments and other material choices that protect rotating transmission parts from chemical or environmental degradation, ensuring long-term performance and reliability.
[F16D 2200/0021] Steel -- F16D 2200/0021 in the Cooperative Patent Classification system, used by the European and United States patent offices, specifies materials for couplings that transmit rotation, with this particular code designating steel as the material.
[F16D 2200/0026] Non-ferro -- F16D 2200/0026 designates couplings for transmitting rotation that are made from non-ferrous materials such as aluminium, copper, brass, zinc or their alloys, rather than iron-based steels. [F16D 2200/003] Light metals, e.g. aluminium -- F16D 2200/003 in the Cooperative Patent Classification system refers to materials used in couplings for transmitting rotation, specifically focusing on light metals such as aluminum. [F16D 2200/0034] non-metallic -- F16D 2200/0034 classifies couplings used to transmit rotation that are fabricated from non-metallic materials such as polymers, composites, ceramics or wood. [F16D 2200/0039] Ceramics -- F16D 2200/0039 covers couplings for transmitting rotation that are made from ceramic materials. These couplings use ceramics' high hardness, wear resistance and thermal stability, making them suitable for high-speed or corrosive operating conditions.
[F16D 2200/0043] Ceramic base, e.g. metal oxides or ceramic binder -- The CPC entry F16D 2200/0043 covers couplings for transmitting rotary motion that incorporate a ceramic base, such as metal-oxide or ceramic-binder materials.
