The orbital gearing includes two drive connections via (6) and (8) to the parallel shaft gearing.
[F16H 2037/049] Forward-reverse units with forward and reverse gears for achieving multiple forward and reverse gears, e.g. for working machines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  1a.  Figure 1a illustrates a combination (100) of a multi-speed transmission (200) having gears with orbital motion, and a forward/reverse unit (600) not having gears with orbital motion. Forward/reverse unit (600) achieves multiple forward gear ratios of combination (100) and multiple reverse gear ratios of combination (100).  1b.
[F16H 2037/0866] Power-split transmissions with distributing differentials, with the output of the CVT connected or connectable to the output shaft -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Power-split transmissions including distributing planetary gearing and with a CVT in at least one torque path. The CVT is either connected to or connectable with the output shaft.  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this place:  1.  Figure 1 illustrates a combination of a distributing planetary or differential gearing and a non-fluid CVT. Torque from the input shaft is divided by the distributing planetary gear set or differential into two internal power paths. Torque from the two internal power paths is summed at the output shaft.  2.  Figure 2 illustrates a combination of a distributing planetary gear set or differential (15) and a ball-type CVT (12). Torque from input shaft (11) is divided into two internal power paths by the distributing planetary gear set or differential (15), and is summed at the output shaft (19).
[F16H 2037/0873] with switching means, e.g. to change ranges -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates a combination of a distributing planetary gear set or differential (14) and a non-fluid CVT (16). The combination comprises switching means (K1), (K2) and (K3). In a drive mode with (K2) and (K3) closed, torque from input shaft (10) is divided by distributing planetary gear set or differential (14) into two power paths, i.e. a CVT path (14A2) and a CVT-bypass path (14A1). Torque from these two paths is summed at point (18) on output shaft (12).
[F16H 2037/088] Power-split transmissions with summing differentials, with the input of the CVT connected or connectable to the input shaft -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Power-split transmissions including summing planetary gearing and with a CVT in at least one torque path. The CVT is either connected to or connectable with the input shaft.  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this place:  1.  Figure 1 illustrates a combination of a summing planetary or differential gearing and a non-fluid CVT. Torque from the input shaft is divided into two power paths. Torque from the two power paths is summed by the summing planetary gear set or differential and output to the output shaft.  2.  Figure 2 illustrates a combination including a summing planetary gear set or differential (12) and a belt-type CVT (10). Input torque is divided into two internal power paths, and summed by summing planetary gear set or differential (12) and output to the output shaft (6).
[F16H 2037/0886] with switching means, e.g. to change ranges -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  1a.  Figure 1a illustrates a combination of a summing planetary gear set or differential (RS1), (RS2) and a non-fluid CVT (12). The combination comprises switching means (B1), (B2) and (K1) for switching between three drive modes (V1), (V2) and (R1). In drive mode (V2), power of input shaft (1) is divided into two power paths. Torque from these two paths is summed by summing planetary gear set or differential (RS1), (RS2) and output to output shaft (2).  1b.
[F16H 2037/0893] characterised in that the ratio of the continuously variable transmission is different from zero when the output shaft speed is zero -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Power-split transmissions including summing or distributing planetary gearing and with a CVT in at least one torque path. The transmission includes a speed ratio where the output shaft speed is zero while the individual CVT ratio is different from zero, i.e. a "geared neutral" speed ratio.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  1a.  Figure 1a illustrates a power-split transmission including a combination of a toroidal-type CVT (8), (9) and planetary or differential gearing (16), (30). The overall ratio of the transmission may be changed from reverse speed ratio R through zero speed ratio N to forward speed ratio FL merely by changing the ratio of the CVT. At speed ratio N, the speed of output shaft (40) is zero while the speed of input shaft (1) is different from zero and the ratio of the toroidal CVT (8) and (9) is different from zero. The speed ratio N is otherwise known as "geared neutral" since this results in zero rotation speed of the output shaft while the input shaft is rotating.  1b.  **Glossary:** - geared neutral: input shaft speed is different from zero though output shaft speed is zero. It is noted that this enables drive-off without using a torque converter or disconnecting clutch.
[F16H 2037/101] Power-split transmissions with one differential at each end of a continuously variable transmission, i.e. CVT -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates a combination of two summing/distributing planetary or differential gearings and a CVT. The two summing/distributing planetary gear sets or differentials are at each end of the CVT in the torque flow. Torque from the input shaft is divided at distributing planetary gear set or differential (1), summed at summing planetary gear set or differential (2), and output to the output shaft.
[F16H 2037/102] the input or output shaft of the transmission is connected or connectable to two or more differentials -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates an example of a combination of two summing/distributing planetary or differential gearings and a CVT. Torque from the input shaft is divided at distributing planetary gear set or differential (1), summed at summing planetary gear set or differential (2), and output to the output shaft. The output shaft is connected to the outputs of both of distributing planetary gear set or differential (1) and summing planetary gear set or differential (2).
[F16H 2037/103] Power-split transmissions with each end of a CVT connected or connectable to a planetary gear set having four or more connections, e.g. a Ravigneaux set -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Power split variators with each end of the CVT connected or connectable to the same Ravigneaux set.  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this place:  1.  Figure 1 illustrates a power-split transmission including a combination of a summing/distributing Ravigneaux set and a CVT. Both the input and output of the CVT are connected to the summing/distributing Ravigneaux set. Torque from the input shaft is divided at the Ravigneaux set, summed at the Ravigneaux set, and output to the output shaft.  2.  Figure 2 illustrates a power-split transmission including a combination of a summing/distributing planetary gear set (4) and a belt-type CVT (32). A first end (40) of the CVT (32) is connected to the sun gear (24) of planetary gear set (4) via gear (39), and a second end (38) of the CVT (32) is connected to the ring gear (22) of planetary gear set (4) via gear (37). Planetary gear set (4) includes four connections to other part of the transmission, specifically ring gear (22), carrier (12), sun gear (24), and ring gear (23).
[F16H 2037/104] Power-split transmissions with at least one end of a CVT connected or connectable to two or more differentials -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates a power-split transmission including a combination of two summing/distributing planetary or differential gearings and a CVT. The CVT has a first end connected to both of planetary gear set or differential (1) and planetary gear set or differential (2), and further has a second end connected to both of planetary gear set or differential (1) and planetary gear set or differential (2). Torque from the input shaft is divided at distributing planetary gear set or differential (1), summed at summing planetary gear set or differential (2), and output to the output shaft.
[F16H 2037/105] characterised by number of modes or ranges, e.g. for compound gearing -- The CPC group F16H 2037/105 covers transmission devices whose design is defined by the number of operating modes or by offering a range of gear ratios, such as compound gear sets that provide multiple speed options. It includes gear arrangements where the characteristic feature is the quantity of distinct modes or the span of ratios available rather than the specific geometry of individual gears.
[F16H 2037/106] with switching means to provide two variator modes or ranges -- F16H 2037/106 covers power-transmission units, such as continuously variable or fluid couplings, that include a switching mechanism enabling the variator to operate in two separate speed-ratio modes or ranges, typically allowing an abrupt shift between a low and a high transmission range.
[F16H 2037/107] with switching means to provide three variator modes or ranges -- This subclass covers gear transmission devices that incorporate a selector or switching mechanism to provide three distinct variator speed or torque ranges, allowing the gearbox to operate in three separate modes.
[F16H 2037/108] with switching means to provide four or more variator modes or ranges -- This subclass covers hydraulic control devices that incorporate a switching mechanism allowing the operator to select at least four distinct variator settings or operating ranges. The switching means adjusts the flow or pressure characteristics of the system to provide multiple performance modes, typically for variable-speed drives or load-control applications.
[F16H 2037/128] Generating reciprocating motion by a planetary gear (ratio 2:1) using endless flexible members -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this place:  1.  Figure 1 illustrates a combination of a belt-type gearing (41), (16), (42) and a lever gearing (12) and (14). Rotary motion of input pulley (41) is converted via levers (12) and (14) into reciprocating motion of lever end (45). Pulley (42) partially orbits around pulley (41) such that belt-type gearing (41), (16), (42) constitutes a planetary gear using an endless flexible member (16). The ratio of pulley (41) to pulley (42) is 2:1. This combination conveys oscillating motion of input pulley (41) into reciprocating motion of lever end (45).  2a.  2b.  Figures 2a and 2b illustrate a combination a belt-type gearing (22), (40), (30) and a lever gearing (26) and (36). Rotary motion of input pulley (22) is converted via levers (26) and (36) into reciprocating motion of carriage (10). Pulley (30) partially orbits around pulley (22) such that belt-type gearing (22), (40), (30) constitutes a planetary gear using an endless flexible member (40). The ratio of pulley (22) to pulley (30) is 2:1. This combination conveys rotary motion of input pulley (22) into reciprocating motion of carriage (12).
[F16H 2039/005] comprising arrangements or layout to change the capacity of the motor or pump by moving the hydraulic chamber of the motor or pump -- This subclass covers devices whose hydraulic motor or pump capacity is altered by moving the hydraulic chamber, including arrangements and layouts that shift the chamber to vary displacement.
[F16H 2039/105] at least one pair of motors or pumps sharing a common swash plate -- F16H 2039/105 relates to hydraulic or pneumatic devices in which two or more motors or pumps are coupled to a single swash plate that controls their displacement simultaneously, allowing coordinated operation and compact construction. This subclass covers arrangements where the shared swash plate drives at least one pair of such power units.
[F16H 2041/243] Connections between pump shell and cover shell of the turbine -- The subclass covers mechanical joining techniques that link the pump or turbine's outer pressure shell to its protective cover shell, such as bolting, welding, clamping or sealing arrangements that provide structural integrity and prevent leakage between the two housings.
[F16H 2041/246] relating to one way clutch of the stator -- This subclass covers one-way (overrunning) clutch devices built into the stator of rotating machines, allowing torque to be transmitted in only one direction while preventing reverse motion. It is used to lock the stator, provide safety or control in gearings, pumps and turbines.
[F16H 2041/285] of stator blades -- Subclass F16H 2041/285 deals with stationary blades - the fixed vanes that form a stator in turbines, compressors or similar rotating machines. It covers the design, shape, profile, mounting or material characteristics of those stator blades, including specific geometric features or manufacturing aspects.
[F16H 2045/002] comprising a clutch between prime mover and fluid gearing -- The CPC subclass F16H 2045/002 covers mechanical arrangements where a clutch is placed between the prime mover and a fluid-gearing system such as a hydraulic or hydrostatic transmission, enabling the drive to be engaged or disengaged. It addresses devices that control power transmission to the fluid gear for start-up, speed changes or idle operation.
[F16H 2045/005] comprising a clutch between fluid gearing and the mechanical gearing unit -- A fluid-power transmission that includes a clutch positioned between the hydraulic (fluid) gearing and a mechanical gearing unit, enabling the engagement or disengagement of the two stages and providing control over torque transmission. This arrangement allows the system to start, stop or vary load transfer without interrupting the fluid circuit.
[F16H 2045/007] comprising a damper between turbine of the fluid gearing and the mechanical gearing unit -- **Definition:** This place covers:  A combination of fluid gearing for conveying rotary motion with couplings or clutches and comprising a damper in the power path between a turbine of the fluid gearing and a mechanical gearing unit or transmission.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates a combination of a hydrodynamic torque converter (14) and a torque converter lock-up clutch (50). Two dampers 16 are in the power path between the turbine (32) and the input shaft (40) of a mechanical transmission.
[F16H 2045/0205] two chamber system, i.e. without a separated, closed chamber specially adapted for actuating a lock-up clutch -- The entry falls under CPC group F16H 2045/0205, which covers hydraulic or pneumatic devices that use a two-chamber arrangement where the chambers are not fully sealed from each other, specifically designed to activate a lock-up clutch. Such systems provide direct fluid-pressure transmission to engage or disengage the clutch.
[F16H 2045/021] three chamber system, i.e. comprising a separated, closed chamber specially adapted for actuating a lock-up clutch -- The CPC entry relates to transmission elements, specifically lock-up clutches that use a three-chamber arrangement in which a separate closed chamber is dedicated to supplying fluid or pressure for clutch actuation, as found in torque converters and automatic gearboxes.
[F16H 2045/0215] Details of oil circulation -- The CPC subclass F16H 2045/0215 relates to the specific aspects of oil circulation within hydraulic or fluid power systems, covering features such as oil supply, routing, filtration, cooling and return paths that ensure proper lubrication and temperature control. It addresses mechanisms and arrangements that manage the flow of oil to maintain system performance and reliability.
[F16H 2045/0221] with damping means -- F16H 2045/0221 classifies valves that incorporate damping means to control the movement of the closing or opening element. The damping device-hydraulic, pneumatic, magnetic, spring-loaded or similar-reduces impact, vibration, noise or wear during operation, and is integral to the valve's actuation mechanism.
[F16H 2045/0226] comprising two or more vibration dampers -- The CPC class F16H 2045/0226 covers mechanical devices or assemblies that incorporate at least two vibration-damping units, such as mounts, pads, or absorbers, to reduce oscillatory motion of a structure or component. It includes arrangements where multiple dampers work together to enhance stability, noise reduction, or fatigue resistance in machines, vehicles, or structural systems. This subclass is used for inventions that specify the presence of two or more coordinated dampers.
[F16H 2045/0231] arranged in series -- F16H 2045/0231 designates valve or fluid-control devices that are positioned in series, where the fluid passes through one valve and then directly into another. This layout enables staged regulation, pressure reduction, or redundancy by linking multiple valve elements along the same flow path.
[F16H 2045/0236] with axial dampers, e.g. comprising a ramp system -- This subclass covers valves that incorporate axial dampers to temper the motion of moving parts, often using a ramp-type mechanism that converts axial displacement into controlled resistance. The dampers help reduce shock, noise, and wear during opening or closing, improving reliability in hydraulic and pneumatic systems.
[F16H 2045/0242] with viscous dampers -- This subclass covers valve constructions whose motion is moderated by viscous dampers, usually incorporated in hydraulic, pneumatic or electromechanical actuators. The dampers provide fluid-filled resistance that smooths valve movement, limits impact forces and controls opening or closing speed, improving reliability and sealing performance.
[F16H 2045/0247] having a turbine with hydrodynamic damping means -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates a combination of a hydrodynamic torque converter (1), (2), (3) and a torque converter lock-up or bridging clutch (13). A torsional damper is connected with the turbine (2).
[F16H 2045/0252] having a damper arranged on input side of the lock-up clutch -- The code refers to fluid-power devices where a damper is positioned on the input side of a lock-up clutch to smooth torque transmission and limit shock loads during engagement. Such arrangements are used in automotive or transmission systems to improve durability and comfort.
[F16H 2045/0257] having a pump adapted for use as a secondary mass of the damping system -- The code refers to dampers, particularly hydraulic or pneumatic ones, that incorporate a pump which functions as a secondary inertial mass within the damping system. The pump's movement adds a counter-mass effect, enhancing vibration isolation and energy absorption.
[F16H 2045/0263] the damper comprising a pendulum -- F16H 2045/0263 designates a damper that incorporates a pendulum as the primary energy-absorbing element, typically employed to reduce vibrations in mechanical systems such as rotating shafts or structural components. The pendulum motion converts kinetic energy into controlled damping forces, enhancing stability and prolonging equipment life.
[F16H 2045/0268] the damper comprising a gearing -- F16H 2045/0268 relates to dampers that incorporate gearing to transmit motion or force, typically employed to moderate speed, torque or vibration in mechanical systems such as gear-driven shock absorbers and vibration-control devices.
[F16H 2045/0273] characterised by the type of the friction surface of the lock-up clutch -- The CPC entry F16H 2045/0273 covers lock-up clutches whose design is distinguished by the type of friction surface used, such as material composition, texture or geometry that affect how the clutch engages and transmits torque. It includes variations like dry, wet, sintered or composite friction surfaces that define the clutch's performance characteristics.
[F16H 2045/0278] comprising only two co-acting friction surfaces -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates a combination of a hydrodynamic torque converter (10) and a torque converter lock-up clutch (18). Lock-up clutch (18) includes only two co-acting friction surfaces at (72).
[F16H 2045/0284] Multiple disk type lock-up clutch -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates a combination of a hydrodynamic torque converter (8) and a torque converter lock-up clutch (28). Lock-up clutch (28) includes multiple friction disks (34) and (35) each with friction material on opposing sides.  **Glossary:** - disk: a friction member including friction surfaces on opposing sides
[F16H 2045/0289] Details of friction surfaces of the lock-up clutch -- The CPC subclass F16H 2045/0289 pertains to lock-up clutches, specifically covering the design, material composition, geometry, surface treatment or texture of the friction surfaces that engage the clutch. It includes features that affect frictional performance, wear resistance and heat dissipation during lock-up operation, such as ribbing, grooves, coatings or composite structures. This subclass is used to classify patents describing the detailed construction of those friction interfaces.
[F16H 2045/0294] Single disk type lock-up clutch, i.e. using a single disc engaged between friction members -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates a combination of a hydrodynamic torque converter (16) and a torque converter lock-up clutch (26). Lock-up clutch (26) includes only one disk with friction surfaces on opposing sides.
[F16H 2047/025] the fluid gearing comprising a plurality of pumps or motors -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Combinations of mechanical gearing with fluid gearing of the volumetric type. The fluid gearing includes more than a single pump or a single motor. For example, the volumetric gearing includes two motors and one pump, or two motors and two pumps. The mechanical gearing does not include orbital motion.  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this place:  1. [F16H 2048/02] Transfer gears for influencing drive between outputs -- Transfer gears for influencing drive between outputs cover gear assemblies that split, combine or modify power flow to two or more output shafts, allowing changes in speed, torque or direction among the outputs. The classification includes planetary, epicyclic, and other gear arrangements designed to distribute drive between multiple recipients.
[F16H 2048/04] having unequal torque transfer between two outputs -- This subclass deals with gear or transmission arrangements where the torque delivered to two driven shafts is deliberately different, such as splitters, differential gears, or mechanisms that allocate varying torque based on load or control. Such designs are used to balance power distribution in automotive differentials, industrial drives, and robotics.
[F16H 2048/082] characterised by the arrangement of output shafts -- F16H 2048/082 covers gear or transmission arrangements where the defining feature is the spatial configuration of the output shafts. It includes devices with coaxial, parallel, inclined or spaced shafts that are designed to deliver power to multiple points. The classification applies to any mechanism whose primary inventive aspect is how the output shafts are arranged.
[F16H 2048/085] characterised by shafts or gear carriers for orbital gears -- The class F16H 2048/085 covers gear arrangements in which the orbital gear is mounted on a shaft or a dedicated gear carrier instead of being directly fixed to the housing. It applies to planetary or epicyclic gear sets where the carrier provides support and positional control, common in automotive and industrial transmissions.
[F16H 2048/087] characterised by the pinion gears, e.g. their type or arrangement -- F16H 2048/087 pertains to gear transmissions in which the defining element is the pinion gear, covering aspects such as its type, number, size, tooth profile or arrangement within the gear set.
[F16H 2048/102] with spur gears engaging face gears -- This subclass covers gear arrangements in which a conventional spur gear meshes with a face gear, the latter having teeth cut on a plane perpendicular to its axis. It pertains to power-transmission units that use this spur-to-face gear engagement for compact or right-angle drive configurations.
[F16H 2048/104] characterised by two ring gears -- F16H 2048/104 falls within the CPC section on gear transmissions and design, specifically covering gear assemblies that are characterised by the presence of two ring gears. The code applies to configurations where two annular gears are incorporated, typically meshing with planet gears in planetary or epicyclic gear sets.
[F16H 2048/106] characterised by two sun gears -- F16H 2048/106 designates epicyclic or planetary gear arrangements that incorporate two sun gears, typically driving separate gear trains or providing multiple output speeds. The two sun gears may be fixed, rotating independently, or coupled to achieve compound motion within the same gear housing.
[F16H 2048/201] with means directly braking the orbital gears -- The CPC subclass F16H 2048/201 pertains to devices that provide direct braking of orbital gears, typically by means integrated into the gear housing or shaft to apply a resisting force without intermediate transmission elements. It covers mechanical, hydraulic or pneumatic mechanisms that engage the gears themselves to stop or control rotation.
[F16H 2048/202] using freewheel clutches -- The subclass F16H 2048/202 covers devices that incorporate freewheel clutches to permit rotation in one direction while preventing motion in the opposite direction, often employed in gearboxes, drives and power-transmission systems. It includes arrangements, control methods and applications of such clutches in mechanical or electromechanical assemblies.
[F16H 2048/204] Control of arrangements for suppressing differential actions -- F16H 2048/204 covers hydraulic or fluid-power systems that incorporate control mechanisms intended to minimise or eliminate differential actions such as pressure pulsations, flow fluctuations or uneven force transmission between circuit branches. It includes devices, valves, dampers or feedback loops designed to stabilise pressure and flow, thereby improving system steadiness and performance.
[F16H 2048/205] using the steering as a control parameter -- F16H 2048/205 covers control systems that employ the steering angle or position itself as a feedback or control variable, such as autopilots, dynamic-positioning or stabilization devices that adjust rudders, thrust units or other actuators based on steering input. It includes methods for using steering data to regulate vessel maneuvering, heading or course-keeping functions.
[F16H 2048/207] using torque sensors -- F16H 2048/207 relates to the integration of torque sensors in valve or sealing assemblies for detecting, measuring or controlling the torque applied to moving parts such as pistons, gates or actuators, enabling precise operation and monitoring of mechanical loads.
[F16H 2048/208] using flywheels -- F16H 2048/208 covers inventions that incorporate flywheels as a means of storing or smoothing mechanical energy, including devices that use the rotating mass for power delivery, load balancing, or regenerative braking. The entry also encompasses control systems and methods for managing flywheel operation.
[F16H 2048/265] with a fluid throttling means -- The CPC subclass F16H 2048/265 refers to fluid-control devices that include a throttling means for adjusting flow rate or pressure, such as valves, regulators or similar components integrated into a fluid system. This subclass is applied when the throttling element is a defining feature of the invention.
[F16H 2048/282] using the axial movement of axially movable bevel gears -- F16H 2048/282 covers devices that exploit the axial displacement of bevel gears that can move along their axis to adjust gear engagement. The classification includes mechanisms where the axial motion of the gears or their supporting shafts is used to vary speed, torque or direction, such as in variable-ratio gearboxes and automatic transmissions.
[F16H 2048/305] using manual actuators -- F16H 2048/305 classifies devices that employ manual actuators - such as levers, cranks, pedals or hand-wheels - to generate the force needed for moving, lifting or positioning a load in a mechanical system. The entry covers the design and operation of these human-powered mechanisms, including the transmission of motion to the work piece or supporting structure.
[F16H 2048/343] using a rotary motor -- F16H 2048/343 covers devices in which a rotary motor is employed to drive a pump, compressor or similar fluid-moving apparatus. The classification applies to arrangements where the motor directly or indirectly provides the rotational power needed for fluid displacement, including coupling, gearing and control features specific to the motor-driven operation.
[F16H 2048/346] using a linear motor -- F16H 2048/346 covers inventions that incorporate a linear motor as the driving source for fluid-power transmissions or motor units, such as pumps, actuators, and hydraulic systems. It includes designs where the linear motor directly generates the required linear motion or force, replacing conventional rotary drives or mechanical linkages. This subclass addresses the integration of linear-motor technology into fluid-power devices.
[F16H 2048/362] using a continuously variable transmission -- F16H 2048/362 classifies inventions that incorporate a continuously variable transmission within hydraulic or fluid-power systems, covering designs where speed or torque is varied smoothly without discrete gear steps. It pertains to the integration of CVT mechanisms to control the output of pumps, motors or actuators in such systems.
[F16H 2048/364] using electric or hydraulic motors -- The subclass F16H 2048/364 covers cylinders, pistons or other linear-acting devices whose motion is generated by electric or hydraulic motors rather than by direct fluid pressure. It includes designs in which an electric motor drives a pump or a hydraulic motor directly powers the rod or shaft, providing motor-driven actuation for fluid-power cylinders.
[F16H 2048/366] using additional non-orbital gears in combination with clutches or brakes -- This subclass covers gear arrangements that incorporate supplementary non-orbital gears used together with a clutch or brake to engage or disengage power transmission, providing variable speed or torque control. It applies to gearboxes where the additional gears mesh on different axes and are controlled by clutch or brake mechanisms.
[F16H 2048/368] using additional orbital gears in combination with clutches or brakes -- F16H 2048/368 pertains to gear systems that employ one or more additional orbital gears together with a clutch or brake, enabling control of power transmission, engagement or disengagement, and variable speed or torque characteristics within a mechanical drive.
[F16H 2048/382] Methods for manufacturing differential gearings -- F16H 2048/382 falls under the CPC class for gear devices and covers manufacturing techniques specific to differential gearings, the pair of gears that split torque between wheels. The code groups processes that form, cut, heat-treat or finish the meshing teeth of such gears, enabling efficient production of automotive and machinery differentials.
[F16H 2048/385] of the ring or crown gear -- This CPC subgroup pertains to the design, geometry and related features of ring or crown gears, covering aspects such as tooth profile, relief, mounting and manufacturing methods specific to these annular gears used in gear trains.
[F16H 2048/387] Shields or washers -- F16H 2048/387 covers shields or washers that are used to protect or separate moving components, typically in hydraulic, pneumatic or mechanical assemblies. The devices may be made of metal-plastic, plastic or composite materials and are designed to absorb wear, limit friction or retain fluid.
[F16H 2048/405] characterised by features of the bearing of the rotating case -- This CPC subclass falls under F16H for fluid-bearing devices and concerns rotating cases that incorporate distinctive bearing features, such as integrated fluid-film or hydrostatic elements that support the case during rotation. It is applied to classify pumps, turbines, compressors and similar machines where the bearing of the rotating case is specially designed.
[F16H 2048/423] characterised by bearing arrangement -- F16H 2048/423 covers valves that incorporate a bearing arrangement to support rotating or moving components such as shafts, journals or spindles. The classification applies to designs where bearings reduce friction, facilitate smooth operation, and improve durability under pressure or flow conditions, often including lubricated or self-aligning bearing systems.
[F16H 2048/426] characterised by spigot bearing arrangement, e.g. bearing for supporting the free end of the drive shaft pinion -- F16H 2048/426 classifies hydraulic or screw-type pumps that incorporate a spigot bearing arrangement, where a bearing is mounted as a protruding spigot to support the free end of the drive-shaft pinion. The feature improves alignment and load transfer between the pinion and pump housing.
[F16H 2049/003] Features of the flexsplines therefor -- This CPC group concerns the particular design characteristics of flexible splines (flexsplines) employed in gear or transmission assemblies. It includes details of tooth geometry, material selection, manufacturing methods, and other structural features that affect performance.
[F16H 2049/006] Wave generators producing a non-elliptical shape of flexsplines, i.e. with a qualified different shape than elliptical -- F16H 2049/006 covers wave generators that form flexsplines with shapes other than an ellipse, such as irregular or specially profiled contours, used to drive fluid or mechanical displacement devices. The classification applies to designs where the spline geometry deviates deliberately from the standard elliptical form.
[F16H 2049/008] Linear wave gearings, i.e. harmonic type gearing imposing a strain wave to a straight flexible member engaging a second member with different pitch to generate linear motion thereof -- This class covers linear wave gearings of the harmonic-type in which a strain wave is imposed on a straight flexible member that engages a second member with a different pitch to generate linear motion of the flexible member.
[F16H 2055/065] Moulded gears, e.g. inserts therefor -- F16H 2055/065 pertains to gear components that are formed by moulding techniques, including moulded gear teeth or inserts that can be placed into gear bodies. The class covers the production, design and use of such moulded gears, often made from metal or polymer materials, for transmission applications.
[F16H 2055/086] Silent gear profiles -- F16H 2055/086 pertains to gear tooth shapes and manufacturing methods specifically designed to minimize acoustic emissions. It includes profiles such as involute or helical modifications, tooth profiling, surface treatments, and geometry adjustments that reduce vibration and noise in gear drives. The classification is used for patents dealing with silent or low-noise gear technologies.
[F16H 2055/0866] Profiles for improving radial engagement of gears, e.g. chamfers on the tips of the teeth -- F16H 2055/0866 covers gear tooth profiles that are shaped to improve radial engagement, such as adding chamfers or other tip modifications. These designs aim to smooth meshing, reduce impact forces and noise, and enhance gear durability.
[F16H 2055/0893] for parallel shaft arrangement of toothed members -- F16H 2055/0893 designates gear couplings having parallel shafts in which the toothed members are arranged side-by-side to transmit torque between the shafts. It covers devices where the teeth of the two gears mesh while the shafts remain parallel, typical in power-transmission units and fluid-driven gearboxes.
[F16H 2055/173] Crown gears, i.e. gears have axially arranged teeth -- F16H 2055/173 covers crown gears, a type of gear whose teeth are arranged axially around a crown-shaped rim, with the gear axis perpendicular to the plane of the teeth. These gears transmit motion between intersecting shafts and are used where a change of rotation direction is required.
[F16H 2055/175] specially adapted for easy repair, e.g. exchange of worn teeth -- This group covers gear components designed for straightforward maintenance, allowing worn teeth to be replaced or exchanged without dismantling the entire mechanism. It includes features that facilitate quick removal and re-installation of individual teeth.
[F16H 2055/176] Ring gears with inner teeth -- F16H 2055/176 covers ring gears whose teeth are cut on the inner circumference of a cylindrical or annular member, allowing power transmission between concentric shafts or between a shaft and a housing. Such gears are used in compact gearboxes, planetary gear sets, and equipment requiring enclosed gear engagement.
[F16H 2055/178] combined with clutch means, e.g. gear with integrated synchroniser clutch -- F16H 2055/178 covers gear units that incorporate clutch mechanisms, such as a synchroniser clutch integrated directly with the gear. It addresses arrangements where the clutch and gear are combined to provide automatic speed matching during gear changes in transmissions.
[F16H 2055/185] using compound gears with coincident teeth of different material, e.g. laminated construction of metal and elastomeric gear layers, where elastic layer is slightly oversized -- The code F16H 2055/185 covers gear assemblies that employ compound teeth formed from two or more materials, typically a metal layer bonded to an elastomeric layer, with the elastic portion made slightly larger than the metal to provide a snug, flexible mesh. This construction is used to improve noise reduction, shock absorption and load distribution in gear drives.
[F16H 2055/281] Cylindrical or half-cylindrical bushings around the rack, e.g. using special wedges to reduce play -- The CPC subclass F16H 2055/281 pertains to gear racks that are fitted with cylindrical or half-cylindrical bushings. These bushings encircle the rack and often incorporate special wedge elements to minimise backlash or play between the rack and its mating pinion, improving accuracy and smoothness of motion.
[F16H 2055/306] with means providing resilience or vibration damping in chain sprocket wheels -- The subclass F16H 2055/306 pertains to chain sprocket wheels equipped with resilience or vibration-damping mechanisms. It includes designs where springs, elastomeric inserts or other shock-absorbing components are integrated into the sprocket to reduce impact and noise during operation.
[F16H 2055/325] characterized by roughness or hardness of friction surface -- The CPC code F16H 2055/325 designates inventions in which the friction surface of a bearing or sliding element is defined by its roughness or hardness. It covers devices whose performance relies on a deliberately textured or hardened contact area to control wear, lubrication or grip.
[F16H 2055/363] with special means or properties for lateral tracking of the flexible members running on the pulley, e.g. with crowning to keep a belt on track -- This subclass pertains to pulleys or drive elements that incorporate special features to control lateral movement of belts or other flexible members, for example crowned or profiled surfaces that keep the belt centered during operation. It includes any means designed to improve belt tracking on the pulley.
[F16H 2055/366] with means providing resilience or vibration damping -- This subclass covers hydraulic or pneumatic actuators and related fluid-power devices that incorporate built-in elastic or damping components to absorb shocks, smooth motion and reduce vibration in the transmitted force.
[F16H 2057/0012] for reducing drive line oscillations -- F16H 2057/0012 covers devices or methods that mitigate oscillations in hydraulic or pneumatic drive lines, such as dampers, accumulators, or control systems that smooth torque fluctuations and reduce vibration during power transmission.
[F16H 2057/0043] Mounting or adjusting transmission parts by robots -- The CPC subclass F16H 2057/0043 covers methods and apparatus for mounting or adjusting transmission components-such as gears, shafts, or couplings-using robotic devices. It includes robotic handling, positioning, and securing operations that automate assembly or maintenance of power-transmission parts.
[F16H 2057/005] Mounting preassembled units, i.e. using pre-mounted structures to speed up final mounting process -- F16H 2057/005 covers techniques that employ preassembled or pre-mounted structural units to accelerate the final installation of a system. It includes methods and devices where components are assembled off-site or beforehand, allowing quicker, more efficient mounting on the ultimate structure. This subclass falls under the broader F16H group dealing with fixed or adjustable building and assembly structures.
[F16H 2057/0056] Mounting parts arranged in special position or by special sequence, e.g. for keeping particular parts in his position during assembly -- F16H 2057/0056 pertains to mounting components that are positioned in a specific arrangement or installed in a particular order to secure other elements while they are being assembled. It includes devices such as clamps, brackets or fixtures designed to hold parts in place during the manufacturing process.
[F16H 2057/0062] Tools specially adapted for assembly of transmissions -- The CPC sub-group F16H 2057/0062 covers devices specifically designed to aid the assembly of transmission units such as gearboxes or drivetrains. It includes alignment fixtures, torque applicators and other specialized tools that facilitate precise fitting and joining of transmission components.
[F16H 2057/0068] Repairing of transmissions by using repair kits -- F16H 2057/0068 pertains to the repair of transmission systems using specially provided repair kits. It covers methods and kits designed to restore, replace or modify gears, shafts, bearings and related components of mechanical transmissions without requiring full disassembly of the entire drive unit. This subclass falls under CPC section F for mechanical engineering, focusing on transmission maintenance and refurbishment.
[F16H 2057/0075] Modifying standard transmissions from manufacturer, e.g. by adding an extension for additional ratios -- F16H 2057/0075 covers alterations to factory-supplied gearboxes or transmission systems that increase the number of gear ratios, typically by adding auxiliary gear units, splitters, over-drive stages or other extensions. The modifications are done after manufacture to provide finer speed steps or expanded performance ranges.
[F16H 2057/0081] Fixing of, or adapting to transmission failure -- F16H 2057/0081 concerns devices or control methods that detect a loss of pressure, flow or power in a hydraulic-pneumatic transmission line and automatically isolate, bypass or compensate for the failure to maintain operation. It includes specialised valves, sensors and circuit arrangements designed to adapt to transmission breakdowns in fluid-power systems.
[F16H 2057/0087] Computer aided design [CAD] specially adapted for gearing features; Analysis of gear systems -- F16H 2057/0087 covers computer-aided design software specially configured for creating and analyzing gearing elements, including generation of gear geometry, meshing and performance evaluation. The entry includes tools for simulation, stress analysis and optimization of gear trains.
[F16H 2057/0093] Means or measures for transport, shipping or packaging -- F16H 2057/0093 is a CPC subgroup under mechanical engineering for ships and floating structures that covers devices, arrangements or methods used to move, ship or package goods. It includes handling equipment such as conveyors, pallets, cradles, loading frames and other transport or packaging measures for cargo on vessels.
[F16H 2057/012] of gearings -- F16H 2057/012 relates to gearings, describing the design, arrangement and meshing of gears that transmit torque and motion between rotating shafts or other rotating components.
[F16H 2057/014] of friction elements in transmissions -- F16H 2057/014 covers friction elements employed in transmission units, such as clutches, brakes or other friction-based coupling devices. It relates to the design, material composition and surface characteristics of these components that enable controlled transmission of power through frictional engagement. This subclass addresses specific shapes, treatments or configurations that improve performance or durability in mechanical transmissions.
[F16H 2057/016] Monitoring of overload conditions -- F16H 2057/016 falls under the Cooperative Patent Classification for fluid-power systems and concerns devices or methods that detect or monitor overload conditions, such as excessive pressure, flow, or force in hydraulic or pneumatic circuits, and that provide signals, alarms or protective actions to prevent damage.
[F16H 2057/018] Detection of mechanical transmission failures -- The CPC class F16H 2057/018 covers technologies for detecting failures in mechanical power-transmission systems, such as gears, shafts, couplings and hydraulic or pneumatic drives. It includes sensors, monitoring methods and diagnostic algorithms that identify leaks, wear, mis-alignment, overload or other fault conditions in the transmission's mechanical components.
[F16H 2057/02008] characterised by specific dividing lines or planes of the gear case -- The CPC subclass F16H 2057/02008 covers gear cases whose design is defined by distinct dividing lines or planes, such as internal sections, seams or partition surfaces that segment the housing for assembly, maintenance or modular construction. This classification is used for inventions that specify these geometric divisions to improve strength, accessibility or integration of gear components.
[F16H 2057/02013] Extension units for gearboxes, e.g. additional units attached to a main gear -- The CPC subclass F16H 2057/02013 covers extension units for gearboxes, namely auxiliary gear units that are coupled to a primary gear train to broaden the operating range, increase torque capacity or provide additional speed ratios. These devices are typically mounted on or integrated with the main gearbox and function as extra stages that augment the transmission's capabilities.
[F16H 2057/02017] characterised by special features related to the manufacturing of the gear case, e.g. special adaptations for casting -- The CPC subclass F16H 2057/02017 covers gear casings that incorporate distinctive manufacturing features, particularly those that involve special adaptations for casting processes. It includes design elements aimed at improving casting quality, reducing defects, or facilitating moulding of gear housings. This subclass helps identify inventions that focus on the production technique rather than the gear function itself.
[F16H 2057/02021] with means for adjusting alignment -- The CPC subclass F16H 2057/02 covers mechanisms that incorporate features for adjusting the alignment of rotating elements such as shafts, spindles, or couplings. It includes devices that allow precise positioning or angular correction of these components to maintain proper mechanical relationship and reduce stress during operation. This subclass is used for inventions where alignment-adjustment means are integral to the lubrication, sealing or related functions of machinery.
[F16H 2057/02026] Connection of auxiliaries with a gear case; Mounting of auxiliaries on the gearbox -- This CPC subgroup concerns the mechanical means for attaching auxiliary units-such as pumps, motors, or sensors-to a gear case, detailing how the auxiliaries are connected and mounted directly on the gearbox housing to ensure secure integration and proper alignment.
[F16H 2057/0203] the gearbox is associated or combined with a crank case of an engine -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates housing components (27), (28) and (38) are welded together to form a common housing that houses both the crank of the engine and the belt-type CVT.
[F16H 2057/02034] Gearboxes combined or connected with electric machines -- The CPC subclass F16H 2057/02034 covers gearboxes that are integrated with or directly coupled to electric machines such as motors or generators. It includes designs where the gearbox and the electric drive are combined in a single unit to transmit power, adjust speed or torque, or provide compact power-train solutions. This subclass addresses both mechanical and electromechanical aspects of the combined assembly.
[F16H 2057/02039] Gearboxes for particular applications -- F16H 2057/02039 covers gearboxes that are specially designed for a defined application rather than generic use. It includes power-transmission units adapted to particular operating conditions, load requirements or environmental constraints such as automotive drivetrain, marine propulsion, aerospace or industrial machinery.
[F16H 2057/02043] for vehicle transmissions -- F16H 2057/02 covers gear transmission systems designed for vehicles, including gearboxes, gear arrangements and related components that transfer power from an engine to the wheels. The entry applies to both road and off-road vehicle drivetrains and to any mechanisms that couple, reduce or alter rotational speed for vehicle propulsion.
[F16H 2057/02047] Automatic transmissions -- The CPC subclass F16H 2057/02047 covers automatic transmissions, i.e., gear-changing mechanisms that shift without direct driver input, typically using hydraulic, pneumatic or electronic control systems. It includes designs for vehicle drivetrains where gear ratios are selected automatically based on speed, load or engine parameters. This subclass groups inventions that improve shifting efficiency, reliability or integration with engine management.
[F16H 2057/02052] Axle units; Transfer casings for four wheel drive -- The class F16H 2057/02052 covers the design of transfer casings and axle units used in four-wheel-drive systems, detailing the housing that splits power from the drivetrain to the front and rear axles and the integrated components that enable all-wheel propulsion.
[F16H 2057/02056] for utility vehicles, e.g. tractors or agricultural machines -- F16H 2057/02056 is a CPC subgroup that covers devices, mechanisms or control systems specially designed for utility vehicles such as tractors and other agricultural machines. It includes inventions related to the operation, handling, or auxiliary functions of these vehicles, focusing on features that differentiate them from passenger or commercial vehicle technology.
[F16H 2057/0206] for commercial vehicles, e.g. buses or trucks -- F16H 2057/0206 covers gear units and transmission arrangements specifically designed for use in commercial road vehicles such as buses and trucks. It includes gear types, configurations and mounting solutions intended to meet the power-train requirements of heavy-duty vehicle applications.
[F16H 2057/02065] for motorcycles or squads -- F16H 2057/02065 covers hydraulic valve gear and control mechanisms specially adapted for use in motorcycles or small squad-type vehicles, focusing on fluid-power transmission and direction control within the vehicle's propulsion or braking system.
[F16H 2057/02069] for industrial applications -- F16H 2057/02069 is a CPC sub-group under class F16H (pumps) that specifies a particular pump design or configuration intended for industrial use, such as manufacturing, chemical processing, or energy conversion. It narrows the broader pump category to applications in industrial settings.
[F16H 2057/02073] Reduction gearboxes for industry -- The CPC subclass F16H 2057/02073 covers reduction gearboxes designed for industrial applications, describing gear sets that lower output speed while raising torque for use in machines such as conveyors, mixers and presses. It includes specific configurations, mounting arrangements and performance criteria relevant to heavy-duty manufacturing equipment.
[F16H 2057/02078] for wind turbines -- This subclass groups patents that describe mechanical and structural elements of wind-turbine rotors, including blade-hub connections, shaft and bearing housings, and coupling arrangements that transmit rotation to the drive train. It covers designs aimed at protecting bearings, ensuring alignment and facilitating load transfer specific to wind-turbine operation.
[F16H 2057/02082] for application in vehicles other than propelling, e.g. adjustment of parts -- The CPC subclass F16H 2057/02082 concerns fluid-moving devices-hydraulic or pneumatic pumps, compressors, and related mechanisms-employed in vehicles for functions other than propulsion, such as steering, suspension, braking or other part-adjustment systems. It covers the design and use of such devices to control or modify vehicle components in cars, trucks, aircraft and similar transport machines.
[F16H 2057/02086] Measures for reducing size of gearbox, e.g. for creating a more compact transmission casing -- This CPC subgroup covers inventions that aim to minimise the overall dimensions of a gearbox, for example by designing more compact transmission housings, integrating functions, using modular or stacked gear arrangements, or employing lightweight materials and novel mounting methods to achieve a smaller footprint.
[F16H 2057/02091] Measures for reducing weight of gearbox -- The CPC subclass F16H 2057/02091 covers inventions aimed at decreasing the mass of gearboxes, including lightweight materials, structural optimizations, and design modifications that reduce overall weight while maintaining performance. It falls within the F16H group dealing with gearbox constructions and components.
[F16H 2057/02095] Measures for reducing number of parts or components -- F16H 2057/02095 covers inventions that aim to simplify mechanical assemblies by lowering the number of separate parts or components. It includes design features, integrated functions, or structural modifications that combine tasks previously performed by multiple elements, thereby reducing complexity, weight, cost and assembly time. This subclass is used to classify mechanisms that achieve part reduction through consolidation, modularity or multifunctional elements.
[F16H 2057/0213] Support of worm gear shafts -- The CPC subclass F16H 2057/0213 covers means for supporting the shafts of worm gears, such as bearings, housings, and mounting arrangements that keep the shafts correctly aligned and transmit loads between the worm and its driven gear. It includes designs that improve stability, reduce wear and maintain precise gear positioning.
[F16H 2057/0216] Intermediate shaft supports, e.g. by using a partition wall -- The code F16H 2057/0216 designates mechanisms that support an intermediate shaft within a machine, typically by attaching it to a partition wall or similar structural element. Such supports transmit and bear loads between the main and driven shafts, improving alignment and stability of the power transmission system.
[F16H 2057/0221] Axial adjustment -- The code F16H 2057/0221 covers mechanisms that allow axial movement or positioning of components within fluid-power systems. It includes devices such as adjustable pistons, cylinders, and mounting structures where the distance along the axis can be varied to alter stroke, clearance or alignment. The classification is used for patents describing these axial-adjustment features.
[F16H 2057/0222] Lateral adjustment -- F16H 2057/0222 relates to fasteners, screws or other securing elements that provide a means for lateral adjustment. It covers devices whose design allows side-to-side movement of the attached parts for alignment, positioning or compensation of tolerances, such as adjustable clamps, offset-thread screws or mechanisms that shift a fastened component sideways while remaining secured.
[F16H 2057/0224] using eccentric bushes -- F16H 2057/0224 covers the use of eccentric bushes in hydraulic or pneumatic power units, where an off-center sleeve is employed to vary the stroke or timing of pistons, rods or other moving parts. The feature is applied to pumps, compressors or valve mechanisms to provide adjustable displacement, phase shift or load balancing.
[F16H 2057/0225] with means for adjusting alignment -- The CPC entry F16H 2057/0225 covers dampers, ventilators or similar flow-control devices that include a mechanism for adjusting the alignment of the damper element relative to the surrounding structure. The adjustment means may be manual or powered, such as screws, gears, levers or hydraulic actuators, enabling precise positioning to control sealing or flow rate.
[F16H 2057/0227] Assembly method measuring first tolerances or position and selecting mating parts accordingly, e.g. special sized shims for transmission bearings -- This subclass covers assembly methods that first assess dimensional tolerances or component positions and then select corresponding mating parts, for example using specially sized shims to fit transmission bearings and achieve the desired clearance.
[F16H 2057/0228] Mounting with rough tolerances and fine adjustment after assembly -- F16H 2057/0228 relates to mounting devices that allow a component to be placed with generous initial tolerances and then precisely positioned using adjustment mechanisms after assembly. It covers systems that combine coarse alignment features with fine-tuning elements such as screws, shims or spring-loaded parts to achieve accurate positioning.
[F16H 2057/0235] specially adapted to allow easy accessibility and repair -- F16H 2057/0235 classifies load-handling equipment such as winches, hoists and related drive units that are specially adapted to permit easy accessibility and repair. It includes designs with removable panels, modular components, quick-release fastenings or other features that simplify inspection, maintenance or replacement of parts.
[F16H 2057/0325] Moulded casings made from plastic -- F16H 2057/0325 falls under the CPC group for housings, casings and covers of fluid-power machines, specifying moulded casings made from plastic. It covers the design, manufacture and use of polymeric shells that protect or contain components of pumps, compressors or hydraulic actuators.
[F16H 2057/0335] Series transmissions of modular design, e.g. providing for different transmission ratios or power ranges -- F16H 2057/0335 covers gear or other series transmissions built as modular units that can be combined or re-configured to obtain different transmission ratios or to handle varying power levels, allowing flexible adaptation of speed and torque in mechanical drive systems.
[F16H 2057/085] Bearings for orbital gears -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Bearings of orbital gears, i.e. sliding and/or rolling contact bearings supporting orbital gears on the carrier.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  1a.  Figure 1a illustrates a planet carrier (2) and planet gear (6), including a needle bearing (5) as a rolling contact bearing, and left and right thrust washers (8) as sliding contact bearings which support the planet gear (6) on a planet pin (4) of the planet carrier (2).  1b.
[F16H 2057/087] Arrangement and support of friction devices in planetary gearings, e.g. support of clutch drums, stacked arrangements of friction devices -- This subclass covers how friction components such as clutch drums are positioned and supported within planetary gear systems, including configurations where multiple friction devices are stacked or integrated to transmit torque. It deals with mechanical arrangements that ensure proper engagement and bearing of friction elements in planetary gear trains.
[F16H 2057/121] using parallel torque paths and means to twist the two path against each other -- The subclass relates to power-transmission arrangements that employ two parallel torque paths whose relative twist is used to modify or balance the transmitted torque. It covers mechanisms that deliberately twist the two paths against each other to achieve load sharing, direction change or speed variation in gear or power-take-off systems.
[F16H 2057/122] by using two independent drive sources, e.g. electric motors -- F16H 2057/122 covers fluid-handling devices that employ two separate drive units, such as electric motors, to power the pump or hydraulic system. The dual independent sources provide redundancy, variable speed control or combined output for improved performance and reliability.
[F16H 2057/123] using electric control means -- F16H 2057/123 covers fluid-control devices that are operated by electric control means, such as electrically driven actuators, solenoid or electronic valves, and circuitry that modulate pressure, flow or volume. It is a subcategory of CPC F16H, which addresses devices for regulating fluid pressure, volume, flow or temperature.
[F16H 2057/125] Adjustment of backlash during mounting or assembly of gearing -- The classification covers mechanisms or procedures for correcting gear backlash while a gear train is being mounted or assembled, such as adjustable shims, eccentric sleeves, or fine-tuning devices that allow precise spacing adjustments to achieve the desired mesh clearance.
[F16H 2057/126] Self-adjusting during operation, e.g. by a spring -- F16H 2057/126 covers hydraulic or fluid-power devices that automatically modify their operating parameters while in use, typically by means of a spring-loaded mechanism that compensates for load or pressure changes. The subclass applies to self-adjusting pumps, valves, actuators and similar components that continuously adapt during operation without external control.
[F16H 2057/127] using springs -- F16H 2057/127 classifies inventions that employ springs to supply force, store energy, or generate motion within a device, covering the design, arrangement and functional use of springs in mechanical systems. It includes methods of integrating springs for load-bearing, shock absorption, actuation or timing purposes.
[F16H 2057/128] using axial positioning of gear wheel with addendum modification on gear width, i.e. backlash is compensated by axial positioning of a slightly conical gear wheel -- The code F16H 2057/128 relates to gear assemblies in which backlash is reduced by adjusting the axial position of a gear wheel that has an altered addendum profile across its width. The modification creates a slight conical shape, allowing axial displacement to fine-tune tooth engagement and improve transmission accuracy.
[F16H 2059/003] Detecting or using driving style of a driver, e.g. for adapting shift schedules -- F16H 2059/003 covers technologies that detect a driver's operating style-such as acceleration patterns, braking habits or gear changes-and use that information to modify or optimise work-related parameters, for example by adjusting shift schedules or vehicle settings to match the identified driving behaviour.
[F16H 2059/006] Overriding automatic control -- This subclass relates to devices or methods that permit a manual or secondary control signal to supersede an automatic control function in fluid-power or hydraulic systems, providing a safety or redundancy mechanism that can interrupt or replace the automatic regulation.
[F16H 2059/0221] for selecting modes, e.g. sport, normal, economy -- F16H 2059/0221 covers devices that enable the user to choose among different operating modes of a machine, such as sport, normal or economy, typically via switches, selectors or electronic controls that adjust performance parameters in automotive or other fluid-power systems.
[F16H 2059/0226] for selecting particular shift speeds, e.g. a fast shift speed with aggressive gear change -- The entry refers to transmission gear-shift mechanisms that include control logic for selecting particular shift velocities, such as a rapid, aggressive gear change. It covers devices or methods that adjust timing and torque to achieve fast, forceful gear engagements.
[F16H 2059/023] Selectors for gearings using voice control -- F16H 2059/023 relates to selection devices for gear transmissions that are operated by spoken commands. It covers voice-activated control units, microphones and processing circuits that choose gear ratios or engage gears in mechanical drives. The class is part of CPC group F16H concerning control devices for machines and power transmission.
[F16H 2059/0234] Selectors for gearings using foot control -- F16H 2059/0234 covers foot-actuated selector mechanisms for gearboxes, including pedals, levers and linkage systems that enable a user to change gear ratios by foot pressure. The classification applies to devices that translate foot movement into gear-selection actions in automotive, industrial or other mechanical transmission systems.
[F16H 2059/0239] Up- and down-shift or range or mode selection by repeated movement -- The Cooperative Patent Classification code F16H 2059/0239 pertains to mechanisms for up- and down-shifting, or selecting ranges or modes in transmission systems through repeated movements. This classification is used by the European Patent Office (EPO) and the United States Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO).
[F16H 2059/0243] with push buttons, e.g. shift buttons arranged on steering wheel -- A device for motor vehicles where push-type controls, for example shift buttons, are mounted on the steering wheel to operate functions such as gear selection or auxiliary systems. This subclass covers arrangements that integrate the buttons directly into the wheel rim or hub for driver-hand operation.
[F16H 2059/0247] with lever or paddle behind steering wheel -- F16H 2059/0247 covers steering-control arrangements that use a lever or paddle mounted behind the steering wheel, such as paddle shifters, column-mounted gear selectors or other actuators that the driver operates without removing the hands from the wheel. The classification applies to mechanisms that translate the lever or paddle movement into vehicle control functions while the wheel remains in place.
[F16H 2059/0252] with means for initiating skip or double gear shifts, e.g. by moving selection lever beyond a threshold -- The subclass covers transmissions that include a mechanism for initiating a skip or double gear shift, such as by moving the selector lever beyond a defined threshold. It pertains to gearboxes that enable rapid multi-step shifting through a lever actuation that exceeds a preset position.
[F16H 2059/0256] Levers for forward-reverse selection only, e.g. for working machines having a separate lever for switching between forward and reverse mode -- F16H 2059/0256 pertains to levers that are used solely to select forward or reverse motion, typically found on working machines that employ a distinct lever for switching between forward and reverse operation. The classification covers the mechanical arrangement and functional design of such single-purpose selection levers.
[F16H 2059/026] Details or special features of the selector casing or lever support -- The Cooperative Patent Classification (CPC) code F16H 2059/026 pertains to specific aspects of the design and construction of selector casings or lever supports in mechanical transmission systems, focusing on details that enhance functionality or durability.
[F16H 2059/0265] Selector lever support with pivot axis offset, e.g. support by four bar linkage to create pivoting centre outside the mechanism -- This subclass covers selector levers whose support places the pivot axis away from the lever itself, often by using a four-bar linkage or similar arrangement that moves the pivot centre outside the main mechanism. It addresses designs that achieve offset lever motion for improved ergonomics or space saving.
[F16H 2059/0269] Ball joints or spherical bearings for supporting the lever -- The CPC subclass F16H 2059/0269 concerns ball joints or spherical bearings used to support a lever, such as in linkages, control arms or pivoting devices. It covers designs that permit angular movement while transmitting loads between the lever and its supporting structure, including both integrated and separate bearing arrangements in mechanical systems.
[F16H 2059/0273] Cardan or gimbal type joints for supporting the lever -- The CPC subclass F16H 2059/0273 pertains to cardan or gimbal type joints that support a lever, including universal joints and gimbal assemblies that permit angular movement while transmitting torque. These joints are employed where a lever must be guided or pivoted despite limited misalignment.
[F16H 2059/0282] Lever handles with lock mechanisms, e.g. for allowing selection of reverse gear or releasing lever from park position -- This subclass covers lever handles that incorporate lock mechanisms to control gear selection or to release a lever from a parked position, such as reverse-gear locks or park-release devices in vehicle transmissions.
[F16H 2059/0286] with range or splitter selector on selector lever -- This CPC subgroup deals with hydraulic or pneumatic control devices in which the selector lever includes a range or splitter selector, allowing the operator to choose between different flow or pressure settings. It applies to machines that use such a lever to switch among multiple operating ranges, often for variable speed, torque or pressure control.
[F16H 2059/0291] comprising safety means for preventing injuries in case of accidents -- F16H 2059/0291 covers devices that incorporate safety features designed to reduce or prevent personal injury when a mechanical system fails or is involved in an accident, such as guards, sensors, emergency shut-offs, or other protective mechanisms integrated into the apparatus.
[F16H 2059/0295] with mechanisms to return lever to neutral or datum position, e.g. by return springs -- F16H 2059/0295 covers control devices in which a lever is automatically returned to its neutral or datum position by means of return springs or similar mechanisms. The classification applies to systems that incorporate spring-loaded features to reset levers after actuation.
[F16H 2059/047] with essentially straight linear movement for gear selection, e.g. straight selection movement using detent mechanism for improving feeling -- This subclass covers gear-selection mechanisms that operate with an essentially straight linear movement rather than a rotary motion, often incorporating a detent or similar device that creates discrete positions and a tactile feel for the operator when selecting gears.
[F16H 2059/048] with means for unlocking select or shift movement to allow access to reverse gear position -- The subclass concerns gear-shift mechanisms that incorporate a device for unlocking or permitting a selective movement so the operator can engage the reverse gear. It covers systems where a lock or interlock is released to allow the shift lever or selector to reach the reverse position, typically found in automotive transmissions.
[F16H 2059/065] Inching pedals for setting the ratio of a hydrostatic transmission -- The subclass F16H 2059/065 covers inching or control pedals that are used to vary the gear-ratio setting of a hydrostatic transmission, allowing precise speed adjustments such as slow forward or reverse motion. It includes mechanisms that link pedal travel to the transmission's displacement control.
[F16H 2059/081] using knops or discs for rotary range selection -- This CPC subgroup relates to rotary selectors that employ a knob or disc to choose a specific range of motion or setting, such as adjusting speed, flow, or position in a mechanical control device. The design lets the user rotate the element to predefined positions to alter the operating condition.
[F16H 2059/082] for different transmission modes -- This CPC subclass covers hydraulic or pneumatic transmission devices that can operate in different transmission modes, such as forward/reverse, variable speed/torque, or selectable pressure settings, allowing the system to adapt performance to varying operating conditions.
[F16H 2059/083] Overdrive or overdrive cut-off -- F16H 2059/083 pertains to mechanisms that interrupt or disengage an overdrive gear in a transmission, including cut-off clutches, hydraulic or pneumatic valves and related control devices that stop power flow to the overdrive to improve fuel economy or protect the drivetrain.
[F16H 2059/084] Economy mode -- The CPC entry F16H 2059/084 relates to control strategies that place heating, ventilation, or air-conditioning installations into a low-energy or reduced-performance operating state to save energy, often by limiting temperature set-points, reducing fan speed, or scheduling downtime. It is used to classify inventions that implement an economy or energy-saving mode in climate-control systems.
[F16H 2059/085] Power mode -- F16H 2059/085 covers features for controlling the power mode of hydraulic pumps, motors or similar fluid-power units, such as switching between active, reduced-power or standby states to optimise energy consumption during operation.
[F16H 2059/086] Adaptive mode, e.g. learning from the driver -- The CPC code F16H 2059/086 covers vehicle control systems that can automatically adjust their operating mode by learning from the driver's behavior, using adaptive algorithms or learning functions. It includes technologies such as driver-assistance features that modify performance based on observed driving patterns.
[F16H 2059/087] Winter mode, e.g. to start on snow or slippery surfaces -- F16H 2059/087 covers vehicle or machinery control systems that provide a special winter mode for starting on snow, ice or other slippery surfaces, typically by adjusting engine torque, gear selection or traction-control functions to improve grip and prevent wheel slip during initial movement. It includes methods for detecting low-traction conditions and automatically switching to a mode that enhances start-up performance in winter environments.
[F16H 2059/088] Fast forward-reverse-sequence, e.g. rocking mode -- The code covers hydraulic or pneumatic actuators that operate with a rapid forward-reverse motion, often described as a rocking mode, to generate a pulsating fluid flow. Such mechanisms are used in pumps or fluid-handling systems where quick directional changes improve mixing, agitation or displacement.
[F16H 2059/142] of driving resistance calculated from weight, slope, or the like -- The entry addresses systems or calculations that determine the driving resistance of a moving vehicle or machine by using data such as its weight, the grade of the road or incline, and related parameters, typically for performance assessment, control or energy consumption estimation.
[F16H 2059/144] characterised by change between positive and negative drive line torque, e.g. torque changes when switching between coasting and acceleration -- The Cooperative Patent Classification entry F16H 2059/144 pertains to systems or methods that feature a transition between positive and negative drive line torque, such as the torque fluctuations occurring during shifts from coasting to acceleration in vehicles.
[F16H 2059/145] being a function of power demand of auxiliary devices -- This subclass covers mechanisms or control circuits that modify the operation of a primary system based on the power demand of auxiliary devices, including methods for monitoring auxiliary load and adjusting drive or transmission parameters such as speed, torque or power output accordingly.
[F16H 2059/147] Transmission input torque, e.g. measured or estimated engine torque -- This subclass covers devices, sensors and methods that determine the torque applied to a transmission, typically by measuring or estimating the engine output. It includes torque transducers, pressure-based calculations and electronic estimation techniques used for control, diagnostics or performance monitoring of power-train systems.
[F16H 2059/148] Transmission output torque, e.g. measured or estimated torque at output drive shaft -- F16H 2059/148 concerns the determination of torque delivered by a transmission to its output shaft, covering methods for measuring or estimating the output drive-shaft torque that the gearbox supplies to the driven equipment. It is used to classify devices or systems that monitor or calculate this torque for control, diagnostics or performance assessment.
[F16H 2059/183] Rate of change of accelerator position, i.e. pedal or throttle change gradient -- F16H 2059/183 relates to control devices that detect or regulate how quickly an accelerator pedal or electronic throttle moves, measuring the change-gradient of the input. It is used in vehicle propulsion systems to optimise response, fuel use and emissions by managing the rate of accelerator position change.
[F16H 2059/186] Coasting -- F16H 2059/186 covers pump designs that incorporate a coasting function, allowing the rotor or impeller to continue turning for a short period after power is removed. The feature smooths shutdown, reduces mechanical shock and helps maintain flow or pressure during transient conditions.
[F16H 2059/363] Rate of change of input shaft speed, e.g. of engine or motor shaft -- F16H 2059/363 covers inventions that monitor, control or adjust the rate at which the speed of an input shaft-such as an engine or motor-changes, including acceleration and deceleration, in hydraulic or fluid-power systems. It addresses devices or methods that detect or regulate these speed-change dynamics to improve performance or safety.
[F16H 2059/366] Engine or motor speed -- This subclass covers inventions relating to measuring, controlling, or indicating the speed of engines or motors, including sensors, tachometers, and speed-control devices. It includes methods for detecting rotational speed, devices for displaying speed, and systems for regulating speed in various power plants.
[F16H 2059/385] Turbine speed -- F16H 2059/385 relates to the regulation and control of turbine rotational speed, encompassing speed-governors, monitoring sensors, and feedback mechanisms used to maintain or adjust the speed of hydraulic, steam or other turbine types during operation.
[F16H 2059/405] Rate of change of output shaft speed or vehicle speed -- The CPC subclass F16H 2059/405 covers inventions that monitor, control or modify the rate at which an output shaft or a vehicle changes its speed, including acceleration, deceleration and related feedback mechanisms.
