It includes control algorithms, sensors, actuators and transmission systems designed to regulate speed change for safety, efficiency or performance.
[F16H 2059/425] Rate of change of input or turbine shaft speed -- The CPC subclass F16H 2059/425 pertains to inventions that monitor or control how rapidly an input shaft or turbine shaft speed changes, encompassing speed-governors, acceleration sensors and control methods that adjust operation based on the rate of speed variation.
[F16H 2059/443] Detecting travel direction, e.g. the forward or reverse movement of the vehicle -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Input being a function of the detected vehicle travel direction, and the detected travel direction is based on vehicle speed.
[F16H 2059/446] Detecting vehicle stop, i.e. the vehicle is at stand still, e.g. for engaging parking lock -- The code relates to systems and methods that sense when a vehicle has come to a complete stop and can trigger actions such as activating a parking lock. It includes speed sensors, brake-pressure detectors, inertial devices or control logic that determine zero velocity, and the integration of these signals with parking-brake or other safety functions.
[F16H 2059/462] Detecting synchronisation, i.e. speed difference is approaching zero -- This CPC subclass F16H 2059/462 covers methods and devices for detecting that two rotating components are becoming synchronized, meaning their relative speed difference approaches zero. It includes sensors, algorithms or control systems that monitor phase or speed alignment in machinery such as gears, shafts, turbines or motors to ensure proper timing and prevent damage.
[F16H 2059/465] Detecting slip, e.g. clutch slip ratio -- F16H 2059/465 covers technologies for monitoring slip between rotating components, such as measuring the clutch slip ratio to assess relative speed differences during power transmission. The classification includes sensors, signal-processing methods and control strategies that detect, quantify or compensate for slip in automotive, industrial or other drive systems.
[F16H 2059/467] of torque converter -- F16H 2059/467 is a CPC subclass within the broader F16H group that deals with fluid-driven turbines, pumps and related power-transmission devices; this specific entry covers components or arrangements of torque converters, the hydraulic mechanism that transmits rotating power by fluid coupling.
[F16H 2059/503] Axle-load distribution -- The CPC subclass F16H 2059/503 relates to mechanisms and methods for distributing loads among the axles of a vehicle or similar platform. It covers devices such as load-balancing systems, axle-load regulators, and control strategies that ensure even weight allocation to improve stability, handling, and tire wear.
[F16H 2059/506] Wheel slip -- F16H 2059/506 pertains to systems and methods that detect, monitor, or control wheel slip in wheeled vehicles, including traction control, anti-skid devices, and algorithms that adjust braking or engine torque to maintain optimal grip. It covers both mechanical and electronic implementations for improving vehicle safety and handling.
[F16H 2059/525] the machine undergoing additional towing load, e.g. by towing a trailer -- F16H 2059/525 covers machines designed to transmit power while bearing an additional towing load, such as a vehicle that pulls a trailer, and includes features that allow the main machine to handle the extra forces and deliver power to the towed load.
[F16H 2059/605] Traffic stagnation information, e.g. traffic jams -- The CPC subclass F16H 2059/605 covers systems and methods for providing information on traffic stagnation, such as traffic jams, to road users. It includes devices that detect, transmit, or display congestion data to assist navigation and traffic management.
[F16H 2059/663] Road slope -- F16H 2059/663 designates patents dealing with the gradient or slope of roadways, covering methods and structures for constructing, stabilising, or managing inclined road sections to ensure safe vehicle operation and effective drainage.
[F16H 2059/666] Determining road conditions by using vehicle location or position, e.g. from global navigation systems [GPS] -- The entry F16H 2059/666 in the Cooperative Patent Classification system, used by the EPO and USPTO, pertains to methods for determining road conditions through the use of a vehicle's location or position data, often sourced from global navigation satellite systems like GPS.
[F16H 2059/6807] Status of gear-change operation, e.g. clutch fully engaged -- The subclass covers mechanisms that monitor or indicate the condition of a gear-change operation, for example systems that sense when the clutch is fully engaged or when a gear shift has been completed. It includes sensors, indicators and control devices that provide status information about the transmission during gear changes.
[F16H 2059/6815] Post shift value of gearing, i.e. calculated or estimated parameters after shift is completed, e.g. estimated output torque after shift is performed -- The CPC subclass F16H 2059/6815 covers information on the post-shift performance of gear systems, specifically the calculation or estimation of parameters after a gear shift is completed such as the resulting output torque, speed or efficiency. It addresses methods and data used to predict the gearing's behaviour once the shift has taken place, providing engineers with expected values for design, control or diagnostic purposes.
[F16H 2059/6823] Sensing neutral state of the transmission -- This subclass covers devices or methods for detecting that a mechanical transmission is in neutral, such as sensors that determine gear position or the absence of gear engagement. It includes systems used in automotive or other power-transmission applications for control, monitoring or safety functions.
[F16H 2059/683] Sensing pressure in control systems or in fluid-controlled devices, e.g. by pressure sensors -- This CPC subgroup comprises inventions that detect or monitor pressure within control loops or fluid-driven mechanisms, typically employing pressure transducers, strain gauges, or other sensing elements. It includes devices that convert pressure information into electrical or hydraulic signals for use in regulation, safety, or feedback functions.
[F16H 2059/6838] Sensing gearing status of hydrostatic transmissions -- The entry covers devices and methods for detecting the operating condition of the gear set in a hydrostatic transmission, such as position, engagement, speed or pressure, using sensors integrated with the pump or motor to provide feedback for control, monitoring or diagnostic purposes.
[F16H 2059/6846] the flow in hydrostatic transmissions circuits, e.g. high, low or differential pressures -- F16H 2059/6846 covers the regulation and distribution of fluid within hydrostatic transmission systems, describing how fluid is directed through high-pressure, low-pressure or differential-pressure paths to control torque and speed. It includes circuit designs and control methods that manage pressure differentials for efficient power transmission.
[F16H 2059/6853] the state of the transmission units, i.e. motor or pump capacity, e.g. for controlled shifting of range gear -- This CPC subclass pertains to mechanisms that monitor or control the capacity of a transmission's motor or pump in order to manage shift points in a range-gear system. It covers methods and devices that adjust shifting based on the operating state of the transmission unit.
[F16H 2059/6861] the pressures, e.g. high, low or differential pressures -- The code F16H 2059/6861 is applied to inventions that address the type or level of fluid pressure-such as high, low or differential pressures-within hydraulic, pneumatic or other fluid-power systems, covering the specification, measurement, control or use of those pressure conditions.
[F16H 2059/6869] the pump speed -- This CPC subgroup deals with technical features related to the speed of pumps, covering devices and methods for controlling, varying, or measuring pump rotational speed, including variable-speed drives and associated regulation mechanisms.
[F16H 2059/6876] the motor speed -- F16H 2059/6876 covers the control or measurement of the speed of a motor that drives a pump, including devices such as variable-speed drives, regulators and speed-sensing circuits. The entry pertains to methods for adjusting, monitoring or limiting motor speed to achieve desired pump performance or energy efficiency.
[F16H 2059/6884] Sensing or calculating the pump torque -- F16H 2059/6884 covers any device or method that measures or estimates the torque produced by a pump, including torque sensors, transducers and calculation algorithms integrated into the pump system. The subclass applies to instrumentation that provides real-time torque information for control, monitoring or diagnostic purposes.
[F16H 2059/6892] Sensing or calculating the motor torque -- This subclass deals with methods and devices that detect or compute the torque output of an electric motor, either through direct sensing elements such as strain-gauge or torque transducers, or by indirect calculation using electrical, speed or load data, supporting motor control, monitoring and safety functions.
[F16H 2059/702] Rate of change of gear ratio, e.g. for triggering clutch engagement -- The code designates devices or control methods that regulate how quickly a gear ratio varies, typically to coordinate clutch actuation during shifting. It includes mechanisms that monitor and adjust the rate of ratio change to achieve smooth engagement or disengagement. This covers both mechanical and electronic systems that manage the timing and speed of gear-ratio transitions.
[F16H 2059/704] Monitoring gear ratio in CVT's -- The CPC subclass F16H 2059/704 concerns the detection, measurement or supervision of the instantaneous gear ratio in a continuously variable transmission. It covers sensors, electronic control units or diagnostic methods that monitor the ratio to adjust performance, fuel consumption or safety.
[F16H 2059/706] Monitoring gear ratio in stepped transmissions, e.g. by calculating the ratio from input and output speed -- This subclass concerns systems and methods for determining the gear ratio of a stepped transmission by measuring the speeds of the input and output shafts and calculating the ratio, typically for control, diagnostics or performance monitoring. It includes algorithms, sensors and software that evaluate the instantaneous ratio to detect slip, wear or incorrect gearing.
[F16H 2059/708] Sensing reverse gear, e.g. by a reverse gear switch -- The entry covers devices that detect when a vehicle's transmission is in reverse, such as switches or sensors that generate a signal for the vehicle's control systems. These reverse-gear sensors are used for safety alerts, rear-view camera activation, automatic braking and similar functions.
[F16H 2059/725] Sensing or calculating temperature of oil in friction devices, e.g. wet clutches, to prevent overheating of friction linings -- F16H 2059/725 covers methods and devices that sense or calculate the temperature of oil in friction mechanisms such as wet clutches, providing control signals to avoid overheating of the friction linings.
[F16H 2059/743] using engine performance or power for control of gearing -- The subclass covers transmission systems that use engine performance data-such as speed, torque or power output-to control the selection or engagement of gears, including automatic or semi-automatic gearboxes that adjust ratios based on engine operating conditions.
[F16H 2059/746] Engine running state, e.g. on-off of ignition switch -- The CPC subclass F16H 2059/746 pertains to devices or systems that detect, indicate or control the running condition of an engine, such as the on-off status of an ignition switch. It covers methods for monitoring whether the engine is active or idle and for using that information in vehicle control or safety functions. This subclass is part of the broader group dealing with internal-combustion engine control mechanisms.
[F16H 2061/0012] Transmission control for optimising power output of driveline -- F16H 2061/0012 covers systems and methods that regulate a vehicle's transmission to maximize the power delivered by the driveline, typically by adjusting gear selection, shift timing or torque distribution based on operating conditions. It addresses control strategies aimed at optimizing overall power output and efficiency.
[F16H 2061/0015] Transmission control for optimising fuel consumptions -- F16H 2061/0015 covers methods and devices that manage the operation of mechanical transmissions to reduce fuel-consumption. It includes electronic control strategies, shift scheduling, torque converter control and adaptive algorithms that adjust gear changes for optimal efficiency.
[F16H 2061/0018] Transmission control for optimising exhaust emissions -- The CPC class F16H 2061/0018 covers technologies that manage the operation of vehicle transmissions in order to minimise exhaust emissions. It includes control strategies, sensors and software that adjust gear shifting, torque conversion or hydraulic flow to meet emission standards.
[F16H 2061/0034] Accumulators for fluid pressure supply; Control thereof -- F16H 2061/0034 covers devices that store hydraulic or pneumatic energy in accumulators and the mechanisms used to regulate the pressure, flow rate, or discharge of the stored fluid. It includes designs for controlling accumulator charging and releasing cycles in fluid power systems.
[F16H 2061/0037] characterised by controlled fluid supply to lubrication circuits of the gearing -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Supply of lubrication of the transmission is taken from the hydraulic control supply.
[F16H 2061/004] Venting trapped air from hydraulic systems -- The code F16H 2061/004 covers devices or methods for removing trapped air from hydraulic circuits. It includes vent passages, vents, or valves that allow air to escape from cylinders or pipelines to prevent cavitation and maintain system efficiency. The classification is used for inventions that provide means of venting air in hydraulic or pneumatic power installations.
[F16H 2061/0043] Cleaning of hydraulic parts, e.g. removal of an orifice clogging -- F16H 2061/0043 covers methods for cleaning hydraulic components, particularly techniques used to clear blockages in orifices, passages or other flow-restricting parts of a hydraulic system. It includes flushing, back-pressure, chemical or mechanical means to restore proper fluid flow and maintain system performance.
[F16H 2061/0046] Details of fluid supply channels, e.g. within shafts, for supplying friction devices or transmission actuators with control fluid -- The CPC subclass F16H 2061/0046 covers the design and arrangement of fluid-supply channels embedded in shafts that deliver control fluid to friction devices or transmission actuators, detailing their internal routing, dimensions, and integration.
[F16H 2061/005] Supply of electric power, e.g. batteries for back up supply -- The CPC code F16H 2061/005 covers technologies that provide electrical power, especially for emergency or standby use, including battery units and uninterruptible power-supply systems. It is applied to inventions dealing with backup or auxiliary power sources that ensure continuity of operation when the primary supply fails.
[F16H 2061/0053] Initializing the parameters of the controller -- F16H 2061/0053 covers methods and devices for setting the initial parameters of a fluid-power controller, including loading preset values, calibrating sensor inputs and establishing starting conditions before the controller begins operation. It pertains to the initialization procedures that ensure correct performance of hydraulic or pneumatic control systems.
[F16H 2061/0056] Powering down of the controller -- F16H 2061/0056 classifies inventions that provide means to deactivate or reduce power to a controller for pumps, compressors, fans or similar fluid-moving devices. The code covers methods, sensors or circuitry that detect conditions and safely power down the controller to conserve energy or protect the system. It is a sub-category of F16H 2061 dealing specifically with controller shutdown.
[F16H 2061/0062] Modifying an existing transmission control from a manufacturer for improvement or adaptation, e.g. by replacing a valve or an electric part -- F16H 2061/0062 covers the modification or adaptation of an already installed transmission control unit, such as replacing a valve, actuator or electric component to improve performance, functionality or compatibility with other systems. It includes retrofitting, upgrading or reconfiguring the control for specific operational requirements.
[F16H 2061/0065] Modifying or tuning an existing transmission control for racing, e.g. adaptation of valves for very fast shifting -- F16H 2061/0065 covers modifications to transmission control systems aimed at racing performance, including adjustments of valves, actuators or electronic controls to achieve very fast gear shifts. It addresses tuning existing gear-change mechanisms rather than designing entirely new transmissions. This subclass is used for innovations that enhance shift speed and driver response in high-performance vehicles.
[F16H 2061/0068] Method or means for testing of transmission controls or parts thereof -- F16H 2061/0068 covers devices or procedures used to test transmission control components or their sub-assemblies, such as gearsets, clutches, torque converters or electronic control units, to verify performance, durability or diagnostic parameters. The classification applies to both mechanical and electronic testing methods employed in automotive, industrial or similar power-transmission systems.
[F16H 2061/0071] Robots or simulators for testing control functions in automatic transmission -- F16H 2061/0071 covers robots or simulation devices that are employed to test the control functions of automatic transmissions, such as gear-shift logic, clutch engagement and hydraulic regulation. The classification is part of the Cooperative Patent Classification system used by the EPO and USPTO for organizing inventions related to transmission technology.
[F16H 2061/0075] characterised by a particular control method -- The subclass F16H 2061/0075 covers fluid-jet or hydraulic pumps that are distinguished by a specific control method, such as variable-speed drive, feedback regulation, pressure-or flow-based adjustment, or other electronic or mechanical techniques used to manage the pump's performance. It applies to devices where the control strategy is a defining feature of the invention.
[F16H 2061/0078] Linear control, e.g. PID, state feedback or Kalman -- The CPC subclass F16H 2061/0078 covers linear control methods applied to hydraulic or pneumatic power units, including proportional-integral-derivative (PID) regulators, state-feedback designs and Kalman filter techniques for monitoring and adjusting operation.
[F16H 2061/0081] Fuzzy logic -- F16H 2061/0081 covers control devices or systems that employ fuzzy-logic techniques to process imprecise, uncertain, or approximate data. It includes methods, algorithms and hardware that use fuzzy sets and inference rules for decision-making in automation, robotics, process control and similar applications.
[F16H 2061/0084] Neural networks -- F16H 2061/0084 classifies the application of neural-network methods to the monitoring, control or optimisation of pump systems, including algorithms that adjust flow, pressure, speed or detect faults. It covers software or hardware implementations that use artificial-intelligence techniques to improve pump performance or efficiency.
[F16H 2061/0087] Adaptive control, e.g. the control parameters adapted by learning -- This subclass concerns hydraulic or fluid-power systems that incorporate adaptive control, where the controller continuously modifies its parameters based on observed behavior, often using learning or self-tuning algorithms to optimise performance under varying conditions.
[F16H 2061/009] using formulas or mathematic relations for calculating parameters -- The CPC entry F16H 2061/009 covers methods that use mathematical formulas or relations to calculate parameters of fluid-power devices, such as flow rate, pressure, or power. It addresses analytical computation techniques applied in the design, analysis, or control of hydraulic and pneumatic systems.
[F16H 2061/0093] using models to estimate the state of the controlled object -- F16H 2061/0093 relates to pump control techniques that employ mathematical or simulation models to estimate the current state of the pumped system or component. The entry covers model-based observers and estimators used to infer variables such as pressure, flow or wear when direct sensing is unavailable or insufficient.
[F16H 2061/0096] using a parameter map -- Relates to hydraulic-pneumatic control systems that determine valve settings, flow rates or pressure based on a predefined parameter map that links operating conditions to control actions. The map may be stored digitally or implemented mechanically to adjust fluid supply automatically.
[F16H 2061/0209] with independent solenoid valves modulating the pressure individually for each clutch or brake -- The subclass covers fluid-power arrangements that employ separate solenoid-operated valves to adjust pressure for each clutch or brake individually. It applies to systems where independent electronic control of pressure enables precise modulation of multiple friction elements in a transmission or braking unit.
[F16H 2061/0216] Calculation or estimation of post shift values for different gear ratios, e.g. by using engine performance tables -- The code F16H 2061/0216 relates to methods for determining post-shift values in vehicles with multiple gear ratios, such as calculating engine speed, torque or fuel consumption after a gear change. It includes the use of engine performance tables or similar reference data to estimate these values for each possible gear ratio.
[F16H 2061/0218] Calculation or estimation of the available ratio range, i.e. possible gear ratios, e.g. for prompting a driver with a display -- The subclass covers methods and systems that compute or estimate the range of possible gear-ratios in a transmission, typically to inform the driver via a display. It includes algorithms for determining the available ratio range and presenting it for optimal gear selection.
[F16H 2061/022] Calculation or estimation of optimal gear ratio, e.g. best ratio for economy drive or performance according driver preference, or to optimise exhaust emissions -- This CPC subclass covers methods and algorithms for determining the most suitable gear ratio in a vehicle transmission, aimed at achieving optimal fuel economy, performance, driver specific preferences, or reduced exhaust emissions.
[F16H 2061/0223] Generating of new shift maps, i.e. methods for determining shift points for a schedule by taking into account driveline and vehicle conditions -- F16H 2061/0223 covers methods and algorithms for creating new shift maps, i.e., determining optimal gear-shift points in transmission control schedules by analysing driveline and vehicle operating conditions such as speed, torque, load or driver inputs. This subclass addresses adaptive or predictive strategies that adjust shift timing to improve performance, efficiency or emissions.
[F16H 2061/0225] Modifying of shift maps by manual control, e.g. by learning values from the driver during manual shift mode -- The subclass covers vehicle transmission systems that adjust or fine-tune shift maps through driver-initiated input during manual shifting, enabling the control unit to learn preferred gear-change points. It includes methods for updating shift-map data based on real-time manual control.
[F16H 2061/0227] Shift map selection, i.e. methods for controlling selection between different shift maps, e.g. to initiate switch to a map for up-hill driving -- F16H 2061/0227 covers methods for selecting shift maps in vehicle transmissions, enabling the control system to switch between predefined gear-shift strategies such as an uphill driving map. It includes algorithms and sensor inputs that trigger the map change to optimise performance and efficiency under varying driving conditions.
[F16H 2061/023] Drive-off gear selection, i.e. optimising gear ratio for drive off of a vehicle -- This subclass covers systems or methods that determine the optimal gear ratio when a vehicle is started from rest, adjusting the transmission to provide the most efficient torque and acceleration. It includes algorithms, sensors and control logic used for drive-off gear selection.
[F16H 2061/0232] Selecting ratios for bringing engine into a particular state, e.g. for fast warming up or for reducing exhaust emissions -- The entry F16H 2061/0232 in the Cooperative Patent Classification (EPO/USPTO) pertains to methods for selecting gear ratios in transmission systems to achieve specific engine states, such as rapid warming or emission reduction.
[F16H 2061/0234] Adapting the ratios to special vehicle conditions -- The code covers devices that automatically adjust fluid flow or pressure ratios in vehicle hydraulic or pneumatic systems to match specific operating conditions such as load, speed or terrain, thereby optimizing performance and safety.
[F16H 2061/0237] Selecting ratios for providing engine braking -- The entry covers methods for determining and adjusting transmission or engine-control ratios to achieve desired engine-braking effects. It includes selecting gear, throttle, fuel-injection or valve-timing ratios that increase resistance when the driver releases the accelerator, thereby slowing the vehicle without using the wheel brakes.
[F16H 2061/0239] Selecting ratios for preventing or cancelling wheel slip -- The subclass covers technical solutions that select or vary transmission ratios to prevent or cancel wheel slip in vehicles or machinery, including devices, control methods and algorithms that detect slip and adjust gearing or torque to maintain traction.
[F16H 2061/0241] Adapting the ratio to special transmission conditions, e.g. shifts during warming up phase of transmission when fluid viscosity is high -- The CPC subclass F16H 2061/0241 covers mechanisms that automatically modify the transmission ratio to suit changing operating conditions, such as shifting during the warm-up phase when the transmission fluid is unusually viscous, ensuring smooth power delivery despite variable fluid viscosity.
[F16H 2061/0244] Adapting the automatic ratio to direct driver requests, e.g. manual shift signals or kick down -- The CPC subclass F16H 2061/0244 covers control mechanisms for automatic transmissions that modify the selected gear ratio in response to direct driver commands such as manual shift signals or a kick-down request. It addresses systems that detect these inputs and adjust the transmission's torque converter or gear set to achieve the desired performance or acceleration.
[F16H 2061/0253] Details of electro hydraulic valves, e.g. lands, ports, spools or springs -- F16H 2061/0253 addresses the specific design features of electro-hydraulic valves, including the geometry and arrangement of lands, ports, spools and springs. It concerns the internal components that control fluid flow and valve actuation in electro-controlled hydraulic systems.
[F16H 2061/0255] Solenoid valve using PWM or duty-cycle control -- The code F16H 2061/0255 pertains to hydraulic or pneumatic valve arrangements where a solenoid actuator is controlled by pulse-width-modulated or duty-cycle signals to vary the flow or pressure. It covers designs that modulate the valve opening time rather than using a continuous analogue signal, enabling precise flow regulation and energy saving in fluid power systems.
[F16H 2061/0258] Proportional solenoid valve -- F16H 2061/0258 classifies proportional solenoid valves, which use an electrically actuated solenoid to vary the valve opening continuously rather than simply on or off. The device provides a controllable flow rate proportional to the input current, enabling precise regulation in hydraulic or pneumatic circuits.
[F16H 2061/026] On-off solenoid valve -- F16H 2061/026 covers on-off solenoid valves, which are fluid-handling devices that use an electromechanical coil to move a plug or poppet and open or close a flow passage. The classification includes designs where the valve provides binary flow control-fully open or fully closed-often employed in hydraulic, pneumatic or water-distribution systems. It also addresses related sealing, mounting and actuation arrangements specific to solenoid-driven on-off operation.
[F16H 2061/0279] Details of hydraulic valves, e.g. lands, ports, spools or springs -- The CPC subclass F16H 2061/0279 pertains to the internal design of hydraulic valves, detailing features such as the geometry of lands, the configuration of ports, the shape and arrangement of spools, and the type and placement of springs that control fluid flow and pressure.
[F16H 2061/0281] Rotary shift valves, e.g. with a rotary moveable spool for supply of fluid to different channels -- F16H 2061/0281 covers rotary shift valves in which a cylindrical or disc-shaped spool rotates to connect an inlet to one of several outlets, allowing fluid to be directed to different channels. The design uses a rotating movable element to sequentially align passages for supply or exhaust, commonly applied in hydraulic and pneumatic control circuits.
[F16H 2061/0288] Relay valve, e.g. valve arranged between shift valve and servo -- This subclass covers relay valves placed between a shift valve and a servo valve in fluid-power control systems, where the valve transmits, modifies or amplifies control signals to regulate actuation. It includes designs that provide signal isolation or amplification for sequential or proportional hydraulic or pneumatic operation.
[F16H 2061/0407] by control of clutch in parallel torque path -- F16H 2061/0407 covers mechanisms that control a clutch when it operates in a parallel torque path, meaning the clutch disengages or modulates power transmission without interrupting the main drive line. The classification includes devices that use hydraulic, pneumatic, electronic or mechanical signals to vary clutch engagement while the primary torque continues to flow through an alternative route.
[F16H 2061/0411] by control of shaft brakes -- F16H 2061/0411 classifies devices that control the operation of shaft brakes, encompassing automatic, remote or electronic systems for applying, releasing or modulating a brake on a rotating shaft. It includes control circuits, sensors, command units and associated mechanisms that manage brake actuation in mechanical or hydraulic brake assemblies.
[F16H 2061/0414] by retarder control -- F16H 2061/0414 classifies valves whose flow is regulated by a retarder mechanism, using resistance or damping to control the speed or pressure of the fluid. The subclass covers designs that employ braking or retardation elements to achieve precise flow modulation in hydraulic or pneumatic systems.
[F16H 2061/0418] by using different synchronisation devices simultaneously, e.g. for faster synchronisation -- This CPC subgroup concerns methods that speed up synchronization of valve gear or related fluid-power devices by employing two or more synchronization mechanisms at the same time, such as combining mechanical and hydraulic synchronizers to reduce the time needed to bring moving parts into phase.
[F16H 2061/0422] by an electric machine, e.g. by accelerating or braking the input shaft -- F16H 2061/0422 covers hydraulic or pneumatic power-transmission systems in which an electric machine is used to control the input shaft, typically by accelerating or braking it to regulate fluid flow or pressure. This sub-class applies to arrangements where the electric drive directly influences the shaft speed or torque, enabling precise speed control, overload protection or energy-saving operation.
[F16H 2061/0425] Bridging torque interruption -- F16H 2061/0425 relates to fluid-power mechanisms that temporarily interrupt torque transmission while maintaining a fluid connection, such as hydraulic or hydro-mechanical couplings, clutches or brake systems that allow a bridging phase during torque disengagement. The classification covers designs and control arrangements that achieve this interruption without breaking the fluid circuit.
[F16H 2061/0429] by torque supply with a clutch in parallel torque path -- The CPC subclass F16H 2061/0429 relates to power-transmission mechanisms that deliver torque through a clutch arranged in a parallel torque path alongside the main drive. It covers devices where the clutch provides auxiliary or supplemental torque while the primary transmission continues to operate, such as gearboxes or motor-driven systems with parallel-path clutch coupling.
[F16H 2061/0433] by torque supply with an electric motor -- F16H covers fluid-power installations and hydraulic drive systems, and the sub-group 2061/0433 specifies arrangements where the required torque is supplied by an electric motor, such as hybrid hydraulic circuits that combine electric actuation with fluid power transmission.
[F16H 2061/044] when a freewheel device is disengaged or bridged -- F16H 2061/044 covers arrangements in which a freewheel device is intentionally disengaged or bridged, allowing torque-transmission or blocking across the freewheel. It includes mechanisms such as clutches, brakes or locking devices that connect the driven and driving members when the freewheel is not active.
[F16H 2061/0444] during fast shifting over two gearsteps, e.g. jumping from fourth to second gear -- This subclass relates to speed control mechanisms in power transmission devices that allow rapid shift across two gear positions, such as a direct jump from fourth to second gear, typically used in vehicles or machinery to improve performance.
[F16H 2061/0448] using a particular sequence of gear ratios or friction members -- F16H 2061/0448 covers mechanisms that achieve speed or torque conversion by employing a predetermined sequence of gear ratios or friction members, such as stepped gear trains, planetary gear sets or friction couplings arranged in a specific order to provide defined performance characteristics. This classification is used for transmissions, gearboxes and similar power-transmission devices that rely on a set progression of ratios rather than continuously variable adjustment.
[F16H 2061/0451] during swap-shifts, i.e. gear shifts between different planetary units, e.g. with double transitions shift involving three or more friction members -- The code F16H 2061/0451 covers gear systems that perform swap-shifts, meaning the transmission changes gear ratios by exchanging gear pairs between separate planetary gear units. It includes arrangements where a double transition shift engages three or more friction elements simultaneously to achieve the gear change. This subclass is applied to mechanisms that coordinate multiple planetary stages during a single shifting operation.
[F16H 2061/0455] during shifts involving three or more shift members, e.g. release of 3-4 clutch, 2-4 brake and apply of forward clutch C1 -- Describes a control method for gear shifts that involve three or more shift members, such as the simultaneous release of several clutches and brakes while applying a forward clutch.
[F16H 2061/0459] using map for shift parameters, e.g. shift time, slip or pressure gradient, for performing controlled shift transition and adapting shift parameters by learning -- The code F16H 2061/0459 pertains to devices or methods that employ a map of shift parameters-such as shift time, slip, or pressure gradient-to execute a controlled shift transition, and that adapt those parameters through learning techniques. It covers adaptive control strategies for hydraulic, pneumatic or other shifting systems that adjust operation based on sensed conditions.
[F16H 2061/0462] by controlling slip rate during gear shift transition -- This subclass of F16H relates to transmission devices where the slip rate of a gear set is actively managed during the shift between gears. It includes methods and control systems that adjust clutch engagement, hydraulic pressure, or electronic actuation to achieve a smooth transition and prevent excessive slip. The focus is on improving shift quality and reducing wear.
[F16H 2061/0466] Smoothing shift shock by apply or release of band brake servos, e.g. overlap control of band brake and a clutch or  vice versa -- The code covers devices that mitigate shift shock by coordinating the application and release of band-brake servos with a clutch, typically using overlap control so that the brake and clutch engage or disengage in a synchronized manner. This approach smooths power transmission during gear changes and can be applied in both band-brake-to-clutch and clutch-to-band-brake configurations.
[F16H 2061/047] by preventing or solving a tooth butt situation upon engagement failure due to misalignment of teeth -- The F16H 2061/047 subclass addresses mechanisms that prevent or remedy a tooth-butt condition caused by misalignment of gear teeth, which can lead to engagement failure. It includes designs such as adjustable tooth spacing, guiding devices, or corrective actions that ensure proper meshing and avoid gear damage.
[F16H 2061/0474] by smoothing engagement or release of positive clutches; Methods or means for shock free engagement of dog clutches -- This CPC subgroup covers designs and techniques that smooth the engagement or release of positive, or "dog," clutches to avoid shock, including damping elements, controlled speed differentials and other means that make clutch operation gradual and vibration-free. It relates to methods or mechanisms that provide shock-free engagement of dog clutches in power transmission systems.
[F16H 2061/0477] by suppression of excessive engine flare or turbine racing during shift transition -- The code F16H 2061/0477 pertains to techniques for controlling or preventing excessive engine flare or turbine racing when a vehicle changes gears. It covers methods and devices that modulate power output or turbine speed during shift transitions to ensure smooth operation and avoid overspeed conditions. This falls within the broader class for internal combustion engines and their control systems.
[F16H 2061/0481] during range shift from drive (D) or reverse (R) to neutral (N) -- The CPC subclass F16H 2061/0481 covers transmission or gear-changing devices that include control actions performed while the range selector moves from drive (D) or reverse (R) to neutral (N). It pertains to the mechanisms, sensors or electronic logic that manage the shift transition to ensure smooth disengagement of power. This subclass is used for automotive or similar driveline systems where the range change is part of the operating cycle.
[F16H 2061/0485] during range shift from neutral (N) to reverse (R) -- F16H 2061/0485 relates to variable-displacement pumps and specifies features that manage the pump's operating range while it moves from a neutral (no-flow) position to a reverse-flow condition. It covers control or design elements that facilitate this range-shift transition in hydraulic or pneumatic pumping systems.
[F16H 2061/0488] during range shift from neutral (N) to drive (D) -- The CPC subgroup F16H 2061/0488 covers devices or control systems that manage the shift of a transmission range from neutral to drive. It includes mechanisms that monitor or adjust the range-shift process to ensure smooth engagement. The classification is used for patents involving electronic or hydraulic control of gear range selection during this transition.
[F16H 2061/0492] for high engine torque, e.g. during acceleration or uphill driving -- The code F16H 2061/0492 covers mechanisms or control methods that manage very high engine torque, such as that required during rapid acceleration or when driving uphill. It includes features like torque-converter regulation, clutch-control strategies and torque-limiting devices that ensure power is delivered smoothly and safely under peak-load conditions.
[F16H 2061/0496] for low engine torque, e.g. during coasting, sailing or engine braking -- F16H 2061/0496 covers mechanisms that adjust a propeller's pitch to operate efficiently when the engine delivers low torque, such as during coasting, sailing or engine-braking, enabling smooth speed control and reduced fuel consumption.
[F16H 2061/062] for controlling filling of clutches or brake servos, e.g. fill time, fill level or pressure during filling -- F16H 2061/062 covers devices that regulate the filling of hydraulic or pneumatic clutches and brake servos, including systems that monitor and adjust fill time, fill level or pressure to ensure proper operation.
[F16H 2061/064] for calibration of pressure levels for friction members, e.g. by monitoring the speed change of transmission shafts -- F16H 2061/064 pertains to methods or devices that calibrate pressure levels in friction components, such as brakes or clutches, by detecting how the speed of a transmission shaft varies in response to pressure changes, enabling precise adjustment and monitoring of the friction member's performance.
[F16H 2061/085] Timing of auxiliary gear shifts -- F16H 2061/085 covers devices that control the timing of auxiliary gear shifts in transmission systems, including mechanisms that coordinate shift events with engine or load conditions to improve efficiency and performance. It includes electronic, hydraulic or mechanical control arrangements that schedule secondary gear changes relative to primary operation.
[F16H 2061/1204] for malfunction caused by simultaneous engagement of different ratios resulting in transmission lock state or tie-up condition -- This subclass concerns gear transmission units in which a fault occurs when two or more gear ratios are engaged at the same time, causing the mechanism to lock or tie up. It covers detection, prevention, or mitigation of such simultaneous-engagement malfunctions in hydraulic, mechanical, or planetary gear systems.
[F16H 2061/1208] with diagnostic check cycles; Monitoring of failures -- This subclass covers hydraulic or pneumatic systems that incorporate periodic diagnostic check cycles and continuously monitor for component failures, providing fault detection, condition monitoring and maintenance-triggering functions within the pump, compressor or actuator arrangement.
[F16H 2061/1212] Plausibility checks; Counting means for repeated failures -- The entry covers devices that monitor a hydraulic or pneumatic system to verify that operating conditions remain within expected limits, performing plausibility checks on sensor data or command signals, and includes counting mechanisms that record repeated failure events in order to trigger alarms, safety actions or system shutdown after a predetermined number of occurrences.
[F16H 2061/1216] Display or indication of detected failures -- The code F16H 2061/1216 covers methods or devices that inform the operator when a hydraulic, pneumatic or fluid power system has detected a fault, such as pressure loss, leakage or component malfunction. It includes visual, audible or tactile alerts that indicate the type or location of the failure.
[F16H 2061/122] Avoiding failures by using redundant parts -- This group classifies pump or compressor arrangements that employ duplicate or parallel components so that a malfunction in one element does not halt operation, covering features such as dual motors, parallel flow paths, or redundant sealing parts to improve reliability.
[F16H 2061/1224] Adapting to failures or work around with other constraints, e.g. circumvention by avoiding use of failed parts -- This subclass covers devices or methods that detect a component failure and automatically adjust operation or bypass the faulty part by using alternative paths, constraints or redundant elements, thereby maintaining functionality without relying on the failed component.
[F16H 2061/1228] Fixing failures by repairing failed parts, e.g. loosening a sticking valve -- The CPC subclass F16H 2061/1228 covers techniques for restoring the operation of hydraulic or pneumatic components by addressing failures, specifically methods that involve loosening or otherwise repairing a stuck valve to re-establish proper flow or pressure. This includes procedures to detect the malfunction and apply mechanical or fluid-based actions that free the valve without replacing the entire assembly.
[F16H 2061/1232] Bringing the control into a predefined state, e.g. giving priority to particular actuators or gear ratios -- The code F16H 2061/1232 identifies mechanisms that set a control system to a predefined condition, such as assigning priority to selected actuators or specific gear ratios, ensuring a chosen operating state is achieved before normal operation resumes.
[F16H 2061/1236] using fail priority valves -- F16H 2061/1236 covers fail-priority valves, which automatically switch to a predetermined position when the primary valve cannot operate, providing safe pressure relief or maintenance in hydraulic or pneumatic circuits. These devices prevent over-pressure or loss of control by activating a secondary valve in case of failure.
[F16H 2061/124] Limiting the input power, torque or speed -- The CPC subgroup F16H 2061/124 covers arrangements and devices that restrict the input power, torque or rotational speed of a machine or drive system, such as governors, torque limiters, speed-sensing clutches or electronic controls that prevent overload or excessive operation. It includes both mechanical and electromechanical means used to protect engines, motors, pumps or other power-transmitting components.
[F16H 2061/1244] Keeping the current state -- F16H 2061/1244 relates to pumps that include means to keep the present operating condition, for example latching or hold-in devices that maintain speed, flow or power state when the control signal is removed. The classification covers mechanisms that preserve the current state of the pump without continuous external control.
[F16H 2061/1248] Resuming normal operation -- F16H 2061/1248 covers pump systems that incorporate features for automatically restoring normal operation after an interruption, including restart procedures, control logic, or safety mechanisms that bring the pump back to its intended operating condition.
[F16H 2061/1252] Fail safe valves -- The CPC subclass F16H 2061/1252 covers fail-safe valves, which are designed to automatically assume a safe position-typically closed or open-when a fault, loss of power, or abnormal condition occurs in a fluid-power system. These valves ensure continued safety by preventing uncontrolled flow or pressure buildup in hydraulic, pneumatic, or other fluid circuits.
[F16H 2061/1256] characterised by the parts or units where malfunctioning was assumed or detected -- The Cooperative Patent Classification code F16H 2061/1256 pertains to systems or methods for detecting or diagnosing malfunctions in specific components or units of mechanical power transmission devices, such as gears or couplings.
[F16H 2061/126] the failing part is the controller -- F16H 2061/126 pertains to pump systems whose critical element is the controller, focusing on designs that detect controller malfunction and trigger protective actions such as shutdown, fallback operation or alarm. It covers methods and devices that monitor controller health and ensure safe pump performance when the controller fails.
[F16H 2061/1264] Hydraulic parts of the controller, e.g. a sticking valve or clogged channel -- F16H 2061/1264 covers hydraulic components of control systems that influence fluid flow, such as sticking valves or clogged channels, and addresses their design, operation, failure modes and remedial measures.
[F16H 2061/1268] Electric parts of the controller, e.g. a defect solenoid, wiring or microprocessor -- The CPC subclass F16H 2061/1268 pertains to the electric components of fluid-power controllers, covering items such as defect-detecting solenoids, the wiring that connects them, and microprocessors or other electronic circuitry that monitor, regulate or drive the controller's operation. It groups together the electrical parts that are integral to the controller rather than the hydraulic or pneumatic mechanisms themselves.
[F16H 2061/1272] the failing part is a part of the final output mechanism, e.g. shift rods or forks -- F16H 2061/1272 covers hydraulic or pneumatic cylinder arrangements where the component that fails is the final output device, such as shift rods, forks or other elements that directly transmit motion to the load.
[F16H 2061/1276] the failing part is a friction device, e.g. clutches or brakes -- F16H 2061/1276 classifies hydraulic or fluid-power systems where the component that fails is a friction device such as a clutch or brake. It covers designs, diagnostics or protective measures relating to these failing friction elements within the overall mechanism.
[F16H 2061/128] the main clutch -- The CPC code F16H 2061/128 designates the main clutch of a mechanical power-transmission system, such as the primary clutch in a vehicle gearbox that engages and transmits engine torque to the drivetrain. It covers the design, operation and integration of this principal clutch component.
[F16H 2061/1284] the failing part is a sensor -- F16H 2061/1284 covers pump systems in which a sensor is employed to detect a malfunctioning component, providing condition monitoring, fault indication or automatic shutdown. The classification is used for designs where the failing part itself is a sensor that monitors variables such as pressure, flow, vibration or temperature to signal pump degradation or failure.
[F16H 2061/1288] the failing part is an actuator -- F16H 2061/1288 covers hydraulic or pneumatic systems in which the actuator is identified as the component that fails, including the detection, diagnosis and mitigation of actuator failures and the design features intended to prevent or manage such failures.
[F16H 2061/1292] the failing part is the power supply, e.g. the electric power supply -- F16H 2061/1292 covers hydraulic or fluid-power systems in which the malfunction is attributed to the electric power supply that drives the unit. It includes arrangements for detecting, protecting against or compensating for power-supply failures in pumps, motors or related components.
[F16H 2061/1296] the failing part is an electric machine forming part of the transmission -- The Cooperative Patent Classification code F16H 2061/1296, used by the European and United States patent offices, pertains to systems where a malfunctioning component in the powertrain is an electric machine integral to the transmission. This classification covers innovations related to diagnostic or protective measures for such integrated electric machines.
[F16H 2061/145] for controlling slip, e.g. approaching target slip value -- F16H 2061/145 covers hydraulic drive systems that include mechanisms or control strategies to manage the slip between moving and stationary parts, such as pump or motor rotors, by adjusting operating parameters to approach a predetermined slip value, thereby improving efficiency and performance.
[F16H 2061/146] for smoothing gear shift shock -- This subclass pertains to devices or control strategies that lessen the abrupt torque variations experienced during a gear change. It includes hydraulic, pneumatic, or electronic mechanisms that dampen the shock of shifting to provide a smoother transition between gears.
[F16H 2061/147] during engine braking, e.g. to attenuate gear clunk when torque direction is changed -- F16H 2061/147 falls under the CPC class for engine braking devices for internal combustion engines, specifically addressing mechanisms that reduce gear clunk when the torque direction changes. The entry covers systems that attenuate noise or shock during engine braking by controlling torque transmission or gear engagement.
[F16H 2061/161] by checking feasibility of shifts, i.e. determine if requested shift can be successfully completed and post shift values are in an acceptable range -- The code covers methods that evaluate whether a commanded positional shift can be carried out and whether the resulting post-shift values remain within preset acceptable limits. It is used in control systems to verify shift feasibility before execution, ensuring safe and reliable adjustment of machine or robotic components.
[F16H 2061/163] Holding the gear for delaying gear shifts under unfavorable conditions, e.g. during cornering -- F16H 2061/163 covers mechanisms that retain a selected transmission gear to prevent or postpone gear changes when operating conditions are unfavorable, such as during vehicle cornering. It relates to gear-hold or gear-lock devices that maintain torque transmission and improve stability by delaying shifts.
[F16H 2061/165] Preventing reverse gear shifts if vehicle speed is too high for safe shifting -- This subclass covers mechanisms that block activation of reverse gear when vehicle speed exceeds a safe limit, using electronic or mechanical interlocks to prevent gear engagement and improve safety by avoiding drivetrain damage.
[F16H 2061/166] Preventing or initiating shifts for preventing stall or overspeed of engine -- The F16H 2061/166 subclass covers devices or control methods that automatically adjust gear shifts, fuel delivery, or other engine parameters to prevent stall or overspeed conditions. It includes systems that detect abnormal speed or load and intervene to initiate or inhibit shifts, ensuring safe and stable engine operation.
[F16H 2061/168] Forced shifts into neutral for safety reasons, e.g. in case of transmission failure or emergency braking -- The code F16H 2061/168 covers mechanisms that automatically shift a vehicle's transmission into neutral for safety, such as when a transmission fault occurs or during emergency braking, to prevent damage and improve control.
[F16H 2061/185] Means, e.g. catches or interlocks, for preventing unintended shift into reverse gear -- The CPC code F16H 2061/185 covers devices built into gearboxes or transmission systems that automatically catch, lock or otherwise interlock the shifting mechanism to stop accidental movement into reverse gear. These safety features are intended for vehicles, machinery or equipment where unintended reverse engagement could cause damage or injury.
[F16H 2061/202] Active creep control for slow driving, e.g. by controlling clutch slip -- The CPC subclass F16H 2061/202 covers systems that actively regulate a vehicle's low-speed creep motion by adjusting the slip of a clutch. It includes control strategies that modulate clutch engagement to maintain a steady, slow drive without driver input, often used in automated or assistive transmission designs.
[F16H 2061/205] Hill hold control, e.g. with torque converter or a friction device slightly engaged to keep vehicle stationary -- F16H 2061/205 relates to vehicle hill-hold control systems that keep a vehicle stationary on an incline by applying a small amount of torque or friction, typically through a torque converter or a partially engaged friction device, to prevent rollback when the driver releases the brake. This class covers the mechanical or hydraulic means of providing that slight engagement for automatic hill-hold assistance.
[F16H 2061/207] by neutral control -- The subclass covers fluid-powered actuators, pumps, compressors or similar devices that are operated by neutral control, i.e., the mechanism returns to a neutral or centered position when no active pressure is applied, often using a three-position valve or spring-return arrangement. It applies to systems where motion is achieved by directing fluid to one side for actuation and allowing the device to revert to neutral when the supply is removed.
[F16H 2061/213] for emergency braking, e.g. for increasing brake power in emergency situations -- The CPC subclass F16H 2061/213 relates to systems that boost braking performance during emergency conditions. It includes mechanisms that automatically increase brake force or apply additional braking assistance when a rapid stop is required, such as emergency brake assist, brake-by-wire overload, or supplemental actuators.
[F16H 2061/216] by using exhaust brakes -- The CPC subclass F16H 2061/216 covers systems that achieve vehicle or engine slowing by generating back-pressure in the exhaust line, typically through an exhaust-brake valve or similar device that restricts exhaust flow to reduce engine output and provide a braking effect.
[F16H 2061/223] Electrical gear shift lock, e.g. locking of lever in park or neutral position by electric means if brake is not applied; Key interlock, i.e. locking the key if lever is not in park position -- The code covers systems that use electrical means to lock a vehicle's gear-shift lever in the park or neutral position when the service brake is not applied, and mechanisms that interlock the ignition key so it cannot be removed unless the lever is in park.
[F16H 2061/226] Manual distress release of the locking means for shift levers, e.g. to allow towing of vehicle in case of breakdown -- This subclass covers mechanisms that provide a manual emergency release for the locking device of a vehicle's shift lever, allowing the gear selector to be disengaged and the vehicle to be towed when the normal lock fails or the vehicle breaks down.
[F16H 2061/241] Actuators providing feel or simulating a shift gate, i.e. with active force generation for providing counter forces for feed back -- F16H 2061/241 covers devices that use active actuators to create a tactile sensation in a gear-shift lever or gate, simulating the resistance of mechanical gears and providing feedback to the driver. The technology generates counter-forces to mimic shifting feel in automatic or electronic transmissions.
[F16H 2061/242] Mechanical shift gates or similar guiding means during selection and shifting -- F16H 2061/242 covers mechanical devices that guide or control workpieces during the selection and shifting process, such as shift gates, deflectors, and similar guiding means. These mechanisms are used in material-handling, feeding or sorting equipment to route, isolate or align items as they are transferred from one position to another.
[F16H 2061/243] Cams or detent arrays for guiding and providing feel -- The CPC subclass F16H 2061/243 covers cams or detent arrays that are employed to guide the motion of a component while providing a tactile or positional "feel" to the user. It includes designs where cam profiles or spring-loaded detents create discrete steps or resistance in mechanisms such as switches, knobs, and adjustable interfaces.
[F16H 2061/245] Ramp contours for generating force threshold, e.g. cams or pushers for generating additional resistance for a reverse path -- This subclass covers mechanical arrangements that use ramp-shaped contours such as cams or pushers to create a defined force threshold that must be overcome to move backward, providing added resistance on the reverse path. It includes designs where the contour geometry generates the required force level for reverse motion prevention.
[F16H 2061/246] Additional mass or weight on shift linkage for improving feel -- A device that adds mass or weight to a shift-linkage assembly to enhance the operator's tactile perception of gear changes, typically by attaching a calibrated weight or inertial element to the linkage or its components. It falls within CPC group F16H 2061/246, which covers auxiliary means for improving the feel of shifting mechanisms.
[F16H 2061/247] Detents for range selectors -- The CPC subclass F16H 2061/247 covers detents that provide discrete stopping positions for range-selection mechanisms in fluid-control devices. It includes mechanical features such as pins, springs or blocks that lock a selector at a chosen setting, ensuring the valve or actuator remains in the desired operating range. This subclass is used for designs where precise, repeatable positioning of a control element is required.
[F16H 2061/248] with audible signals for providing selection or shift feed back -- The classification covers mechanisms that control the supply or feed of material and incorporate audible signals to indicate a selection has been made or to provide feedback on a shift operation. It applies to devices where sound cues are used to confirm positioning, speed changes or feed adjustments in mechanical feeding systems.
[F16H 2061/2823] Controlling actuator force way characteristic, i.e. controlling force or movement depending on the actuator position, e.g. for adapting force to synchronisation and engagement of gear clutch -- This subclass covers mechanisms that adjust the force or movement of an actuator according to its position, allowing the force to be varied to match synchronization and engagement of a gear clutch. It includes control systems that modulate force output as the actuator travels, enabling precise coupling of gears.
[F16H 2061/283] Adjustment or calibration of actuator positions, e.g. neutral position -- F16H 2061/283 relates to methods and devices for adjusting or calibrating the positions of hydraulic or pneumatic actuators, such as setting a neutral or reference position. It includes mechanisms that sense, control or compensate actuator movement to achieve accurate positioning, commonly applied in industrial or vehicle control systems.
[F16H 2061/2838] Arrangements with single drive motor for selecting and shifting movements, i.e. one motor used for generating both movements -- This subclass covers fluid-power or electromechanical drive arrangements in which a single motor provides both the selecting motion that chooses a gear, valve or piston position and the shifting motion that moves the selected element, eliminating the need for separate actuators.
[F16H 2061/2846] Arrangements of actuators for enabling jump shifting for skipping of gear ratios -- The code covers transmission designs that use one or more actuators arranged to press or release clutch elements so the gearbox can move directly between non-adjacent gears, skipping intermediate ratios. Such jump-shifting systems are employed in automotive and industrial gearboxes to achieve faster gear changes or improve efficiency.
[F16H 2061/2853] Electromagnetic solenoids -- F16H 2061/2853 covers electromagnetic solenoids, devices that transform electrical energy into linear motion using a magnetic field generated by a coil. The classification includes the design and construction of the magnetic circuit, the movable armature, power-feeding arrangements and methods of controlling the actuation, as well as associated protection and mounting features.
[F16H 2061/2861] Linear motors -- F16H 2061/2861 relates to linear motors, which are electric machines that produce direct linear motion without rotating components. The subclass covers designs, arrangements and control methods for linear synchronous and induction motors used in positioning, transport and actuation systems. It includes details on stator and mover configurations, magnetic guideways and power-supply schemes.
[F16H 2061/2869] Cam or crank gearing -- The CPC entry F16H 2061/2869 covers gear arrangements that incorporate a cam or crank mechanism, i.e., gear trains in which a cam or crank is used to transmit motion or to convert rotational movement into reciprocating or oscillating motion. It includes designs where gears drive a cam profile or a crank shaft for timing, valve actuation or other motion-control functions in machinery.
[F16H 2061/2876] Racks -- F16H 2061/2876 covers racks used in conveyor or handling systems to support, space, or hold articles while they are conveyed or processed. The racks may be fixed or adjustable, and are employed in manufacturing, warehousing, and logistics equipment to organize items and facilitate automated movement.
[F16H 2061/2884] Screw-nut devices -- F16H 2061/2884 covers screw-nut mechanisms used to secure or adjust components, especially bearings or mounting parts, by converting rotational motion of a threaded shaft into linear displacement of a nut. The classification includes threaded shafts, nuts, and associated features that provide precise positioning, locking or load-bearing functions.
[F16H 2061/2892] other gears, e.g. worm gears, for transmitting rotary motion to the output mechanism -- The CPC subclass F16H 2061/2892 pertains to gear arrangements, such as worm gears and similar non-standard gears, that transmit rotary motion from a drive to an output mechanism. It covers designs where the gear geometry provides specific motion conversion or speed reduction, commonly used in gearboxes, pumps and other mechanical power-transmission systems.
[F16H 2061/301] for power assistance, i.e. servos with follow up action -- F16H 2061/301 covers hydraulic or pneumatic cylinders that incorporate power-assistance mechanisms, such as servos that provide a follow-up action to adjust force or speed in response to load variations. These devices enhance precision and efficiency in actuator control.
[F16H 2061/302] with variable force amplification, e.g. force is depending on selected gear or on actuator force (non-linear amplification) -- F16H 2061/302 covers mechanisms that provide variable force amplification, where the transmitted force changes non-linearly according to the selected gear ratio or the magnitude of the actuator force. It includes gear-driven or hydraulic amplifiers that adjust output force in response to control inputs rather than maintaining a fixed ratio.
[F16H 2061/304] using telemotors, i.e. systems with master cylinder and linked shift actuator without external pressure source -- F16H 2061/304 covers hydraulic transmission arrangements that use telemotors, meaning a master cylinder directly linked to a shift actuator and operating without any external pressure source. The system transmits force through the fluid to move the actuator.
[F16H 2061/305] Accumulators for fluid supply to the servo motors, or control thereof -- The code F16H 2061/305 covers systems that use accumulators to store hydraulic or pneumatic fluid and supply it to servo-driven motors, as well as arrangements for controlling the pressure, flow or timing of that fluid to optimise motor performance.
[F16H 2061/307] Actuators with three or more defined positions, e.g. three position servos -- F16H 2061/307 designates actuators that provide three or more distinct, predefined positions, such as three-position servos used in hydraulic, pneumatic or electric systems. The class covers mechanisms that can be indexed or switched between these fixed positions, typically by electronic or mechanical control.
[F16H 2061/308] Modular hydraulic shift units, i.e. preassembled actuator units for select and shift movements adapted for being mounted on transmission casing -- The F16H 2061/308 subclass covers modular hydraulic shift units that are pre-assembled actuator assemblies used for selecting and shifting gears, designed to be mounted directly onto a transmission casing. It includes compact, interchangeable hydraulic modules that integrate the pump, valve and actuator functions for gear-changing operations.
[F16H 2061/323] for power assistance, i.e. servos with follow up action -- The subclass F16H 2061/323 covers fluid-power-assisted mechanisms that incorporate servos to provide follow-up action, meaning an auxiliary power source augments the primary input to produce the required motion or force. It includes devices in which a control signal activates a hydraulic, pneumatic or hybrid servo that supplies additional assistance, improving responsiveness or load-carrying capability.
[F16H 2061/326] Actuators for range selection, i.e. actuators for controlling the range selector or the manual range valve in the transmission -- F16H 2061/326 covers actuators used to select or change the gear range in a transmission, including devices that operate the range selector or manual range valve. The code applies to hydraulic, pneumatic or electromechanical mechanisms that move the selector elements to engage different gear sets. It is part of the broader class for fluid-power transmission components that control gear shifting.
[F16H 2061/6601] with arrangements for dividing torque and shifting between different ranges -- This CPC subclass describes mechanisms that divide engine torque and transfer it among multiple output ranges, such as gearboxes, differentials and other torque-splitting arrangements used to shift power between speed or gear ranges in vehicles and machinery.
[F16H 2061/6602] with at least two dynamo-electric machines for creating an electric power path inside the transmission device, e.g. using generator and motor for a variable power torque path -- This CPC subclass covers transmission arrangements that incorporate at least two dynamo-electric machines, such as a generator and a motor, to create an internal electrical power circuit. The machines exchange energy within the device, allowing the torque delivered to be varied by controlling the electrical conversion.
[F16H 2061/6603] characterised by changing ratio in the mechanical gearing -- F16H 2061/6603 relates to gear assemblies whose transmission ratio is not fixed but varies during operation. It includes mechanisms such as continuously variable transmissions, variable-ratio gear sets and adjustable gearing that changes the speed or torque relationship while the device is running.
[F16H 2061/6604] Special control features generally applicable to continuously variable gearings -- This CPC subclass covers control mechanisms used in continuously variable transmissions, such as hydraulic, electronic or mechanical systems that adjust the transmission ratio continuously, focusing on features applicable across different CVT designs.
[F16H 2061/6605] Control for completing downshift at hard braking -- F16H 2061/6605 deals with control systems that automatically complete a downshift during hard braking, synchronising torque transmission and engine speed to maintain traction and avoid stalling. The device monitors brake pressure or deceleration and initiates the gear change at the appropriate moment.
[F16H 2061/6607] Controls concerning lubrication or cooling -- This subclass pertains to pumps and pumping systems where the invention provides monitoring or regulation of lubrication or cooling functions, such as sensors, controllers, and feedback loops that automatically adjust oil flow, coolant supply, or temperature to maintain optimal performance and prevent wear.
[F16H 2061/6608] Control of clutches, or brakes for forward-reverse shift -- The CPC subclass F16H 2061/6608 covers devices and methods for controlling clutches or brakes that enable forward-reverse shifting in mechanical transmissions. It includes control mechanisms-hydraulic, pneumatic, electromechanical or electronic-that engage or disengage the clutch or brake to change the direction of rotation. This subclass is used for inventions that automate or improve the switching between forward and reverse drive.
[F16H 2061/6609] Control of clutches or brakes in torque split transmissions -- The code F16H 2061/6609 falls under the Cooperative Patent Classification for mechanical power transmission devices and relates to control systems for clutches or brakes used in torque-split transmissions, where power is divided between two output shafts. It covers methods and apparatus that manage clutch or brake engagement to regulate torque distribution.
[F16H 2061/661] Conjoint control of CVT and drive clutch -- F16H 2061/661 relates to transmission systems where a continuously variable transmission and a drive clutch are managed by a shared control strategy, covering methods for synchronising clutch engagement with CVT ratio changes to optimise torque transfer, improve efficiency and ensure smooth vehicle acceleration.
[F16H 2061/6611] Control to achieve a particular driver perception, e.g. for generating a shift shock sensation -- F16H 2061/6611 covers control systems that deliberately create a particular driver perception, such as producing a shift-shock sensation during a gear change. The classification addresses methods and devices that manipulate vehicle dynamics or feedback to generate a defined tactile or audible cue for the driver, enhancing the perceived experience of shifting.
[F16H 2061/6612] for engine braking -- CPC F16H 2061/6612 covers devices and methods that provide engine-braking functions, such as systems that use engine resistance to reduce vehicle speed without applying the friction brakes. It includes mechanical, hydraulic or electronic arrangements that generate retarding torque by modifying engine operation.
[F16H 2061/6614] Control of ratio during dual or multiple pass shifting for enlarged ratio coverage -- This classification pertains to transmission systems that employ dual-pass or multiple-pass shifting techniques to extend the available gear-ratio range. It covers methods and control mechanisms that manage ratio selection during successive shift passes, ensuring smooth transition and expanded coverage in automatic or semi-automatic gearboxes.
[F16H 2061/6615] Imitating a stepped transmissions -- The CPC subclass F16H 2061/6615 covers mechanisms that reproduce the operation of a stepped transmission without using actual gear sets, such as hydraulic, electronic or mechanical systems that emulate discrete gear ratios to provide step-like torque and speed changes. It includes designs that mimic the feel and performance of traditional gearboxes while using alternative transmission principles.
[F16H 2061/6616] the shifting of the transmission being manually controlled -- This group covers mechanisms that allow an operator to manually change the gear ratio or speed setting of a transmission, including levers, cams, gears or other shifting elements that are directly controlled to engage different output ranges. It applies to hydraulic or mechanical power-train assemblies where the transmission shift is performed by manual actuation.
[F16H 2061/6617] Manual control of CVTs while continuously varying the ratio -- The subclass covers devices or methods that let a driver manually adjust a continuously variable transmission while the gear ratio is being varied without discrete steps. It includes control levers, electronic interfaces or mechanical linkages that provide real time ratio selection during operation.
[F16H 2061/6618] Protecting CVTs against overload by limiting clutch capacity, e.g. torque fuse -- The classification F16H 2061/6618 relates to protective measures for continuously variable transmissions that prevent overload damage by restricting the clutch's torque-transmitting capability. It covers devices such as torque fuses, slip-limiting clutches or other limiters that disengage or reduce torque when a preset load threshold is exceeded, thereby safeguarding the CVT components.
[F16H 2061/66204] Control for modifying the ratio control characteristic -- This CPC subclass covers hydraulic or fluid-power control devices that adjust an output ratio such as flow, pressure or speed, typically by proportioning valves, variable-displacement pumps or similar mechanisms. The control element is designed to modify the ratio characteristic in response to operational demands or feedback signals.
[F16H 2061/66209] dependent on ambient conditions -- F16H 2061/66209 classifies pumps whose operation or control is ambient-condition-sensitive, such as changes in temperature, pressure, humidity or weather. It includes designs, sensors or methods that adjust performance based on the surrounding environment.
[F16H 2061/66213] dependent on driver's choice -- Class F16H 2061/66213 covers vehicle-mounted load-carrying or handling devices whose mode of operation-such as activation, positioning or power distribution-is selected by the driver at the time of use. The claim is limited to systems where the driver's input directly determines the functional state of the equipment, rather than automatic or pre-programmed control.
[F16H 2061/66218] dependent on control input parameters other than ambient conditions or driver's choice -- This subclass groups fluid-control elements, such as valves or regulators, that adjust their operation based on measured input signals like pressure, flow, level or other sensor data, rather than on ambient conditions or a manual choice by the driver. It applies to systems where the control logic depends on these internal parameters to modulate fluid flow.
[F16H 2061/66222] the ratio is varied in order to reduce surface wear of belt or pulley -- This CPC subgroup covers belt or pulley drive arrangements where the transmission ratio is intentionally varied during operation to lessen surface wear, for example by adjusting pulley diameters, belt tension or other ratio-changing mechanisms. It applies to designs that use a variable ratio as a wear-reduction strategy in belt-driven power transmission.
[F16H 2061/66277] by optimising the clamping force exerted on the endless flexible member -- The CPC entry F16H 2061/66277 covers technical solutions that adjust or control the clamping force applied to an endless flexible member-such as a belt, chain or rope-in a drive system, aiming to optimise grip, minimise slip and accommodate wear or tension changes. It includes mechanisms, sensors or control methods that dynamically vary the clamping pressure to maintain efficient power transmission.
[F16H 2061/66281] by increasing the line pressure at the occurrence of input torque peak -- Covers hydraulic transmission devices that automatically raise the line pressure when a peak in input torque is detected, using torque-responsive sensors or control mechanisms to prevent overload and maintain performance, typically applied in variable-load machinery.
[F16H 2061/66286] Control for optimising pump efficiency -- The CPC subclass F16H 2061/66286 covers control mechanisms and strategies designed to maximise the efficiency of hydraulic or mechanical pumps. It includes variable-speed drives, feedback loops, sensor-based monitoring and adaptive algorithms that adjust flow, pressure or motor speed to reduce energy consumption while maintaining required performance.
[F16H 2061/6629] Detection of slip for determining level of wear -- F16H 2061/6629 covers methods and apparatus that sense slip between moving parts to assess wear, typically by monitoring changes in friction, vibration or displacement. The technology is used in mechanical systems such as pumps, gearboxes or bearings to provide early warning of degradation and schedule maintenance.
[F16H 2061/66295] characterised by means for controlling the geometrical interrelationship of pulleys and the endless flexible member, e.g. belt alignment or position of the resulting axial pulley force in the plane perpendicular to the pulley axis -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Geometrical relationships.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates a belt-type CVT comprising a primary pulley (1) and a secondary pulley (6). The pulleys (1) and (6) are intentionally misaligned in the range of transmission ratios that are most frequently used during operation of the transmission and/or wherein the belt is maximally loaded.
