[F16K 17/006] specially adapted for shelters -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Adapted for bomb-shelters.
[F16K 17/02] opening on surplus pressure on one side -- A safety valve that is designed to open when the pressure on one side exceeds a set limit, releasing excess fluid to prevent damage. This type of valve automatically relieves surplus pressure, protecting equipment and systems from over-pressurization.
[F16K 17/025] and remaining open after return of the normal pressure -- This CPC subclass covers safety valves that stay open after the pressure that triggered them returns to normal, allowing continued venting or preventing re-pressurization. It applies to devices designed to remain in the open position after the relieving event, typically used in pressure-sensitive systems where residual flow is required.
[F16K 17/04] spring-loaded -- F16K 17/04 refers to spring-loaded safety valves, devices that automatically open to relieve excess pressure using a spring mechanism. They are classified under Mechanical Engineering → Engineering Elements and Units → Valves, specifically safety valves.
[F16K 17/0406] in the form of balls -- Ball-type safety valves are pressure-relief devices that employ a spherical ball as the sealing element, opening automatically when pressure exceeds a set limit to protect equipment and pipelines. The ball rotates or lifts from its seat, allowing fluid to vent quickly, and reseats when pressure falls below the set point, providing reliable and rapid shut-off in mechanical systems.
[F16K 17/0413] in the form of closure plates -- This subclass of the Cooperative Patent Classification covers safety valves that use closure plates as the sealing element. The plates are arranged to open or close in response to pressure variations, providing rapid relief to prevent over-pressure damage.
[F16K 17/042] with locking or disconnecting arrangements -- F16K 17/042 classifies safety valves that incorporate locking or disconnecting arrangements, meaning the valve can be secured in a closed or open position to prevent accidental actuation. These mechanisms are used to enhance safety and maintenance control in pressure-relief systems.
[F16K 17/0426] with seat protecting means -- A safety valve equipped with mechanisms that shield the sealing seat from wear, impact or corrosion, ensuring reliable operation and extending service life. This sub-category falls under mechanical engineering → engineering elements and units → valves → safety valves, and is identified by the CPC code F16K 17/0426.
[F16K 17/0433] with vibration preventing means -- F16K 17/0433 pertains to safety valves that incorporate vibration-preventing means. The subclass covers designs where dampers, absorbers or other devices are integrated to reduce oscillation of the valve body or internal components during operation, enhancing reliability and reducing wear.
[F16K 17/044] with more than one spring -- Safety valves that incorporate more than one spring use multiple spring elements to balance or increase the force needed to open the valve, improving reliability and providing redundancy against spring failure. This design is applied in mechanical engineering where precise pressure relief and fail-safe operation are critical.
[F16K 17/0446] with an obturating member having at least a component of their opening and closing motion not perpendicular to the closing faces -- The CPC subclass F16K 17/0446 covers safety valves whose closing element (obturating member) moves in a direction that is not perpendicular to the sealing faces, meaning the opening or closing motion includes an angular component relative to the valve seat. This design can improve sealing reliability or flow control in mechanical engineering applications.
[F16K 17/0453] the member being a diaphragm -- F16K 17/0453 covers safety valves whose active element is a flexible diaphragm that separates the pressure chamber from the outlet and lifts to vent excess pressure. The diaphragm acts as the moving member that responds to over-pressure, providing rapid and reliable relief in pipelines, vessels or equipment.
[F16K 17/046] the valve being of the gate valve type or the sliding valve type -- This subclass covers safety valves that use a gate-type or sliding-type closure, where a solid disc or plate moves perpendicular to the flow to shut off or relieve pressure. The design is applied in mechanical systems requiring reliable pressure-relief or shut-off functions.
[F16K 17/0466] with a special seating surface -- This subclass covers safety valves that incorporate a specially shaped or treated seating surface to improve sealing performance, control pressure release, and reduce wear. It is located in the CPC under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, valves, safety valves (F16K 17/0466).
[F16K 17/0473] Multiple-way safety valves -- Multiple-way safety valves are devices that automatically relieve pressure by opening one of several possible outlets when a predetermined pressure limit is exceeded, protecting equipment from over-pressure. They are used in hydraulic and pneumatic systems where fluid can be discharged through more than one line, ensuring safe operation under fault conditions.
[F16K 17/048] combined with other safety valves, or with pressure control devices -- The code F16K 17/048 covers safety valves that are integrated with additional safety valves or with pressure-control devices, forming combined assemblies for regulating or limiting pressure in mechanical systems. It applies to valve units where the safety function is merged with other protective or control components to simplify installation and improve reliability.
[F16K 17/0486] with mechanical actuating means -- This subclass covers safety valves that incorporate mechanical actuating means such as levers, cams, or spring-loaded mechanisms to trigger valve opening when preset pressure limits are exceeded. The mechanical actuation provides reliable, repeatable response without reliance on electronic or hydraulic control. It applies to devices designed to protect equipment from over-pressure.
[F16K 17/0493] with a spring other than a helicoidal spring -- The subclass F16K 17/0493 defines safety valves that employ a spring other than a helicoidal spring, such as disc, conical or other non-helical springs, to provide the retarding force for valve actuation. It falls under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, valves, safety valves.
[F16K 17/06] with special arrangements for adjusting the opening pressure -- F16K 17/06 relates to safety valves equipped with special arrangements for adjusting the opening pressure, enabling precise control of the pressure at which the valve releases. The classification includes mechanisms such as adjustable springs, weights or other devices that can be set to modify the triggering pressure.
[F16K 17/065] with differential piston -- A safety valve that employs a differential piston to control or modulate the opening pressure, using the piston's unequal areas to create a pressure-dependent movement. This design improves response accuracy and stability in mechanical engineering valve systems.
[F16K 17/08] with special arrangements for providing a large discharge passage -- F16K 17/08 is a subclass of safety valves that incorporate special arrangements to provide a large discharge passage when the valve opens. Such designs include pilot-operated, spring-assisted or other mechanisms that enlarge the outlet to allow rapid release of fluid or pressure.
[F16K 17/082] with piston -- F16K 17/082 covers safety valves that employ a piston as the moving element to open or close the valve in response to excess pressure. The piston typically works against a spring or fluid pressure to provide rapid relief and restore normal operating conditions. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering valve designs where piston-actuated protection is required.
[F16K 17/085] with diaphragm -- F16K 17/085 covers safety valves that incorporate a diaphragm as the primary moving element to sense pressure changes and control the opening of the valve. The diaphragm flexes under load, providing a reliable, leak-tight shut-off mechanism in mechanical engineering applications.
[F16K 17/087] with bellows -- F16K 17/087 covers safety valves that incorporate bellows, using the flexible element to provide sealing, compensating for thermal expansion or pressure changes, and to transmit motion between the valve components. The bellows design enhances reliability in mechanical-engineering applications where precise pressure relief is required.
[F16K 17/10] with auxiliary valve for fluid operation of the main valve -- F16K 17/10 covers safety valves that incorporate an auxiliary valve to control the fluid pressure or flow that operates the main valve. The auxiliary valve can be used for automatic resetting, pressure modulation, or staged opening of the primary safety device.
[F16K 17/105] using choking or throttling means to control the fluid operation of the main valve -- The class F16K 17/105 deals with safety valves that control the fluid operation of the main valve by means of a choking or throttling device, such as a restriction or adjustable orifice, to modulate pressure and prevent over-pressure situations.
[F16K 17/12] weight-loaded -- F16K 17/12 covers safety valves that are actuated by a weight or load, where the valve remains closed under normal pressure due to the force of the weight and opens when the system pressure exceeds this force. The classification includes designs that use gravity or other mass-based mechanisms to provide pressure relief or shutdown functions.
[F16K 17/14] with fracturing member -- F16K 17/14 covers safety valves that incorporate a fracturing member designed to break or shear when the pressure exceeds a set limit, providing rapid pressure relief. The fracturing element ensures the valve opens fully and independently of the main sealing mechanism, improving safety in high-pressure systems such as boilers, pipelines, and pressure vessels.
[F16K 17/16] with fracturing diaphragm -- F16K 17/16 covers safety valves that incorporate a fracturing diaphragm, a thin membrane designed to burst at a predetermined pressure to rapidly release fluid and protect equipment from over-pressure. The diaphragm acts as a sacrificial element that separates the valve's sealing mechanism until it ruptures, providing a reliable, fail-safe pressure-relief function.
[F16K 17/1606] of the reverse-buckling-type  F16K17/1633  takes precedence -- F16K 17/1606 covers safety valves that operate by a reverse-buckling principle, where the valve disk is held in a convex shape and snaps to an open position when internal pressure exceeds a set limit, then reseats when pressure falls. This design provides rapid, reliable over-pressure protection in a variety of mechanical systems.
[F16K 17/1613] with additional cutting means -- This subclass relates to safety valves that are equipped with a cutting device, such as a blade or saw, which can sever a pipe, rupture disc or other system component when the valve opens to relieve excess pressure. The additional cutting means creates a rapid discharge path or isolates the protected equipment.
[F16K 17/162] of the non reverse-buckling-type  F16K17/1633  takes precedence -- F16K 17/162 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to safety valves that are not of the non-reverse-buckling type, indicating a specific design where the valve does not rely on reverse buckling for its operation. This classification is used by both the European and United States patent offices to categorize such mechanical engineering elements.
[F16K 17/1626] with additional cutting means -- This subclassifies safety valves that incorporate an auxiliary cutting mechanism, allowing the valve to sever or open a line when pressure exceeds a preset limit for emergency shut-off or pressure relief. It is part of the hierarchy mechanical engineering → engineering elements and units → valves → safety valves, coded F16K 17/1626.
[F16K 17/1633] made of graphite -- The code F16K 17/1633 designates safety valves whose construction elements are made of graphite. It falls under mechanical engineering, specifically engineering elements and units for valves, and groups devices that use graphite for its high temperature resistance and lubricity in pressure-relief applications.
[F16K 17/164] and remaining closed after return of the normal pressure -- F16K 17/164 covers safety valves that remain closed after the system pressure returns to its normal level. The design prevents automatic reopening, providing protection against over-pressure once the fault condition has cleared. It is used in mechanical engineering applications where continuous sealing after pressure relief is required.
[F16K 17/168] combined with manually-controlled valves, e.g. a valve combined with a safety valve -- F16K 17/168 relates to valve assemblies that integrate a safety valve with a manually-operated valve, enabling the same unit to provide routine flow control and automatic pressure relief. These combined devices are used in pipelines and pressure systems to simplify installation and improve reliability.
[F16K 17/18] opening on surplus pressure on either side -- F16K 17/18 covers safety valves that relieve excess pressure by opening when the pressure on either side of the valve exceeds a predetermined limit. These devices are designed to protect equipment and pipelines from over-pressure conditions by venting fluid automatically. The classification applies to valves where the opening mechanism responds to surplus pressure on both the inlet and outlet sides.
[F16K 17/19] Equalising valves predominantly for tanks -- F16K 17/19 covers equalising valves that are mainly used on tanks to balance pressure between the tank interior and the external atmosphere or another vessel. These safety-oriented devices automatically admit or release fluid to maintain a set pressure range, preventing over-pressurisation and ensuring stable operation.
[F16K 17/192] with closure member in the form of a movable liquid column -- F16K 17/192 classifies safety valves that incorporate a closure element formed by a movable column of liquid, which acts as the sealing component to regulate pressure. These devices belong to the mechanical engineering group for valves and are used where liquid displacement provides the safety function.
[F16K 17/194] weight-loaded -- Weight-loaded safety valves are devices that use a mass or weight to provide the opening force needed to release excess pressure from a system. The valve remains closed until the internal pressure exceeds the force exerted by the weight, at which point it lifts to vent fluid. This simple mechanical arrangement is common in boiler and pressure-vessel protection.
[F16K 17/196] spring-loaded -- F16K 17/196 pertains to safety valves that use a spring as the actuation element. The spring stores energy and releases it to lift the valve plug when system pressure exceeds a predetermined limit, providing rapid pressure relief in hydraulic, pneumatic or steam installations. This subclass falls under Mechanical Engineering → Engineering Elements and Units → Valves → Safety valves.
[F16K 17/20] Excess-flow valves -- F16K 17/20 covers excess-flow valves, a subclass of safety valves within the mechanical-engineering field of valves and engineering elements. These devices automatically restrict fluid flow when the pressure or flow rate exceeds a predetermined limit, protecting pipelines and equipment from over-pressurization and potential damage.
[F16K 17/205] specially adapted for flexible gas lines -- F16K 17/205 pertains to safety valves that are specially adapted for flexible gas lines, allowing pressure relief while accommodating the movement and bending of flexible tubing. These valves are used in mechanical engineering applications to protect pipelines and equipment from over-pressure in systems where rigid pipe sections are impractical.
[F16K 17/22] actuated by the difference of pressure between two places in the flow line -- F16K 17/22 covers safety valves that open or close automatically when a pressure difference exists between two points of a fluid line, using that differential as the actuation force. The valve typically senses the upstream and downstream pressures and responds without external power, providing protection against over-pressure or back-flow in mechanical systems.
[F16K 17/24] acting directly on the cutting-off member -- This subclass covers safety-valve designs in which the actuation mechanism applies force directly to the cutting-off member, such as the valve disc or piston, without intermediary linkages. It includes devices where the moving element is driven straight by the control element to open or close the valve.
[F16K 17/26] operating in either direction -- F16K 17/26 classifies safety valves that are capable of operating in either direction, meaning they can open to relieve pressure from both upstream and downstream sides of a system. The code applies to devices that automatically discharge fluid when pressure exceeds a set limit regardless of flow orientation, and is used in mechanical engineering for protection of pipelines, pressure vessels and similar equipment.
[F16K 17/28] operating in one direction only -- F16K 17/28 relates to safety valves that permit flow in only one direction, preventing reverse flow and over-pressure damage. These one-direction devices are used in mechanical engineering systems where unidirectional protection is required.
[F16K 17/285] the cutting-off member being a ball  F16K17/30  takes precedence -- F16K 17/285 covers safety valves whose shut-off member is a spherical ball that moves to block flow when pressure exceeds a set limit. This subclass is a specific case within the broader group F16K17/30, which deals with safety valves having a cutting-off member.
[F16K 17/30] spring-loaded -- F16K 17/30 is a Cooperative Patent Classification for safety valves that operate by a spring-loaded mechanism. It falls under the mechanical engineering branch, specifically within engineering elements and units and the broader valve category. The code covers designs where a spring provides the force to open or close the valve in response to pressure changes.
[F16K 17/32] acting on a servo-mechanism or on a catch-releasing mechanism -- F16K 17/32 covers safety valves whose operation is controlled by a servo-mechanism or a catch-releasing device. It includes designs where an auxiliary mechanism releases or adjusts the valve position to provide automatic or semi-automatic protection against over-pressure.
[F16K 17/34] in which the flow-energy of the flowing medium actuates the closing mechanism -- Safety valves that obtain the closing force directly from the energy of the fluid in motion, such as pressure or kinetic energy, causing the valve to shut when flow conditions exceed a set limit.
[F16K 17/36] actuated in consequence of extraneous circumstances, e.g. shock, change of position -- F16K 17/36 relates to safety valves that automatically open in response to external events such as shocks, impacts or a change in position. The valve releases pressure when these extraneous circumstances occur, providing protection without manual activation.
[F16K 17/363] the closure members being rotatable or pivoting  F16K17/386  takes precedence -- F16K 17/363 in the Cooperative Patent Classification pertains to safety valves where the closure members are designed to rotate or pivot, but this classification is secondary to F16K 17/386 when applicable.
[F16K 17/366] the closure member being a movable ball  F16K17/38  takes precedence -- This subgroup covers safety valves whose shutoff element is a movable ball that seats against a seat to stop flow, including designs where the ball itself constitutes the closure member. It falls under the broader class of safety valves with movable closure members (F16K 17/38) which takes precedence.
[F16K 17/38] of excessive temperature -- F16K 17/38 covers safety valves that act when temperature climbs above a prescribed limit, automatically opening to relieve pressure and protect equipment. These devices are part of mechanical-engineering valve assemblies, designed to prevent overheating damage in pipelines, boilers and other pressure-bearing systems.
[F16K 17/383] the valve comprising fusible, softening or meltable elements, e.g. used as link, blocking element, seal, closure plug  F16K17/386  takes precedence -- F16K 17/383 covers safety valves that incorporate fusible, softening or meltable elements which function as a link, blocking device, seal or closure plug. The meltable component is designed to fail or open under predetermined temperature or pressure conditions, providing a fail-safe response. The more specific sub-group F16K 17/386 supersedes this entry.
[F16K 17/386] the closure members being rotatable or pivoting -- This subclass covers safety valves whose closing element moves by rotation or pivoting rather than linear motion. It includes designs where the valve disc, plug or similar member swings about a fixed axis to seal or release the flow, typically using spring or weight-powered mechanisms.
[F16K 17/40] with a fracturing member, e.g. fracturing diaphragm, glass, fusible joint -- F16K 17/40 classifies safety valves that contain a fracturing member - such as a diaphragm, glass element or fusible joint - which ruptures at a set pressure to safely release excess fluid or gas.
[F16K 17/403] with a fracturing valve member -- The F16K 17/403 class covers safety valves that incorporate a fracturing valve member, a component that breaks or ruptures at a predetermined pressure to quickly release excess fluid or gas. It pertains to mechanical engineering valve designs where controlled fracturing provides rapid pressure relief, enhancing system protection against over-pressure events.
[F16K 17/406] the fracturing member being a generally elongated member, e.g. rod or wire, which is directly connected to a movable valve member, the breaking or buckling of the elongated member allowing the valve member to move to a closed or open position -- In this safety-valve design a rod-like fracturing element is attached directly to the movable valve part; when the rod fractures or buckles the valve is released to either close or open, providing a simple mechanical actuation for over-pressure protection.
[F16K 17/42] Valves preventing penetration of air in the outlet of containers for liquids -- F16K 17/42 covers valves designed to stop air from entering the outlet of liquid containers. These devices protect the stored liquid from oxidation, contamination or pressure loss by preventing back-flow of air when the container is emptied or pressure changes.
[F16K 19/00] Arrangements of valves and flow lines specially adapted for mixing fluids  multiple-way valves  F16K11/00 -- **Definition:** This place covers:  When no details of the valve members or seats are disclosed in the document.
[F16K 19/003] Specially adapted for boilers -- The CPC subclass F16K 19/003 is part of the mechanical-engineering group for valves and describes arrangements of valves and flow lines that are specially adapted for boiler applications. It includes designs that mix fluids and use multiple-way valves to control steam, water, and other boiler fluids.
[F16K 19/006] Specially adapted for faucets -- F16K 19/006 covers valve arrangements and flow-line configurations that are specifically designed for faucets, enabling controlled mixing, dispensing and regulation of liquids. The subclass includes multiple-way or mixing valves integrated into plumbing fixtures to manage flow, temperature and pressure.
[F16K 2099/0069] Bistable microvalves -- F16K 2099/0069 pertains to bistable microvalves, which are miniature fluid-control components that remain in either an open or closed position without continuous actuation power. The classification includes designs that latch mechanically or magnetically, various actuation methods such as thermal, piezoelectric or electrostatic triggers, and integration in microfluidic and MEMS systems for precise, low-energy flow regulation.
[F16K 2099/0071] with latching means -- F16K 2099/0071 covers fluid-control devices such as gates or valves that incorporate a latch mechanism to retain a chosen position without continuous external actuation. The latching means may be mechanical, spring-loaded or magnetic, allowing the device to stay open or closed until deliberately released.
[F16K 2099/0073] Fabrication methods specifically adapted for microvalves -- F16K 2099/0073 covers manufacturing techniques specially adapted to produce micro-scale valves, such as micromachining, MEMS processes, thin-film deposition, and other precision fabrication methods used to create valves with dimensions in the micrometer range and to integrate them into microfluidic or miniaturised fluid-handling systems.
[F16K 2099/0074] using photolithography, e.g. etching -- F16K 2099/0074 covers the manufacturing of valve components by photolithographic methods, such as etching, to create precise micro-structured features on the parts. It relates to applying semiconductor-type patterning techniques in the production of valves and their sub-assemblies.
[F16K 2099/0076] using electrical discharge machining [EDM], milling or drilling -- The CPC entry F16K 2099/0076 covers machining operations in which a workpiece is shaped or refined by means of electrical discharge machining, milling or drilling. It includes processes that combine these techniques to remove material, create fine features or finish surfaces, typically in hard or conductive materials where conventional cutting is difficult.
[F16K 2099/0078] using moulding or stamping -- F16K 2099/0078 classifies inventions that employ moulding or stamping techniques to produce fluid-power components such as seals, packs and related parts. The entry covers processes like injection, compression or compression-moulding of polymers and metal-stamping of housings used in hydraulic or pneumatic systems.
[F16K 2099/008] Multi-layer fabrications -- F16K 2099/008 covers machines and processes for producing multi-layer packaging articles, including the creation of composite films, laminates or containers that combine two or more material layers in a single structure. The classification applies to equipment that forms, bonds, or assembles these layers during the manufacturing of packaging containers.
[F16K 2099/0082] Microvalves adapted for a particular use -- Microvalves adapted for a particular use are miniature flow-control devices-typically at the micrometer scale-designed and optimized for specific applications such as medical instruments, micro-fluidic chips, or precision dispensing systems. The subclass covers their specialized constructions, actuation methods, and integration into targeted functional environments.
[F16K 2099/0084] Chemistry or biology, e.g. "lab-on-a-chip" technology -- F16K 2099/0084 covers apparatus that integrate chemical or biological processes on a miniature chip, such as lab-on-a-chip devices that combine microfluidic channels, sensors and reactors for analysis, synthesis or diagnostic functions.
[F16K 2099/0086] Medical applications -- F16K 2099/0086 covers sealing elements, gaskets, or grooving features that are specially adapted for medical use, such as seals in implants, diagnostic or therapeutic devices, drug containers, and other health-care equipment.
[F16K 2099/0088] Implanted devices -- The CPC subclass F16K 2099/0088 covers implanted devices that incorporate electrical connectors or contact structures intended for permanent or temporary placement within a living organism, including medical implants such as pacemakers, neurostimulators, and implantable sensors.
[F16K 2099/009] Fluid power devices -- F16K 2099/009 relates to fluid-power devices that do not fall under more specific headings, encompassing apparatus for generating, transmitting, controlling or utilizing hydraulic or pneumatic energy such as hydraulic cylinders, accumulators, control valves and similar components used in power-transmission systems. It is a catch-all subclass within the broader F16K group for miscellaneous fluid-power mechanisms.
[F16K 2099/0092] Inkjet printers -- F16K 2099/0092 covers inkjet printers, encompassing devices that form images by propelling droplets of ink onto a substrate. It includes various mechanisms for ink delivery, printhead designs, and control systems used in commercial and consumer printing equipment.
[F16K 2099/0094] Micropumps -- F16K 2099/0094 covers micropumps, which are pumps designed to move very small fluid volumes, typically in the microlitre-per-minute range, for applications such as medical devices, lab-on-a-chip systems, and miniature cooling. The classification includes various actuation methods - mechanical, piezoelectric, electroosmotic, or thermal - and applies to pumps integrated in micro-fluidic circuits or stand-alone devices.
[F16K 2099/0096] Fuel injection devices -- F16K2099/0096 covers fuel injection devices used to deliver fuel into internal combustion engines, including mechanisms that atomize, pressurise, or meter fuel, and components such as injectors, fuel rails, and control units. It also includes electronic and hydraulic control arrangements that adjust injection timing and quantity.
[F16K 2099/0098] Refrigeration circuits, e.g. for cooling integrated circuits -- F16K 2099/0098 falls within the Cooperative Patent Classification for fluid power and thermal systems and covers refrigeration circuits specifically designed to cool integrated circuits. It includes the structure and operation of miniature cooling loops, heat exchangers, and related components used to manage heat in electronic devices.
[F16K 21/00] Fluid-delivery valves, -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Valves that are either self-closing or provide a continuous small flow.
[F16K 21/02] providing a continuous small flow -- F16K 21/02 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to fluid-delivery valves designed to provide a continuous small flow, typically used in applications requiring precise control of fluid delivery.
[F16K 21/04] Self-closing valves, i.e. closing automatically after operation -- F16K 21/04 refers to self-closing fluid-delivery valves that automatically return to the closed position after the actuating event, eliminating the need for external control and ensuring flow stops promptly in hydraulic or pneumatic systems.
[F16K 21/06] in which the closing movement, either retarded or not, starts immediately after opening -- F16K 21/06 classifies fluid-delivery valves whose closing stroke is initiated immediately after the valve has been opened, whether the closure is delayed or not. It covers designs where the valve's mechanism triggers the closing movement directly following the opening action, typically using spring-return or hydraulic timing.
[F16K 21/08] with ball-shaped closing members -- The subclass F16K 21/08 refers to fluid-delivery valves that employ a ball-shaped closing member to stop or regulate flow. The spherical plug rotates within a valve body, seating against seats to achieve a seal and is typically actuated by a stem, lever or pneumatic mechanism, and is used in shut-off, mixing or control applications.
[F16K 21/10] with hydraulic brake cylinder acting on the closure member -- F16K 21/10 covers fluid-delivery valves that integrate a hydraulic brake cylinder which directly operates the valve's closure element, providing braking force to control the valve's movement. This subclass is used for designs where the brake cylinder assists in sealing or stopping the valve plug or disc.
[F16K 21/12] with hydraulically-operated opening means -- F16K 21/12 covers fluid-delivery valves whose opening mechanism is powered hydraulically. It includes designs where hydraulic pressure moves a piston, diaphragm or other actuator to open the valve passage, as well as related control and sealing arrangements. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, and the broader category of valves.
[F16K 21/14] with special means for preventing the self-closing -- F16K 21/14 pertains to fluid-delivery valves equipped with mechanisms that prevent the valve from closing on its own, ensuring uninterrupted flow. Examples include spring-biased, pressure-assisted, or other actuating means that keep the valve open under normal operating conditions, commonly used in hydraulic, pneumatic, or liquid supply systems.
[F16K 21/16] closing after a predetermined quantity of fluid has been delivered -- This subclass covers valves that automatically shut off the flow once a set volume of fluid has passed, used in metering and dosing applications. It includes mechanisms that count the delivered quantity and actuate closure without external control.
[F16K 21/165] with means sensing the weight of said fluid quantity -- The subclass F16K 21/165 covers fluid-delivery valves that incorporate a mechanism to sense the weight of the fluid being discharged, allowing the valve to control or monitor flow based on the measured mass.
[F16K 21/18] closed when a rising liquid reaches a predetermined level -- F16K 21/18 covers fluid-delivery valves that automatically shut off when a rising liquid reaches a predetermined level, typically used to prevent over-filling in tanks, reservoirs or similar containers. The mechanism detects the liquid height and closes the valve at the set point, ensuring controlled discharge and protection against spillage.
[F16K 21/185] with electrical or magnetical means, e.g. with magnetic floats, for sensing the liquid level -- F16K 21/185 classifies fluid-delivery valves that incorporate electrical or magnetic devices to detect the level of liquid inside, for example using magnetic floats or other sensor elements that generate a signal for level monitoring. This falls under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, and valves.
[F16K 21/20] by means making use of air-suction through an opening closed by the rising liquid -- F16K 21/20 covers fluid-delivery valves that operate by drawing air through an opening which is sealed when the liquid level rises, using the suction effect to control flow. The design relies on the rising liquid to close the inlet, allowing air-based actuation without external power. This subgroup falls under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, and valves.
[F16K 2200/00] Details of valves -- F16K 2200/00 covers the detailed aspects of valves, including their internal geometry, sealing arrangements, actuation mechanisms, flow-control features and methods of adjusting or monitoring performance. The subclass groups inventions that specify or improve particular valve components, configurations or operating characteristics.
[F16K 2200/10] Means for compensation of misalignment between seat and closure member -- The classification covers devices that accommodate or correct misalignment between a valve seat and its closure member, ensuring proper sealing and operation. It includes adjustable seats, flexible seals, or other mechanisms that compensate for positional errors, wear, or deformation in valve assemblies.
[F16K 2200/101] closure member self-aligning to seat -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[F16K 2200/102] seat self-aligning to closure member -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[F16K 2200/20] Common housing having a single inlet, a single outlet and multiple valve members -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices classified in this group have a single inlet and a single outlet.  Multi-port and multi-way valves are not classified in this group.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[F16K 2200/201] of diverse type, size or shape -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices in which the valves are of different types, e.g. disc valves and ball valves, or are of different size so that they operate at different pressures. [F16K 2200/202] one valve arranged inside of the valve member of a second valve, e.g. nested valve members -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices in which one of the valves carries the seat for at least one other valve, and also serves as the support for the other valve. [F16K 2200/203] in parallel -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.

[F16K 2200/30] Spring arrangements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The spring may be any type of resilient element.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[F16K 2200/301] Common spring for multiple closure members -- **Definition:** This place covers:  A single spring biasing a plurality of closure members.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[F16K 2200/302] Plurality of biasing means, e.g. springs, for opening or closing single valve member -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Valves in which the plural springs bias the single valve member open or closed.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[F16K 2200/303] Means for protecting the spring in the fluid flow path -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The spring must be in the flow path of the fluid, and the means for protecting the spring must prevent or reduce the impingement of the fluid on the spring.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[F16K 2200/304] Adjustable spring pre-loading -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Valves provided with means adjusting the preload of the biasing means, usually in terms of the pressure at which the valve will open. In devices classified in this group, only the preload is adjustable. The spring rate of the spring is not modified.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[F16K 2200/305] Constructional features of springs -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Valves with spring arrangements of non-conventional type.
[F16K 2200/3051] Generally flat springs -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Springs that are generally planar in the uncompressed state.  Magnetically operated valve with spider armature
[F16K 2200/3052] Cantilever springs -- **Definition:** This place covers:  At least a portion of the spring projects along a plane extending from one or more attachment points.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.  Actuating mechanism for a rolling ball valve
[F16K 2200/3053] Helicoidal springs of variable pitch, diameter or spring rate -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Spring includes a pitch, diameter or spring rate value that is not constant.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[F16K 2200/40] Bleeding means in closed position of the valve, e.g. bleeding passages -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Valves provided with at least one passage for allowing a limited amount of fluid to flow through the valve when the valve is in the closed position, including details of leak passages.
[F16K 2200/401] arranged on the closure member -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Valves provided with at least one passage arranged on the closure member for allowing a limited amount of fluid to flow through the valve when the valve is in the closed position.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.  Bleeding passage 68 provided on closure member
[F16K 2200/402] arranged on the valve housing or seat -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Valves provided with at least one passage arranged on the valve housing or seat for allowing a limited amount of fluid to flow through the valve when the valve is in the closed position.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.  Bleeding passage 68 provided on seat/housing 20.
[F16K 2200/50] Self-contained valve assemblies -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Valve assemblies with most or all of its components housed in a separate housing that are then inserted into the valve housing.  Cartridge valve 10 is inserted into housing 12
[F16K 2200/501] Cartridge valves -- **Definition:** This place covers:  A valve assembly contained within a housing, which is usually replaceable and designed to permit ready insertion or removal into another housing or manifold.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[F16K 2200/502] Cages for valves, i.e. means to be inserted within the valve housing, surrounding and guiding the closure member -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Valves provided with a separate housing component (i.e. cage) that is inserted within the valve housing and with said cage surrounding and guiding the closure member.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.  Cage 14 guides closure member 13.
[F16K 23/00] Valves for preventing drip from nozzles -- F16K 23/00 covers valves designed to stop or reduce unintended leakage from spray or jet nozzles after flow cessation. These devices typically use spring-loaded or gravity-controlled mechanisms to seal the nozzle and prevent drips in applications such as spray painting, fuel injection, or irrigation.
[F16K 24/00] Devices, e.g. valves, for venting or aerating enclosures -- The F16K 24/00 subclass covers devices, primarily valves, designed to vent or aerate enclosed spaces by allowing controlled passage of gases or liquids. It includes mechanisms that relieve pressure, introduce air, or maintain a desired internal atmosphere in containers, tanks, or other sealed structures. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, and the broader category of valves.
[F16K 24/02] the enclosure being itself a valve, tap, or cock -- F16K 24/02 covers devices in which the enclosure itself functions as a valve, tap or cock, allowing venting or aeration directly through the container wall. The classification applies to integrated valve mechanisms that are part of the housing rather than separate components, typically used in fluid handling or pressure regulation systems.
[F16K 24/04] for venting only -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The air (or fluid) from the circuit is released.
[F16K 24/042] actuated by a float -- F16K 24/042 covers valves whose operation is controlled by a float that moves in response to the level of a fluid; the float's motion opens or closes the valve to vent or aerate an enclosure. Such devices are common in tanks, reservoirs and similar containers.
[F16K 24/044] the float being rigidly connected to the valve element, the assembly of float and valve element following a substantially translational movement when actuated, e.g. also for actuating a pilot valve -- F16K 24/044 pertains to valve devices in which a float is rigidly attached to the valve element so that, when actuated, the float and valve move together in a substantially translational motion, a configuration that can also be used to drive a pilot valve.
[F16K 24/046] the assembly of float and valve element being a single spherical element -- F16K 24/046 covers devices in which the float and the valve are combined into a single spherical element that both senses fluid level and provides venting or aeration of an enclosure. This integrated design is used in mechanical engineering applications such as tanks, reservoirs and pipelines to simplify construction and improve reliability.
[F16K 24/048] a transmission element, e.g. arm, being interposed between the float and the valve element, the transmission element following a non-translational, e.g. pivoting or rocking, movement when actuated -- F16K 24/048 relates to transmission elements, such as arms, placed between a float and a valve component, where the transmission element operates by a non-translational motion like pivoting or rocking when the valve is actuated. These parts are used in devices that vent or aerate enclosures.
[F16K 24/06] for aerating only -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fluid (air) is admited in the circuit.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Venting of plumbing installations, waste pipes -> E03C
[F16K 25/00] Details -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Materials and arrangements of valve members and seats not otherwise provided for
[F16K 25/005] Particular materials for seats or closure elements -- The CPC entry F16K 25/005 concerns the choice or specification of particular materials used for valve seats or other closure components. It is part of the mechanical engineering hierarchy, under engineering elements and units and specifically within the valves group.
[F16K 25/02] Arrangements using fluid issuing from valve members or seats -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[F16K 25/04] Arrangements for preventing erosion, not otherwise provided for -- F16K 25/04 covers designs of valve arrangements that incorporate features to prevent erosion of components, such as protective linings, flow-direction control, or wear-resistant inserts, when the erosion mitigation is not covered by other specific subclasses.
[F16K 27/00] Construction of housing -- F16K 27/00 covers the design and manufacture of valve housings, describing the construction of the enclosure that contains the moving parts of a valve. It includes choices of materials, methods of joining or assembling components, and features that protect the valve from pressure, corrosion or mechanical damage, focusing on the structural aspects of the valve unit.
[F16K 27/003] Housing formed from a plurality of the same valve elements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[F16K 27/006] of hydrants -- F16K 27/006 covers the construction of the housing or outer casing for hydrants, including the design, material selection and manufacturing methods used to form the valve enclosure of fire-or water-hydrant units. It falls under mechanical engineering, specifically engineering elements and units for valves.
[F16K 27/02] of lift valves -- This CPC subgroup covers the design and construction of the housing that encloses a lift valve, including features that support the valve body, guide its vertical motion and provide sealing or mounting arrangements.
[F16K 27/0209] Check valves or pivoted valves -- F16K 27/0209 pertains to the construction of housings for check or pivoted valves, covering the design features and methods used to enclose the valve mechanism. It includes details on the shape, material selection, sealing arrangements and integration of moving valve components within the housing. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, and valves.
[F16K 27/0218] Butterfly valves -- Butterfly valves are rotary-disc flow-control devices in which a circular disc mounted on a shaft pivots within a hollow housing to open or close a pipeline. The CPC subclass F16K 27/0218 pertains to the construction of the valve housing and related sealing or mounting features specific to butterfly valves.
[F16K 27/0227] with the valve members swinging around an axis located at the edge of or outside the valve member -- Group F16K27/0227 is impacted by reclassification into group F16K27/0232 . Groups F16K27/0227 and F16K27/0232 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[F16K 27/0232] the valve member retained by a removable closure -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices in which the valve housing is provided with a removable closure element which retains the valve member in assembled relation with its seat.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[F16K 27/0236] Diaphragm cut-off apparatus -- A diaphragm cut-off apparatus is a valve unit that uses a flexible diaphragm to seal off fluid flow, providing precise shut-off and isolation in hydraulic or pneumatic circuits. It is mounted within a housing construction, allowing reliable control of pressure and preventing leakage in mechanical systems.
[F16K 27/0245] with ball-shaped valve members -- F16K 27/0245 classifies valves that use ball-shaped valve members as the moving sealing element, applied in the construction of housing within mechanical engineering. The code covers designs where a spherical ball controls fluid flow by rotating to open or close the valve.
[F16K 27/0254] with conical shaped valve members -- F16K 27/0254 classifies valves whose housing incorporates one or more conical shaped valve members. It falls under mechanical engineering, specifically engineering elements and units for valves, focusing on the construction of the valve housing. The code is used by the EPO and USPTO to index patents involving conical valve components.
[F16K 27/0263] multiple way valves -- F16K 27/0263 covers multiple-way valves, focusing on the design and construction of their housings. It pertains to valves that direct flow among several outlets or inlets and includes details of casing geometry, sealing arrangements and assembly methods.
[F16K 27/0272] valves provided with a lining -- F16K 27/0272 concerns valves whose interior surfaces or seats are equipped with a protective or functional lining. The lining, which may be ceramic, polymer, metal or composite, is applied to the valve housing to enhance wear resistance, corrosion protection or sealing performance. This subclass includes designs where the lining is integral to the valve construction.
[F16K 27/0281] Housings in two parts which can be orientated in different positions -- This subclass covers valve housings made of two separate sections that can be assembled in more than one relative orientation. The design allows the housing to be positioned differently to accommodate varying flow directions, service access, or installation constraints, while still forming a sealed enclosure for the valve components.
[F16K 27/029] Electromagnetically actuated valves -- F16K 27/029 designates valves that are opened or closed by electromagnetic force, using coils or magnetic fields to move the valve element. The code covers the construction of the valve housing that incorporates this electromagnetic actuation system.
[F16K 27/04] of sliding valves -- F16K 27/04 relates to the construction of housing for sliding valves, covering designs that support the guiding, sealing and mounting of the moving valve member within a valve body. It addresses features such as guideways, sealing arrangements and attachment methods that enable reliable linear motion of the valve.
[F16K 27/041] cylindrical slide valves -- F16K 27/041 covers cylindrical slide valves and the methods for constructing their housings. The classification addresses design features, machining processes, and assembly techniques that ensure proper sealing and flow control in hydraulic or pneumatic systems. It falls under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, and the broader valve category.
[F16K 27/042] Hydraulic fluid leak traps -- F16K 27/042 covers hydraulic fluid leak traps incorporated in valve housings. These devices collect or redirect fluid that escapes from seals or joints, minimizing loss and contamination in hydraulic systems. They are part of the construction of housing for valves within mechanical engineering.
[F16K 27/044] slide valves with flat obturating members -- Slide valves that incorporate flat, rectangular sealing surfaces to close the flow path within a housing. The flat obturating members slide perpendicular to the flow direction, providing a simple, low-friction sealing method suitable for hydraulic, pneumatic or fluid power systems. These designs are used where precise control and easy maintenance are required.
[F16K 27/045] with pivotal obturating members -- A valve in this class uses a sealing member that is mounted on a pivot so it swings to open or close the flow path, allowing quick and reliable shut-off. The design is suited for housings such as container doors, protective covers or other enclosure structures where a rotating plug provides sealing.
[F16K 27/047] with wedge-shaped obturating members -- F16K 27/047 covers valve designs that use wedge-shaped obturating members to achieve a tight shut-off within the valve housing. The wedge shape provides a sealing surface that slides against a seat, allowing reliable flow control and resistance to pressure.
[F16K 27/048] Electromagnetically actuated valves -- F16K 27/048 covers valves whose movement is driven by electromagnetic forces, such as solenoid or coil-actuated devices used for opening or closing fluid passages. These valves are employed in mechanical systems where rapid or remote control of flow is required.
[F16K 27/06] of taps or cocks -- The F16K 27/06 sub-class covers the construction of valve housings specifically for taps or cocks, detailing the design and assembly of the internal chambers, ports and sealing arrangements that form the water-flow control element in plumbing fixtures.
[F16K 27/062] with conical plugs -- F16K 27/062 refers to valves whose housing incorporates a conical plug that slides or rotates to open and close the flow passage. The conical shape creates a sealing contact with a matching seat, allowing tight shut-off and precise regulation of fluid or gas flow within mechanical systems.
[F16K 27/065] with cylindrical plugs -- The CPC subclass F16K 27/065 covers valves whose closing member is a cylindrical plug. It pertains to mechanical-engineering valve designs where a straight-sided, rod-like plug moves axially to open or seal the flow passage. This type of valve is used in applications requiring simple, robust shut-off mechanisms.
[F16K 27/067] with spherical plugs -- CPC subclass F16K 27/067 concerns valves whose housing is built to incorporate spherical plugs. It includes designs where a ball-shaped plug rotates or moves within the valve body to open or close the flow path, covering both manually and automatically operated versions used in fluid control systems.
[F16K 27/07] of cutting-off parts of tanks, e.g. tank-cars -- The CPC subclass F16K 27/07 covers devices or methods for cutting off portions of tanks, such as railway tank-cars, to enable removal, cleaning, emptying or emergency discharge of the tank contents. It falls under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, valves, and construction of housing.
[F16K 27/08] Guiding yokes for spindles -- Guiding yokes for spindles are components that support and align the spindle within a valve housing, preventing lateral movement and ensuring precise operation. They are typically mounted in the housing and may be adjustable or fixed, used in various mechanical engineering valve applications.
[F16K 27/10] Welded housings -- F16K 27/10 covers welded housings for valves, referring to the construction of the valve body by welding methods. It includes designs where the housing is formed from welded pieces, providing strength and leak resistance for various pressure applications.
[F16K 27/102] for lift-valves -- F16K 27/102 covers lift-valves, a type of valve in which the sealing element is raised vertically to open a passage, typically used in fluid-handling systems. The subclass falls under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, valves, and specifically addresses the construction of the valve housing that accommodates the lifting mechanism.
[F16K 27/105] for gate valves -- The F16K 27/105 subclass covers the construction of the housing for gate valves, detailing the design, materials and manufacturing methods used for the valve body that contains the moving gate. It includes features such as sealing arrangements, reinforcement structures and methods for assembling the housing to ensure reliable pressure containment.
[F16K 27/107] for taps or cocks -- F16K 27/107 covers the design, construction and arrangement of the housing or body of taps and cocks, the components that control the flow of fluids in a pipe. It deals with the structural features that secure the valve seat, stem and operating mechanisms within the enclosure, ensuring proper sealing and durability under pressure. This subclass falls under Mechanical Engineering → Engineering Elements and Units → Valves → Construction of housing.
[F16K 27/12] Covers for housings -- F16K 27/12 pertains to protective covers designed for the housings of valves, addressing the construction, material selection, and attachment methods that shield the internal valve components from external influences and wear. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, specifically the valves category.
[F16K 29/00] Arrangements for movement of valve members other than for opening and closing the valve, e.g. for grinding-in, for preventing sticking -- F16K 29/00 covers devices and mechanisms that move valve components for purposes other than the normal opening or closing action, such as grinding-in, cleaning, or preventing the parts from sticking. These arrangements are used to maintain valve function and reliability.
[F16K 29/02] providing for continuous motion -- F16K 29/02 covers mechanisms that keep valve components in continuous motion for functions other than opening or closing, such as grinding-in, self-lubricating actions, or preventing sticking. The classification applies to arrangements that ensure ongoing movement of valve members to maintain performance and reduce friction.
[F16K 3/00] Gate valves or sliding valves, i.e. cut-off apparatus with closing members having a sliding movement along the seat for opening and closing -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[F16K 3/02] with flat sealing faces -- F16K 3/02 covers gate or sliding valves whose closing members slide along the seat and incorporate flat sealing faces to achieve a seal. These valves are used as cut-off devices in fluid handling systems where a simple planar contact surface provides reliable sealing.
[F16K 3/0209] the valve having a particular passage, e.g. provided with a filter, throttle or safety device -- F16K 3/0209 denotes gate or sliding valves that incorporate an auxiliary passage in the flow path, such as a filter, throttle or safety device, integrated within the valve body to provide additional flow control, protection or monitoring functions.
[F16K 3/0218] with only one sealing face -- F16K 3/0218 covers gate or sliding valves in which the moving closing member contacts the seat on only a single sealing face, providing a cut-off function without a second mating surface. This design simplifies the sealing arrangement while maintaining the sliding motion required for opening and closing.
[F16K 3/0227] Packings -- Packings are sealing components used in gate or sliding valves to fill the clearance between the moving closing member and its seat, preventing leakage of fluid or gas. They can be made of compressible materials such as rope, graphite, PTFE, or composite sheets, and are sized to accommodate the valve's travel while maintaining a reliable seal throughout operation.
[F16K 3/0236] the packing being of a non-resilient material, e.g. ceramic, metal -- F16K 3/0236 pertains to gate valves or sliding valves where the packing material used for sealing is non-resilient, such as ceramic or metal, ensuring a tight seal without elastic deformation.
[F16K 3/0245] Curtain gate valves -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[F16K 3/0254] being operated by particular means -- F16K 3/0254 relates to gate or sliding valves whose closing member moves along the seat and is driven by particular operating means such as hydraulic, pneumatic, electric or manual actuators, specifying the mechanism used to open and close the valve.
[F16K 3/0263] using particular material or covering means -- F16K 3/0263 pertains to the selection or application of specific materials or protective coverings for gate and sliding valves, such as corrosion-resistant alloys, polymer linings or surface treatments, aimed at enhancing durability, sealing or reducing wear.
[F16K 3/0272] permitting easy assembly or disassembly -- F16K 3/0272 relates to gate or sliding valves that are constructed to allow easy assembly or disassembly, often by using hinged sections, removable fasteners or modular components that simplify maintenance, repair or replacement.

[F16K 3/029] with two or more gates -- F16K 3/029 covers gate or sliding valves that use two or more gate elements to control flow. The gates move linearly along the seat and can be operated together or independently to start, stop, or modulate the passage of fluid.
[F16K 3/03] with a closure member in the form of an iris-diaphragm -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[F16K 3/04] with pivoted closure members -- F16K 3/04 relates to gate or sliding valves whose closing element is mounted on a pivot, allowing it to swing into or out of the seat rather than move strictly linearly. The pivoted closure members reduce friction and enable compact, reliable operation in fluid-control systems such as water, gas or oil pipelines.
[F16K 3/06] in the form of closure plates arranged between supply and discharge passages -- F16K 3/06 concerns gate or sliding valves that use a flat closure plate positioned between the supply and discharge passages. [F16K 31/00] Operating means -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Magnetic constructions with armature (actuators) -> H01F7/08 - Control or regulation of electric motors -> H02P7/00
[F16K 31/001] actuated by volume variations caused by an element soluble in a fluid or swelling in contact with a fluid  life-boats  B63C9/24 -- F16K 31/001 covers valves whose actuation relies on volume changes of a material that either dissolves in a fluid or swells when in contact with it. Such devices are commonly used in safety-critical applications like lifeboat release systems, where the fluid-induced expansion or dissolution triggers the opening of the valve.
[F16K 31/002] actuated by temperature variation  thermo-electric  F16K31/025 -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Sensitive elements producing movements (e.g. bimetallic strips). -> G12B1/02
[F16K 31/003] operated without a stable intermediate position, e.g. with snap action  F16K31/56  takes precedence -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[F16K 31/004] actuated by piezoelectric means -- F16K 31/004 covers valves whose operating means are driven by piezoelectric actuators, using the material's rapid deformation under an electric field to open or close the valve. These devices provide fast, precise flow control and are commonly used in fuel injection, micro-fluidic, and other high-speed valve applications.
[F16K 31/005] Piezoelectric benders -- A piezoelectric bender is a thin, flexible actuator that bends when an electric voltage is applied, using the piezoelectric effect to produce precise linear or angular motion. In the CPC, it falls under operating means for valves, classified under F16K 31/005.
[F16K 31/006] having a free end -- F16K 31/006 covers valve operating mechanisms in which the moving part terminates with a free end, i.e., not fixed to a guide or enclosure. Such configurations are used where the valve element can swing or slide without a supporting stem, typically in simple or compact flow-control devices.
[F16K 31/007] Piezoelectric stacks -- F16K 31/007 covers piezoelectric stacks, which are assemblies of multiple piezoelectric elements bonded together to produce a single actuator capable of generating high forces and precise linear displacements when voltage is applied. They are used as operating means in valve systems to provide fast, fine-controlled opening and closing, especially in high-pressure or high-frequency applications.
[F16K 31/008] for sliding valves -- This subclass covers mechanisms for operating sliding valves, where the valve element moves linearly to open or close a passage. It includes drive arrangements, guides, seals and control devices specifically for sliding motion.
[F16K 31/02] electric -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Magnetic circuits and magnets in general -> H01F
[F16K 31/025] actuated by thermo-electric means -- F16K 31/025 pertains to valves that are operated by thermo-electric means, where temperature-induced electrical effects or thermoelectric devices generate the force needed to open or close the valve. This classification covers mechanisms that convert heat or temperature differences into mechanical motion for valve control.
[F16K 31/04] using a motor -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Details of the magnetic circuit of the motor -> H02K1/00
[F16K 31/041] for rotating valves  F16K31/055  takes precedence -- F16K 31/041 classifies rotating valves used as operating means in mechanical-engineering valve assemblies. It covers designs where the valve element turns to open or close the flow path. The more specific sub-class F16K 31/055 supersedes it.
[F16K 31/042] with electric means, e.g. for controlling the motor or a clutch between the valve and the motor -- The CPC class F16K 31/042 covers valve systems that include electric components to regulate the drive, such as electric control of the motor or a clutch linking the motor to the valve. It falls under operating means for mechanical engineering valves.
[F16K 31/043] characterised by mechanical means between the motor and the valve, e.g. lost motion means reducing backlash, clutches, brakes or return means -- This subgroup of F16K covers valves that use mechanical devices between the motor and the valve to modify motion, absorb backlash or provide holding and return functions, such as lost-motion mechanisms, clutches, brakes or return means.
[F16K 31/045] with torque limiters -- F16K 31/045 covers valve operating mechanisms that incorporate torque limiters, which are devices that prevent the transmission of excessive torque to the valve stem or actuator. The torque-limiting feature protects the valve and its drive system from overload, ensuring safe and reliable operation under abnormal load conditions.
[F16K 31/046] with electric means, e.g. electric switches, to control the motor or to control a clutch between the valve and the motor  F16K31/041  takes precedence -- F16K31/046 covers valve operating mechanisms that use electric devices such as switches to control the drive motor or to engage a clutch between the motor and the valve. This subcategory refines the broader class F16K31/041, which deals with electric control of valve actuation.
[F16K 31/047] characterised by mechanical means between the motor and the valve, e.g. lost motion means reducing backlash, clutches, brakes or return means  F16K31/043  takes precedence -- F16K 31/047 classifies valves whose actuation includes mechanical devices between the motor and the valve, such as lost-motion mechanisms that reduce backlash, clutches, brakes or return-means that control motion. It is a sub-category of the broader F16K 31/043 group.
[F16K 31/048] with torque limiters  F16K31/041  takes precedence -- F16K 31/048 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to valves equipped with torque limiters, a specific type of operating mechanism. This classification is part of a broader category dealing with engineering elements and units, specifically focusing on mechanical engineering aspects of valves. However, it notes that F16K 31/041 takes precedence when applicable.
[F16K 31/05] specially adapted for operating hand-operated valves or for combined motor and hand operation -- F16K 31/05 classifies devices that are specially adapted to operate hand-operated valves, including mechanisms that combine manual and motor drive. It belongs to the mechanical-engineering hierarchy under engineering elements and units, valves, and operating means.
[F16K 31/055] for rotating valves -- F16K 31/055 covers valves whose operating principle relies on rotation of the valve element to regulate flow, including rotary ball, disc, plug and multi-port designs. The classification applies to mechanisms that turn the valve body or stem to align openings and achieve shut-off, throttling or switching functions in hydraulic, pneumatic or fluid-handling systems.
[F16K 31/06] using a magnet -- F16K 31/06 covers valve operating means that employ a magnet to actuate, control, or hold the valve member. It includes magnetic actuation, magnetic latching, and magnetic assistance for opening or closing valves in mechanical engineering applications. This subclass falls under the broader categories of mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, and valves.
[F16K 31/0603] Multiple-way valves -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[F16K 31/0606] fluid passing through the solenoid coil -- F16K 31/0606 covers devices where fluid flows through the coil of a solenoid, typically used in valve actuators that combine magnetic actuation with fluid passage. The classification falls under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, valves, operating means, and addresses designs where the solenoid coil itself forms part of the fluid path.
[F16K 31/061] Sliding valves -- The code F16K 31/061 refers to sliding valves, a type of operating means for controlling fluid flow in mechanical systems. [F16K 31/062] the valve element being at least partially ball-shaped -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[F16K 31/0624] Lift valves -- Lift valves are a type of operating means for controlling fluid flow in which a movable element is raised vertically to open a seat and allow passage, then lowered to close the passage and stop flow. They are commonly used in water distribution, irrigation and fire-protection systems where a reliable, rapid opening action is required, and are classified under CPC code F16K 31/0624 within mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, valves.
[F16K 31/0627] with movable valve member positioned between seats -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
