[F16K 31/0634] with fixed seats positioned between movable valve members -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[F16K 31/0637] with ball shaped valve members -- F16K 31/0637 covers operating mechanisms for valves that use ball-shaped members to control flow. It is classified under mechanical engineering → engineering elements and units → valves → operating means, and details designs such as ball-type shut-off, throttle or mixing devices.
[F16K 31/0641] the valve member being a diaphragm -- F16K 31/0641 covers valves in which the flow-controlling element is a flexible diaphragm, the diaphragm acting as the moving member that opens or closes the passage. This design provides a seal that can conform to irregular surfaces and is commonly used in pressure-regulating or safety-relief applications.
[F16K 31/0644] One-way valve -- A one-way valve is an operating means that permits fluid flow in a single direction while automatically preventing reverse flow. It is used in pipelines, hydraulic and pneumatic circuits to protect equipment and maintain system pressure, employing designs such as swing, flap, or ball mechanisms.
[F16K 31/0648] the armature and the valve member forming one element  F16K31/0651  takes precedence -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[F16K 31/0651] the fluid passing through the solenoid coil -- F16K 31/0651 covers the passage of fluid through the solenoid coil of a valve, describing how the coil's magnetic field interacts with the fluid and the related operating-means design and performance considerations.
[F16K 31/0655] Lift valves -- F16K 31/0655 covers lift valves, a type of operating means for controlling fluid flow in mechanical systems. These valves raise or lower a movable closure element, typically a disk or gate, to open or shut a passage, allowing precise regulation of pressure and volume in pipelines, hydraulic circuits, and similar applications. The classification falls under Mechanical Engineering → Engineering Elements and Units → Valves.
[F16K 31/0658] Armature and valve member being one single element -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[F16K 31/0662] with a ball-shaped valve member -- F16K 31/0662 covers valves that incorporate a ball-shaped valve member as the moving part that controls flow. The ball rotates within a seat to open or close the passage, typical in ball valves used in pipelines and fluid systems. This entry falls under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, valves, operating means.
[F16K 31/0665] with valve member being at least partially ball-shaped  F16K31/0662  takes precedence -- This subclass covers valves in which the moving, flow-controlling element is at least partially spherical, such as ball-type valve members. It pertains to operating means for regulating fluid or gas passage and refines the broader category of valves with ball-shaped components.
[F16K 31/0668] Sliding valves -- Sliding valves are linearly moving devices that control fluid flow by translating across a seat to open or close a passage, often used in pumps, compressors and engine systems. In the CPC they fall under class F16K for valve elements, specifically the subgroup 31/0668 that covers mechanisms where the valve element slides rather than rotates. This type of valve is typical in mechanical engineering applications requiring precise regulation of liquids or gases.
[F16K 31/0672] the valve member being a diaphragm -- F16K 31/0672 relates to valves whose operating member is a flexible diaphragm that separates inlet and outlet chambers and transmits pressure changes to control flow. It is situated in the mechanical-engineering hierarchy under engineering elements and units, valves, operating means, and is used in EPC and USPTO patent classifications.
[F16K 31/0675] Electromagnet aspects, e.g. electric supply therefor -- F16K 31/0675 pertains to valves' operating means that incorporate electromagnets, covering features such as the electric supply and related magnetic components used to control valve actuation. It falls under Mechanical Engineering → Engineering Elements and Units → Valves → Operating Means in the Cooperative Patent Classification.
[F16K 31/0679] with more than one energising coil -- F16K 31/0679 covers valves that incorporate more than one energising coil, typically solenoid or electromagnetic devices where separate coils control different ports, functions or stages of operation, enabling independent or combined actuation. These multi-coil operating means are used to regulate fluid flow in mechanical engineering systems.
[F16K 31/0682] with an articulated or pivot armature -- F16K 31/0682 refers to valves in which the operating means incorporates an articulated or pivot armature that moves relative to the valve body to open or close the flow passage. The armature pivots about a point or axis, translating external actuation into valve movement and is used in various control and shut-off applications.
[F16K 31/0686] Braking, pressure equilibration, shock absorbing -- F16K 31/0686 pertains to operating means for valves that provide braking, pressure equilibration, or shock-absorbing functions, such as devices that control fluid flow by resisting motion, equalising pressure spikes, or dampening sudden impacts.
[F16K 31/0689] Braking of the valve element -- F16K 31/0689 covers devices that apply a braking or holding action to a valve element to control its movement, typically used in hydraulic or pneumatic systems to prevent undesired opening or closing. The classification falls under operating means for valves within the mechanical engineering section of the CPC.
[F16K 31/0693] Pressure equilibration of the armature -- F16K 31/0693 concerns mechanisms that provide pressure equalisation to a valve armature, balancing forces during actuation to improve response and positioning reliability. It includes ducts, chambers or compensating elements that control armature pressure and prevent over-pressure in operating means for valves.
[F16K 31/0696] Shock absorbing, e.g. using a dash-pot -- This class deals with shock-absorbing devices used in valve operating mechanisms, such as dash-pots that damp rapid movements to protect components from impact and wear. It falls under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, valves, operating means.
[F16K 31/08] using a permanent magnet -- F16K 31/08 pertains to valve operating mechanisms that employ a permanent magnet as the driving or controlling element. The classification covers designs where the magnetic field directly actuates the valve stem, seat or other moving parts, enabling contactless or energy-efficient operation.
[F16K 31/082] using a electromagnet and a permanent magnet -- F16K 31/082 pertains to valves whose actuating mechanism combines an electromagnet with a permanent magnet to control opening and closing. The arrangement uses the permanent magnet to retain the valve in a default position while the electromagnet supplies the force needed for movement when energized. This hybrid magnetic actuation is applied in various mechanical engineering valve systems.
[F16K 31/084] the magnet being used only as a holding element to maintain the valve in a specific position, e.g. check valves  F16K31/082 ,  F16K31/086  take precedence -- F16K 31/084 covers valves in which a magnet is employed solely as a holding element to keep the valve member in a predetermined position, such as retaining a check valve closed, without providing the actuation force itself. The magnetic device only maintains the valve's state until other mechanisms open or release it. This subclass follows the related provisions F16K 31/082 and F16K 31/086.
[F16K 31/086] the magnet being movable and actuating a second magnet connected to the closing element -- The code F16K 31/086 covers valve actuation systems in which a movable magnet is used to drive a second magnet that is mechanically linked to the closing element, providing a non-contact means of opening or closing the valve. This design is classified under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, and valves, specifically operating means.
[F16K 31/088] the movement of the first magnet being a rotating or pivoting movement -- F16K 31/088 relates to valve operating mechanisms in which the primary magnet is displaced by a rotating or pivoting motion to open or close the valve. It covers designs where the magnetic element's angular movement, rather than linear travel, provides the actuation for the valve function.
[F16K 31/10] with additional mechanism between armature and closure member -- F16K 31/10 covers valves in which an extra mechanism, such as a linkage, cam or gear, is placed between the armature that moves the valve and the closure member that seals the flow path. This additional device transmits or modifies the motion, allowing finer control, increased force, or a different movement pattern than a direct armature-closure connection.
[F16K 31/105] for rotating valves -- F16K 31/105 designates rotating-valve mechanisms, covering valves that open and close by a rotary motion of the valve body or its internal components. Typical applications include rotary spool, plate, or disc valves used in hydraulic, pneumatic and internal-combustion systems where a turning action provides flow control.
[F16K 31/12] actuated by fluid -- F16K 31/12 concerns valve operating means that are powered by fluid pressure. The class includes hydraulic or pneumatic actuators such as pistons, diaphragms or fluid-driven cylinders that move the valve member to open, close or modulate flow.
[F16K 31/122] the fluid acting on a piston -- The subclass F16K 31/122 covers the fluid that acts on a piston in hydraulic or pneumatic devices, describing the characteristics, pressure, flow and volume of the liquid or gas that transmits force to the piston. It relates to operating means for valves that control such fluid-driven motion.
[F16K 31/1221] one side of the piston being spring-loaded -- F16K 31/1221 covers valve operating mechanisms in which a piston is loaded on one side by a spring, so that the spring force assists or opposes piston movement to open or close the valve. This arrangement is used in hydraulic or pneumatic systems for controlled pressure-dependent actuation.
[F16K 31/1223] one side of the piston being acted upon by the circulating fluid -- This subclass covers piston arrangements where only one side of the piston is exposed to pressure from the working fluid, while the opposite side is sealed or subjected to a different pressure source, commonly used in hydraulic or pneumatic actuators. It includes designs where the fluid acts on a single face to generate motion or force, with the other side potentially linked to a spring, accumulator, or external load.
[F16K 31/1225] with a plurality of pistons -- This subclass covers valve mechanisms that incorporate a plurality of pistons to control fluid flow, pressure, or timing. It includes designs where multiple pistons operate within a single valve body, often to provide staged opening, increased force, or synchronized action for improved performance.
[F16K 31/1226] the fluid circulating through the piston -- F16K 31/1226 relates to the fluid that circulates through a piston, covering the passages, channels or lubrication systems that deliver hydraulic or cooling fluid inside the piston. It includes arrangements for managing fluid flow to reduce friction, provide cooling, or assist in actuation within piston-driven mechanisms.
[F16K 31/1228] with a stationary piston -- F16K 31/1228 covers valves whose operating mechanism incorporates a stationary piston that interacts with fluid to control flow. The piston remains fixed while a movable element, such as a diaphragm or slide, presses against it to open or close the valve. This subclass is used for designs where the piston provides a reliable sealing surface in hydraulic or pneumatic control devices.
[F16K 31/124] servo actuated -- F16K 31/124 pertains to operating means for valves that employ a servo-actuated system. It covers valves whose position or flow is controlled by a servo mechanism, using feedback to provide precise, automated adjustment of valve opening or closing. This subclass falls under Mechanical Engineering → Engineering Elements and Units → Valves → Operating Means.
[F16K 31/1245] with more than one valve -- F16K 31/1245 pertains to operating means that incorporate more than one valve, describing devices where multiple valves cooperate to control flow, pressure or direction of fluids within mechanical systems.
[F16K 31/126] the fluid acting on a diaphragm, bellows, or the like -- The F16K 31/126 subclass covers valve operating mechanisms in which the fluid pressure is applied directly to a flexible element such as a diaphragm, bellows or similar component. This fluid-driven actuation moves the valve member, providing control without mechanical linkages.
[F16K 31/1262] one side of the diaphragm being spring loaded -- The subclass F16K 31/1262 covers valve operating mechanisms in which a diaphragm is pressed on one side by a spring, providing a bias that assists opening or closing of the valve. It falls under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, valves, and operating means.
[F16K 31/1264] with means to allow the side on which the springs are positioned to be altered -- F16K 31/1264 covers valve designs in which the spring placement can be switched from one side of the valve to the opposite side, allowing the valve to be adapted for different pressure directions or flow configurations without redesigning the entire assembly.
[F16K 31/1266] one side of the diaphragm being acted upon by the circulating fluid -- The entry covers diaphragm-type valves in which the circulating fluid exerts pressure on only one side of the diaphragm to cause it to flex and control flow. It includes designs where the fluid pressure on that single side serves as the actuating force for opening or closing the valve.
[F16K 31/1268] with a plurality of the diaphragms -- F16K 31/1268 designates valves whose operating means incorporate two or more diaphragms to control flow, often used to improve sealing, pressure compensation, or safety functions. The multiple diaphragms may be arranged in a series-or-parallel configuration within a single valve body, allowing independent or coordinated movement for precise regulation.
[F16K 31/128] servo actuated -- Servo-actuated valves use a servo motor or similar actuator to drive the valve stem, providing precise electronic control of opening and closing positions. This method enables fine regulation of flow, rapid response, and integration with feedback systems for automated process control. It is commonly applied in hydraulic, pneumatic, and fluid-handling applications where accurate valve positioning is essential.
[F16K 31/14] for mounting on, or in combination with, hand-actuated valves -- This subgroup covers devices or arrangements that are mounted on hand-actuated valves or combined with them to provide support, connection, or functional enhancement. It includes brackets, housings and other accessories that facilitate installation, sealing or operation of manually operated valve mechanisms.
[F16K 31/143] the fluid acting on a piston -- F16K 31/143 deals with the fluid that acts on a piston in valve operating mechanisms. It covers the use of hydraulic, pneumatic or other fluid media to move pistons that control valve opening or closing, including the pressure supply and flow arrangement.
[F16K 31/145] the fluid acting on a diaphragm -- F16K 31/145 covers the use of a fluid-liquid or gas-to act on a diaphragm that forms part of a valve's operating mechanism. It includes the flow paths, pressure conditions and fluid properties that cause the diaphragm to deflect and open or close the valve.
[F16K 31/16] with a mechanism, other than pulling-or pushing-rod, between fluid motor and closure member -- F16K 31/16 covers valve designs where a fluid-driven actuator is linked to the valve's closure element by a transmission mechanism other than a direct pulling or pushing rod, such as gears, cams, levers or hydraulic couplings. This arrangement allows the motion to be altered, redirected or amplified before moving the valve seat.
[F16K 31/163] the fluid acting on a piston -- F16K 31/163 relates to devices in which a fluid exerts pressure on a piston to produce motion, typical of hydraulic or pneumatic cylinders and similar actuators. The entry covers arrangements of the fluid supply, sealing, and piston design that enable conversion of fluid power into linear movement.
[F16K 31/1635] for rotating valves -- This CPC subgroup covers rotating valve designs where the valve element rotates relative to the body to control fluid flow, such as rotary ball, disc, or plug valves. It includes the mechanisms for actuation, sealing and positioning of these rotating valves.
[F16K 31/165] the fluid acting on a diaphragm -- F16K 31/165 covers valve operating mechanisms that use fluid pressure acting on a flexible diaphragm to open, close or modulate the valve. The classification applies to designs where the diaphragm transmits the fluid's force to a moving element, enabling control of flow in hydraulic, pneumatic or similar systems.
[F16K 31/1655] for rotating valves -- The F16K 31/1655 entry covers rotating valve designs, including rotary, piston and other valves that turn or swing to control flow in hydraulic, pneumatic or fluid systems. It focuses on the operating mechanisms that enable the valve to rotate, rather than on static or linear valve types. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, valves, and operating means.
[F16K 31/18] actuated by a float -- The entry covers valve operating mechanisms that use a float to sense fluid level and move the valve, common in water supply, irrigation, and wastewater applications. It includes designs where the float is directly linked to a valve stem or lever to open or close the flow path.

[F16K 31/20] actuating a lift valve -- The classification F16K 31/20 concerns mechanisms that operate a lift valve, a valve type in which a disc or gate is raised or lowered to start or stop flow. It includes hydraulic, pneumatic, electric or manual actuators and the control arrangements that move the valve element.
[F16K 31/22] with the float rigidly connected to the valve -- F16K 31/22 designates valves in which the float is rigidly connected to the valve, so the float moves together with the valve seat or stem rather than independently. This arrangement is typical for float-type control or relief valves where a fixed float provides a simple means of regulating flow or pressure.
[F16K 31/24] with a transmission with parts linked together from a single float to a single valve -- This class includes valve operating mechanisms in which a single float actuates a transmission that mechanically links multiple components to operate one valve, using levers, gears, rods or similar linkages to convert the float's motion into coordinated valve movement.
[F16K 31/26] with the valve guided for rectilinear movement and the float attached to a pivoted arm -- This subgroup covers valves whose moving element is constrained to straight-line motion while the float that detects fluid level is linked to a pivoted arm, enabling the arm's rotation to open or close the valve for level-controlled flow.
[F16K 31/265] with a second lever or toggle between the pivoted arm and the valve -- F16K 31/265 refers to valves whose operation includes a second lever or toggle placed between the pivoted arm that moves the valve stem and the valve itself, providing an intermediate control element for adjusting valve position. This falls under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, valves, and operating means.
[F16K 31/28] with two ore more floats actuating one valve -- In the Cooperative Patent Classification, code F16K 31/28 is placed under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, valves and operating means, and it covers valve mechanisms where two or more floats are linked to actuate a single valve, a design used for coordinated level or flow control in fluid-handling systems.
[F16K 31/30] actuating a gate valve or sliding valve -- The F16K 31/30 subclass covers devices and mechanisms that provide the force or motion needed to open or close a gate valve or sliding valve, including hydraulic, pneumatic, electric, or manual actuators. It is part of the operating means group for valves in the mechanical engineering hierarchy.
[F16K 31/32] actuating a tap or cock -- The F16K 31/32 subclass of the Cooperative Patent Classification deals with devices and methods for actuating a tap or cock, i.e., the mechanisms that open, close or regulate a valve. It is nested under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, valves, and operating means.
[F16K 31/34] acting on pilot valve controlling the cut-off apparatus -- The subclass concerns devices or methods that act on the pilot valve that governs a cut-off apparatus, adjusting or actuating the pilot to start, stop or modulate fluid flow in hydraulic or pneumatic systems. It includes mechanisms that modify the pilot valve's position or response to control the main valve's cut-off function.
[F16K 31/36] in which fluid from the circuit is constantly supplied to the fluid motor -- F16K 31/36 covers hydraulic or pneumatic arrangements in which the working fluid is continuously supplied from the circuit to a fluid motor, providing uninterrupted drive power. It includes designs that maintain a steady-flow or constant-pressure supply line to the motor and related control features that keep the motor energized at all times.
[F16K 31/363] the fluid acting on a piston -- F16K 31/363 covers valve-operating arrangements in which a piston is driven by the fluid that contacts it, using the fluid's pressure to open, close or modulate the valve. The classification includes hydraulic or pneumatic piston actuators that convert fluid force into the valve's motion.
[F16K 31/365] the fluid acting on a diaphragm -- F16K 31/365 relates to valve operating means where a fluid pressure is applied to a diaphragm to control valve movement. It covers designs in which the fluid acts directly on a flexible diaphragm to open or close the valve, typically used in pneumatic or hydraulic actuators.
[F16K 31/38] in which the fluid works directly on both sides of the fluid motor, one side being connected by means of a restricted passage and the motor being actuated by operating a discharge from that side -- F16K 31/38 relates to valve assemblies in which a fluid motor is acted on by fluid on both sides, one side being fed through a restricted passage while the motor is driven by discharging fluid from that side. This configuration provides direct control of motion and pressure within the valve's operating means. It falls under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, and valves.
[F16K 31/383] the fluid acting on a piston -- F16K 31/383 classifies valve operating means where the action of a fluid on a piston is the primary driving principle, covering hydraulic or pneumatic pistons that translate fluid pressure into movement for opening, closing or modulation of the valve.
[F16K 31/3835] the discharge being effected through the piston and being blockable by a mechanically-actuated member making contact with the piston -- This subclass pertains to valve mechanisms in which the fluid discharge occurs through a moving piston and can be stopped by a mechanically-driven element that makes direct contact with the piston to block the flow.
[F16K 31/385] the fluid acting on a diaphragm -- This subgroup concerns valves whose operating means rely on a fluid that directly contacts a flexible diaphragm, using the fluid's pressure to move the diaphragm and thereby actuate the valve. The fluid may be gas, liquid or vapor.
[F16K 31/3855] the discharge being effected through the diaphragm and being blockable by a mechanically-actuated member making contact with the diaphragm -- A valve design in which fluid discharge passes through a flexible diaphragm and is stopped by a mechanically-actuated member that makes contact with the diaphragm, sealing the flow path when engaged.
[F16K 3/14] with special arrangements for separating the sealing faces or for pressing them together -- F16K 3/14 covers gate-type or sliding valves that incorporate special mechanisms to separate the sealing faces or to press them together, enhancing the valve's sealing action during opening or closing. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, and valves.
[F16K 31/40] with electrically-actuated member in the discharge of the motor -- F16K 31/40 covers valves in which an electrically-actuated element is placed in the discharge side of a motor, typically to control the flow of fluid or gas generated by the motor's operation. It falls under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, valves, and operating means, describing devices that use electric power to open or close the discharge path of a motor-driven system.
[F16K 31/402] acting on a diaphragm -- F16K 31/402 covers devices that act on a diaphragm to control fluid flow, such as valves where the diaphragm is moved by pneumatic, hydraulic or mechanical means. It includes operating means that directly deform or shift the diaphragm to open or close the valve.
[F16K 31/404] the discharge being effected through the diaphragm and being blockable by an electrically-actuated member making contact with the diaphragm -- The subclass relates to valves in which the fluid is discharged through a diaphragm that can be sealed by an electrically-actuated component that makes contact with the diaphragm, providing a blockable discharge path within the operating-means category of valve technology.
[F16K 31/406] acting on a piston -- F16K 31/406 covers devices or mechanisms that exert force on a piston within a valve to control fluid flow, typically through hydraulic, pneumatic, or mechanical actuation. The classification includes various actuation methods such as springs, cam levers, or electric motors that move the piston to open or close the valve.
[F16K 31/408] the discharge being effected through the piston and being blockable by an electrically-actuated member making contact with the piston -- The code F16K 31/408 specifies valve arrangements in which fluid flow is discharged through a piston and can be stopped by an electrically-actuated element that makes direct contact with the piston. It falls under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, valves, and operating means.
[F16K 31/42] by means of electrically-actuated members in the supply or discharge conduits of the fluid motor -- F16K 31/42 covers fluid-motor valve arrangements where flow is controlled by electrically actuated components placed in the supply or discharge conduits. It is a subcategory of operating means for valves within mechanical engineering.
[F16K 31/423] the actuated members consisting of multiple way valves -- F16K 31/423 covers operating means that comprise actuated members formed by multiple-way valves, describing devices where a single actuator controls several flow paths within a valve assembly. This subgroup falls under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, and specifically valves, addressing complex valve actuation mechanisms.
[F16K 31/426] the actuated valves being cylindrical sliding valves -- F16K 31/426 covers actuated cylindrical sliding valves, a type of valve where the moving element slides within a cylindrical housing and is operated by a mechanical or hydraulic actuator. These valves are used in fluid power systems to control flow, pressure, or direction.
[F16K 31/44] Mechanical actuating means -- F16K 31/44 covers mechanical actuating means for valves, including levers, cams, gears, screw drives and other purely mechanical devices that provide the movement required to open, close or adjust a valve without the use of hydraulic, pneumatic or electrical power. It falls under the CPC hierarchy of Mechanical Engineering → Engineering Elements and Units → Valves → Operating Means.
[F16K 31/445] with exterior sleeve -- The subclass F16K 31/445 covers valves that incorporate an exterior sleeve surrounding the valve body or moving part. The sleeve may provide additional sealing, reinforcement, or protection against wear and corrosion while the valve operates. This feature is applied to various types of operating-means valves within mechanical engineering.
[F16K 31/46] for remote operation -- F16K 31/46 covers valve operating mechanisms that are actuated from a distance, including remote-control devices, pneumatic, hydraulic or electric actuators, and control systems that enable opening, closing or throttling of valves without manual intervention at the valve site. It pertains to the remote operation of various valve types.
[F16K 31/465] by flexible transmission means, e.g. cable, chain, bowden wire -- F16K 31/465 covers valve operating mechanisms that use flexible transmission elements such as cables, chains or Bowden wires to convey motion from the actuator to the valve stem.
[F16K 31/48] actuated by mechanical timing-device, e.g. with dash-pot -- F16K 31/48 covers valves whose operating means are driven by a mechanical timing device, for example a dash-pot or other timed actuator that controls opening and closing cycles.
[F16K 31/485] and specially adapted for gas valves -- The code F16K 31/485 classifies operating mechanisms that are specially adapted for gas valves, covering designs that modify standard actuation devices to suit the pressure, sealing, or safety requirements of gas flow control. It includes features such as insulated components, spark-proof actuation, and adjustments for high-temperature or corrosive gas environments.
[F16K 31/50] with screw-spindle -- F16K 31/50 covers valves whose opening, closing or throttling is achieved by a screw-spindle mechanism, where rotation of a threaded spindle converts torque into linear motion to move the valve element. It is placed under Mechanical Engineering → Engineering Elements and Units → Valves → Operating means.
[F16K 31/502] actuating pivotable valve members -- The F16K 31/502 subclass covers devices that provide the actuation of pivotable valve members, such as levers, cams, linkages or other mechanical means that cause a valve to turn about a fixed axis for opening, closing or throttling flow. It pertains to the arrangement and operation of these actuating elements within valve systems.
[F16K 31/504] the actuating means being rotable, rising, and having internal threads which co-operate with threads on the outside of the valve body -- The F16K 31/504 subgroup concerns valve actuators that rotate and rise while possessing internal threads that engage with matching threads on the exterior of the valve body. It specifies devices where the motion is transferred by a threaded coupling between the moving actuator and the stationary valve housing.
[F16K 31/506] with plural sets of thread, e.g. with different pitch -- F16K 31/506 covers valve operating mechanisms that incorporate more than one set of threads, such as assemblies using threads of different pitches to achieve varied motion or torque characteristics. This subclass addresses designs where multiple thread geometries are employed to control valve actuation, sealing, or adjustment functions.
[F16K 31/508] the actuating element being rotatable, non-rising, and driving a non-rotatable axially-sliding element -- This CPC subclass concerns valve operating devices in which the actuator rotates without moving axially and transmits its motion to a fixed component that slides axially, such as a rotary cam driving a valve stem. It describes mechanical arrangements where a rotatable, non-rising element drives a non-rotatable, axially-moving part.
[F16K 31/52] with crank, eccentric, or cam -- F16K 31/52 covers valves that are operated by a crank, eccentric or cam mechanism. It is a subclass of operating means for valves within the mechanical engineering section of the CPC.
[F16K 31/521] comprising a pivoted disc or flap -- It covers valve operating mechanisms that incorporate a pivoted disc or flap as the flow-control element, allowing the disc to rotate or hinge to open or close a passage. The classification is used for patents describing simple, movable valve elements that regulate fluid flow by pivoting.
[F16K 31/522] comprising a tap or cock -- The CPC subclass F16K 31/522 falls under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, valves, and operating means, and covers valve-operating mechanisms that incorporate a tap or cock as part of the device. It specifically classifies arrangements where the control element itself includes a tap or cock for regulating flow.
[F16K 31/523] comprising a sliding valve -- F16K 31/523 is a subclass in the mechanical-engineering group for valves that covers devices whose operating means include a sliding valve, a component that moves linearly to open or close a flow passage and thereby regulate fluid flow.
[F16K 31/524] with a cam -- The F16K 31/524 subgroup pertains to valve operating means that employ a cam as the actuation element. It includes designs where a cam profile converts rotary motion into the linear or angular movement needed to open, close or modulate a valve, covering the cam-driven mechanisms and their specific configurations.
[F16K 31/52408] comprising a lift valve -- The CPC subclass F16K 31/52408 relates to hydraulic or pneumatic systems whose operating means include a lift valve, a component that regulates fluid flow by moving a valve element up or down to control pressure, displacement or speed.
[F16K 31/52416] comprising a multiple-way lift valve -- F16K 31/52416 refers to a multiple-way lift valve, a type of operating means for controlling fluid flow in mechanical systems. They fall under the broader categories of valves within engineering elements.
[F16K 31/52425] with a ball-shaped valve member -- F16K 31/52425 covers valves whose operating means includes a ball-shaped valve member. It classifies ball valves used in mechanical engineering, describing the configuration where a spherical element controls fluid flow within the valve body.
[F16K 31/52433] with a streamlined or helically shaped valve member, e.g. for reducing flow losses or guiding the fluid flow -- The CPC subgroup F16K 31/52433 covers valves that incorporate a streamlined or helically shaped valve member intended to reduce flow losses and guide fluid movement more efficiently, falling under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, valves, operating means.
[F16K 31/52441] with a pivoted disc or flap -- F16K 31/52441 designates valves whose operating means employ a pivoted disc or flap that swings about a fixed axis to open or close a flow path. [F16K 31/5245] with a valve member of conical shape -- F16K 31/5245 pertains to valves that use a conically shaped member as the moving closing element. [F16K 31/52458] comprising a tap or cock -- The classification F16K 31/52458 covers valve operating means that include a tap or cock, i.e., a manually operated plug, lever or similar element used to open or close the flow passage. It is a sub-group within mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, valves, operating means.

[F16K 31/52475] comprising a sliding valve -- The CPC subclass F16K 31/52475 falls under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, valves and operating means, and covers inventions that incorporate a sliding valve as a functional element. [F16K 31/52483] comprising a multiple-way sliding valve -- F16K 31/52483 falls within the Mechanical Engineering section, under Engineering Elements and Units for Valves, specifically Operating Means. [F16K 31/52491] comprising a diaphragm cut-off apparatus -- The code F16K 31/52491 refers to valves that incorporate a diaphragm cut-off apparatus, where a flexible diaphragm is employed to block or allow fluid flow in response to control signals. It falls under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, valves and operating means.
[F16K 31/528] with pin and slot -- A valve operating mechanism that uses a pin inserted into a corresponding slot to transmit motion or position, allowing precise control of the valve's opening and closing. [F16K 31/5282] comprising a pivoted disc or flap -- The code designates valves whose operating means consist of a pivoted disc or flap that swings about a pivot point to open or close the flow passage. It covers check, shut-off and safety valves that regulate fluid by the rotation or swing of a disc or flap.
[F16K 31/5284] comprising a tap or cock -- The CPC code F16K 31/5284 falls under Mechanical Engineering → Engineering Elements and Units → Valves → Operating means and designates valve assemblies that incorporate a tap or cock as part of the operating mechanism, enabling flow control or shut-off within the valve system.
[F16K 31/5286] comprising a sliding valve -- F16K 31/5286 pertains to valves that employ a sliding element which moves linearly to open or close the flow path, commonly found in hydraulic or pneumatic systems. The sliding valve may be guided by rails or seals to ensure efficient sealing and controlled motion.
[F16K 31/5288] comprising a diaphragm cut-off apparatus -- The code F16K 31/5288 defines valves that include a diaphragm cut-off apparatus, employing a flexible diaphragm to seal or release flow when the device is actuated. This operating means provides isolation or shut-off without moving metal parts, often used in hydraulic or pneumatic systems for safety or precise control.
[F16K 31/53] with toothed gearing -- A valve whose operating mechanism incorporates toothed gearing to transmit motion or torque, enabling precise control of opening and closing actions. The gear arrangement is part of the valve's moving parts and may be used to adjust flow rate, pressure or timing in mechanical systems. This falls under the CPC class F16K 31/53 for valves with toothed gearing.
[F16K 31/535] for rotating valves  F16K31/54  takes precedence -- F16K 31/535 classifies rotating valve devices that operate by turning a shaft or spindle to open or close the flow path, such as rotary plug, butterfly or disk valves. It applies to designs where the valve body itself rotates to control flow and it takes precedence over the broader F16K31/54 category.
[F16K 31/54] with pinion and rack -- This group covers valve-operating arrangements in which a rotating pinion gear engages a linear rack to convert rotary motion into the linear displacement of the valve plug, stem or disc, providing precise opening and closing in hydraulic, pneumatic or other fluid-power systems.
[F16K 31/56] without stable intermediate position, e.g. with snap action -- This subclass covers valves that operate without a stable intermediate position, such as snap-action devices that move directly between fully closed and fully open states. It includes mechanisms that use a latch, stored energy or a rapid release to achieve an instantaneous transition, with no resting position in between.
[F16K 31/563] for rotating or pivoting valves -- This subclass covers operating means specifically designed to drive rotating or pivoting valves, such as actuators, gears, cams, linkages or other mechanisms that convert motion to turn or swing valve elements in fluid control systems.
[F16K 31/566] using a bistable spring device arranged symmetrically around the actuating stem -- The classification covers valve operating mechanisms that employ a bistable spring device positioned symmetrically around the actuating stem, allowing the valve to snap between two stable positions without continuous power. This arrangement provides rapid, reliable switching and maintains the stem in either the open or closed state with minimal friction.
[F16K 31/58] comprising a movable discharge-nozzle -- F16K 31/58 covers valves that incorporate a movable discharge-nozzle, enabling the outlet of fluid or gas to be repositioned during operation. The class applies to mechanical-engineering valve assemblies where the nozzle can be shifted to control flow direction or distribution.
[F16K 3/16] with special arrangements for separating the sealing faces or for pressing them together -- F16K 3/16 covers gate or sliding valves that include special mechanisms for separating the sealing faces or for pressing them together, such as actuators, springs, or hydraulic devices that control the opening and closing force.
[F16K 31/60] Handles -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Form, features and function of taps or faucets handles -> E03C1/04
[F16K 31/602] Pivoting levers, e.g. single-sided  F16K31/605  takes precedence -- F16K 31/602 in the Cooperative Patent Classification pertains to valves with operating means that include pivoting levers, such as those actuated on one side; however, for more specific cases, classification F16K 31/605 should be used instead.
[F16K 31/605] for single handle mixing valves -- F16K 31/605 covers single-handle mixing valves, devices that combine hot and cold water streams into a single output using one control lever. These valves belong to the operating means subgroup of valve elements in mechanical engineering, enabling temperature regulation in plumbing and hydraulic systems.
[F16K 31/607] characterised by particular material, by special measures to obtain aesthetical effects, or by auxiliary functions, e.g. storage -- F16K 31/607 covers valves whose distinguishing features are the use of specific materials, design elements that create aesthetic effects, or added auxiliary functions such as built-in storage, falling under the operating-means subgroup of valve technology.
[F16K 31/62] Pedals or like operating members, e.g. actuated by knee or hip -- F16K 31/62 classifies pedal-type or similar operating members used to control valves, where the actuation is performed by a user's leg, knee or hip. It falls under the hierarchy Mechanical Engineering → Engineering Elements and Units → Valves → Operating means, and addresses designs that translate lower-body motion into valve opening or closing.
[F16K 3/18] by movement of the closure members -- This subgroup covers gate or sliding valves whose closing element is actuated by linear displacement of the closure member along the seat, such as pistons, stems or wedges that slide to seat or unseat the valve. It excludes designs where the motion is rotational.
[F16K 3/182] by means of toggle links -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[F16K 3/184] by means of cams -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[F16K 3/186] by means of cams of wedge from -- The Cooperative Patent Classification entry F16K 3/186 pertains to gate valves or sliding valves that utilize a closing mechanism actuated by means of cams or wedges, specifically detailing the method of operation for these mechanical components in controlling fluid flow.
[F16K 3/188] by means of hydraulic forces -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The closure members are pushed towards the seats by hydraulic forces.  Illustrative example:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Fluid actuation of the valve -> F16K31/12
[F16K 3/20] by movement of the seats -- F16K 3/20 covers gate and sliding valves in which the seat moves to open or close the flow path, rather than the closing member. It includes cut-off apparatus where a sliding seat engages the sealing surface to regulate fluid flow.
[F16K 3/202] by movement of toggle links -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[F16K 3/205] by means of cams -- F16K 3/205 covers gate or sliding valves that are operated through cam mechanisms. The cam converts rotational motion into the linear sliding movement of the valve's closing member along the seat, providing precise opening and closing control. This subclass is part of the mechanical engineering hierarchy for valve actuation.
[F16K 3/207] by means of hydraulic forces -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The seat members are pushed towards valve members by hydraulic forces.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Fluid actuation of the valve -> F16K31/12

[F16K 3/24] with cylindrical valve members -- F16K 3/24 classifies gate or sliding valves that use cylindrical moving members which slide along the seat to open and close the flow path. It resides in the mechanical engineering hierarchy under engineering elements and units, specifically the valve group for cut-off apparatus.
[F16K 3/243] Packings  F16K3/246  takes precedence -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.

[F16K 3/30] Details -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Constructional details of the valve member, guiding and attachment to the spindle, lubrication and other details of special arrangements for influencing the flow.
[F16K 33/00] Floats for actuation of valves or other apparatus -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Construction of the float per se.
[F16K 3/312] Line blinds -- Line blinds are a type of gate or sliding valve that provides a shut-off function by moving a closing element across a seat to isolate a pipe section. They are used where a compact, often manually-operated, isolation device is required, typically in pipelines and process plants. The classification F16K 3/312 covers such line blind designs within mechanical engineering.
[F16K 3/314] Forms or constructions of slides -- F16K 3/314 relates to the specific designs and constructions of the sliding elements used in gate or sliding valves, detailing the shape, material, guiding features and mounting arrangements that enable the closing member to move along its seat. It includes variations in slide geometry and sealing means intended to improve valve operation and reliability.
[F16K 3/316] Guiding of the slide -- F16K 3/316 covers the guiding arrangements for the sliding member of gate or sliding valves, including rails, guides, bushings and other devices that keep the slide correctly aligned with its seat during opening and closing. The subclass addresses the design and positioning of these guiding elements to ensure smooth linear motion and reliable sealing.
[F16K 3/3165] with rollers or balls -- This subclass covers gate or sliding cut-off valves whose moving closing member is guided or supported by rollers or spherical balls to lower friction and improve sealing performance. It falls under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, and the valves group.
[F16K 3/32] Means for additional adjustment of the rate of flow -- F16K 3/32 covers devices that provide a secondary or fine-tuning means for controlling the flow rate through gate or sliding (cut-off) valves. Examples include adjustable stems, auxiliary ports, trim inserts or other mechanisms that modify the valve opening geometry to vary the discharge without altering the primary sliding movement.
[F16K 3/34] Arrangements for modifying the way in which the rate of flow varies during the actuation of the valve -- The code covers designs that alter the flow characteristic as a gate or sliding valve moves, such as progressive opening, variable-orifice, differential-pressure devices or hydraulic damping that shape the flow curve during opening or closing.
[F16K 3/36] Features relating to lubrication -- F16K 3/36 pertains to lubrication features of gate or sliding valves, covering oil or grease delivery systems, lubricating channels, bearing arrangements and other means that ensure smooth sliding movement of the closing member along the valve seat.
[F16K 35/00] Means to prevent accidental or unauthorised actuation -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Locking or interlocking the valve actuating device. Not the lock per se.
[F16K 35/02] to be locked or disconnected by means of a pushing or pulling action -- F16K 35/02 covers valve safeguarding devices that are engaged or released by a simple push or pull motion, providing a lock or disconnect function to stop unintended actuation. The subclass includes mechanisms where a linear manual action directly secures the valve stem or separates it from its control system.
[F16K 35/022] the locking mechanism being actuated by a separate actuating element -- F16K 35/022 covers valve locking mechanisms that are triggered by an independent actuating component rather than the valve's own movement, providing a means to avoid accidental or unauthorised operation. This sub-class falls under Mechanical Engineering → Engineering Elements and Units → Valves → Means to prevent accidental or unauthorised actuation.
[F16K 35/025] said actuating element being operated manually (e.g. a push-button located in the valve actuator) -- F16K 35/025 covers valve actuating elements that are operated manually, such as a push-button incorporated in the valve actuator, and are designed to prevent accidental or unauthorized actuation of the valve. The classification applies to mechanical means that require intentional human input to open or close the valve.
[F16K 35/027] the locking mechanism being actuated by pushing or pulling the valve actuator, the valve actuator being rotated subsequently to bring the valve closure element in the desired position -- This subclass covers valve locking mechanisms that are engaged by a push or pull of the valve actuator and then secured by rotating the actuator to hold the valve closure element in the desired position, preventing unintended or unauthorised operation.
[F16K 35/04] yieldingly resisting the actuation -- The code F16K 35/04 denotes valve mechanisms that employ a yielding resistance to stop accidental or unauthorised actuation, such as springs or flexible elements that give way only when a preset force is exceeded. This class falls under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, and specifically valves. It covers designs where the resisting element deforms or yields to prevent unintended movement while still allowing intended operation.
[F16K 35/06] using a removable actuating or locking member, e.g. a key -- F16K 35/06 is a CPC subclass for valves that incorporate a removable actuating or locking element, such as a key, to stop accidental or unauthorized operation. The removable member interposes between the valve stem and its drive, providing a simple mechanical safeguard. It falls under Mechanical Engineering → Engineering Elements and Units → Valves.
[F16K 35/08] requiring setting according to a code, e.g. permutation locks -- F16K 35/08 pertains to valve actuation systems that require a predetermined code or permutation lock to be set before the valve can be operated, providing a safeguard against accidental or unauthorized opening. The classification covers mechanisms that use coded settings to control valve movement and enhance security.
[F16K 35/10] with locking caps or locking bars -- F16K 35/10 designates valves equipped with locking caps or locking bars that block the operating mechanism to prevent accidental or unauthorized actuation. These safety features are used in a range of fluid control applications to ensure the valve remains closed or locked in position until deliberately released.
[F16K 35/12] with sealing wire -- In the CPC, class F16K includes valves designed to prevent accidental or unauthorised actuation. Subgroup 35/12 specifies valves that incorporate a sealing wire to maintain closure and prevent unintended opening. The sealing wire provides a mechanical lock, ensuring the valve stays sealed until deliberately released.
[F16K 35/14] interlocking two or more valves -- F16K 35/14 covers systems that link two or more valves so that the operation of one is dependent on the position of the others, creating an interlock that prevents accidental or unauthorized actuation. Such arrangements are used in safety-critical fluid circuits to ensure proper sequencing and to block unintended flow.
[F16K 35/16] with locking member actuated by magnet -- F16K 35/16 covers valves that include a locking member which is released or activated by a magnetic device, providing a means to prevent accidental or unauthorized actuation. The classification is used for mechanical engineering valve designs where magnetic actuation controls the lock.
[F16K 37/00] Special means in or on valves or other cut-off apparatus for indicating or recording operation thereof, or for enabling an alarm to be given -- F16K 37/00 covers devices incorporated in valves or other shut-off apparatus that provide a means to indicate or record when the valve operates, or to generate an alarm signal. The classification includes mechanical or electronic indicators, sensors, counters and alarm-triggering mechanisms installed on or within the valve assembly. It is used for monitoring valve status and alerting operators to act.
[F16K 37/0008] Mechanical means  F16K37/0075  takes precedence -- F16K 37/0008 pertains to mechanical means for indicating or recording the operation of valves or cut-off apparatus, with specific emphasis on mechanisms that take precedence over others in functionality. This classification is part of a broader category dealing with special means enabling alarms or operational notifications in valve systems.
[F16K 37/0016] having a graduated scale -- This subclass refers to valves or cut-off devices equipped with a graduated scale that shows position, flow or pressure, allowing the operator to read or record the valve's setting. The scale may be integral to the valve stem, body or an external indicator and is used for monitoring or alarm triggering.
[F16K 37/0025] Electrical or magnetic means  F16K37/0075  takes precedence -- F16K 37/0025 covers electrical or magnetic devices incorporated in valves or other shut-off apparatus to indicate or record their operation or to trigger an alarm. It falls under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units for valves, and is superseded by the more specific F16K 37/0075.
[F16K 37/0033] using a permanent magnet, e.g. in combination with a reed relays -- This subclass deals with valve or cut-off devices that incorporate a permanent magnet, often combined with reed relays, to detect position or flow and generate a signal for monitoring, recording or alarm purposes.
[F16K 37/0041] for measuring valve parameters  F16K37/0033  takes precedence -- F16K 37/0041 covers devices incorporated in or attached to valves or other shut-off apparatus that measure valve parameters, providing indications, recordings or alarm signals of operation. These specialized means enable real-time monitoring of flow, pressure, position or opening degree, and are subordinate to the broader F16K 37/0033 entry which takes precedence.
[F16K 37/005] for measuring fluid parameters  F16K37/0033  takes precedence -- This subclass covers valve or cut-off devices equipped with features that measure fluid parameters such as pressure, temperature, flow or composition and provide indication, recording or alarm functions. It falls under the CPC group on special means on valves for indicating operation and supersedes the broader subclass F16K37/0033.
[F16K 37/0058] Optical means, e.g. light transmission, observation ports  F16K37/0075  takes precedence -- F16K 37/0058 covers optical devices such as light-transmitting elements or observation ports integrated into valves or cut-off apparatus to indicate or record their operation or to trigger an alarm. This subclass is subordinate to F16K 37/0075, which has priority.
[F16K 37/0066] Hydraulic or pneumatic means  F16K37/0075  takes precedence -- F16K 37/0066 pertains to hydraulic or pneumatic means for indicating, recording, or alarming valve operations, with F16K 37/0075 taking precedence in classification. This code is part of the Mechanical Engineering section, focusing on specialized valve functionalities.
[F16K 37/0075] For recording or indicating the functioning of a valve in combination with test equipment -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Above ground control means for valves in boreholes or wells -> E21B34/16 - Testing static or dynamic balance of machines or structures; Investigate fluid-tightness (leaks) -> G01M3/00 - Testing or monitoring control systems or parts; Electric testing -> G05B23/02
[F16K 37/0083] by measuring valve parameters -- This subclass covers devices or arrangements that determine valve status or trigger alarms by measuring valve parameters such as pressure, flow, position, temperature or force. It includes sensors, transducers and related electronics that convert the measured quantity into a signal for indication, recording or warning purposes.
[F16K 37/0091] by measuring fluid parameters -- F16K 37/0091 covers valve-or cut-off-apparatus devices that sense operation by measuring fluid parameters such as pressure, temperature, flow rate or composition, and use the data to indicate, record or trigger an alarm. It includes integrated sensors or transducers that monitor the fluid inside the valve to provide real-time status information. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, valves.
[F16K 39/00] Devices for relieving the pressure on the sealing faces -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[F16K 39/02] for lift valves -- This subclass addresses lift-type valves that relieve pressure on the sealing faces of mechanical valve assemblies, where the valve element is raised away from the sealing surface to allow pressure release. It includes various designs and mechanisms for actuating the lift movement.
[F16K 39/022] using balancing surfaces -- This subclass covers valve arrangements that incorporate balancing surfaces to offset the hydraulic or pneumatic forces acting on sealing faces, thereby reducing load, wear, and leakage. The balancing surfaces may be placed opposite the sealing face or linked to the valve body to equalize pressure. It is used in devices that relieve pressure on sealing faces.
[F16K 39/024] using an auxiliary valve on the main valve -- F16K 39/024 covers configurations where a secondary or auxiliary valve is incorporated in a primary valve assembly to relieve pressure on the sealing faces. The auxiliary valve opens before or alongside the main valve to reduce load, improve sealing integrity, or enable controlled venting during operation. This arrangement is used in hydraulic or pneumatic systems to enhance safety and performance.
[F16K 39/026] using an external auxiliary valve -- F16K 39/026 covers devices that use an external auxiliary valve to relieve pressure on the sealing faces of a primary valve. The auxiliary valve is located outside the main valve assembly and operates to reduce pressure differentials, improving sealing performance.
[F16K 39/028] with pivoted closure members, e.g. butterfly valves -- This subclass covers valves that relieve pressure on sealing faces by using pivoted closure members such as butterfly valves. The closure element rotates about a pivot to open or close the flow path, providing a compact, quick-acting pressure-relief solution.
[F16K 39/04] for sliding valves -- F16K 39/04 refers to sliding valves designed to relieve pressure on sealing faces, typically used in mechanisms where a movable sealing element slides to open or close a passage and allow pressure reduction across the seal. This sub-group falls under devices for relieving pressure on sealing faces within the broader valve classification.
[F16K 39/045] of rotating or pivoting type -- The code F16K 39/045 covers valve devices that relieve pressure on sealing faces by using a rotating or pivoting mechanism. Such designs employ a movable element that turns or pivots to open a pressure-relief passage, reducing load on the seal and improving safety in mechanical-engineering applications.
[F16K 39/06] for taps or cocks -- This subclass covers devices that relieve pressure on the sealing faces of taps or cocks, such as pressure-relief mechanisms integrated into valve stems. It falls under CPC group F16K 39 for valves and pressure-relief elements within mechanical engineering.
[F16K 41/00] Spindle sealings -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[F16K 41/003] by fluid -- **Definition:** This place covers:  It refers to sealings by fluid in general, not only liquid.
[F16K 41/006] by establishing an under-pressure -- This subgroup concerns valve designs where the sealing of the spindle is achieved by creating a partial vacuum or negative pressure on one side of the seal. By maintaining under-pressure, leakage past the spindle is reduced, enhancing sealing performance in various valve types. Applicable to hydraulic, pneumatic, or liquid-handling valves.
[F16K 41/02] with stuffing-box -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Sealings with stuffing-box OR sealing ring.
[F16K 41/023] for spindles which only rotate, i.e. non-rising spindles  F16K41/043 ,  F16K41/063  and  F16K41/083  take precedence -- F16K 41/023 in the Cooperative Patent Classification covers sealing arrangements for rotating spindles that do not rise, specifically addressing designs where other more specific classifications such as F16K41/043, F16K41/063, and F16K41/083 are not applicable.
[F16K 41/026] for rotating valves -- F16K 41/026 covers sealing solutions for rotating valves, where a valve element or spindle turns relative to a stationary housing. The subclass includes designs, materials and arrangements that prevent fluid leakage during rotation, such as lip seals, cartridge seals and other rotary sealing devices.
[F16K 41/04] with at least one ring of rubber or like material between spindle and housing -- F16K 41/04 relates to spindle sealings that incorporate at least one rubber or elastomeric ring placed between the spindle and its housing to create a seal. The design is used in valves and other mechanical assemblies to prevent fluid leakage while allowing rotational movement.
[F16K 41/043] for spindles which only rotate, i.e. non-rising spindles -- The subclass F16K 41/043 pertains to sealing arrangements designed for spindles that only rotate and do not move axially, such as static seals for shafts in pumps, compressors, or machine-tool spindles where axial travel is absent. It falls under mechanical-engineering elements related to valves and spindle sealings.
[F16K 41/046] for rotating valves -- F16K 41/046 covers rotating valves that incorporate spindle sealing arrangements to prevent leakage between moving and stationary parts. It is applied to devices such as rotary throttles, angular or rotary control valves where the sealing element follows the spindle's motion.
[F16K 41/06] with at least one ring attached to both spindle and housing -- This subclass defines spindle seals in which at least one sealing ring is mechanically attached to both the rotating spindle and the stationary housing, creating a continuous sealing element during operation. It applies to valve arrangements where the ring is integral to both components, improving durability and preventing leakage.
[F16K 41/063] for spindles which only rotate, i.e. non-rising spindles -- The code F16K 41/063 designates seal arrangements intended for spindles that only rotate and do not translate axially, known as non-rising spindles. It covers sealing methods, materials and constructions used to prevent leakage in devices such as rotary pumps, compressors and similar mechanical equipment where the spindle motion is purely rotational.
[F16K 41/066] for rotating valves -- F16K 41/066 covers sealing arrangements for the spindles or stems of rotating valves, ensuring that fluids or gases do not leak along the valve's rotating shaft. It includes designs of packing, glands, lip seals and other sealing elements that accommodate rotational movement while maintaining a pressure-tight seal.
[F16K 41/08] with at least one ring provided with axially-protruding peripheral closing-lip -- F16K 41/08 defines spindle sealing arrangements that incorporate at least one sealing ring equipped with a peripheral lip that projects axially to close the clearance around the rotating shaft. The protruding lip provides an enhanced barrier against fluid leakage in valve-type spindle assemblies.
[F16K 41/083] for spindles which only rotate, i.e. non-rising spindles -- F16K 41/083 covers sealing solutions for spindles that rotate continuously without axial movement, such as non-rising spindles. The class pertains to valve components within mechanical engineering, specifically sealing mechanisms that prevent fluid leakage while allowing rotation.
[F16K 41/086] for rotating valves -- F16K 41/086 is a CPC subgroup that defines sealing solutions applied to rotating valves, particularly spindle sealings that prevent fluid or gas leakage along valve shafts while allowing the valve to turn. The classification groups designs of seals, seal rings or seal assemblies integrated with or attached to rotating valve stems.
[F16K 41/10] with diaphragm, e.g. shaped as bellows or tube -- This subclass covers valve spindle sealing arrangements that incorporate a flexible diaphragm, such as bellows or tubular elements, to provide sealing between a moving spindle and its housing. The diaphragm compensates for wear and pressure variations, improving reliability.
[F16K 41/103] the diaphragm and the closure member being integrated in one member -- The CPC entry F16K 41/103 covers valve spindle sealing arrangements in which the diaphragm and the closure member are formed as a single integrated component. This design merges the flexible sealing element with the moving closure, reducing part count and simplifying assembly while maintaining effective sealing performance.
[F16K 41/106] for use with rotating spindles or valves  F16K41/125  takes precedence -- This CPC subclass (F16K 41/106) relates to sealing solutions designed for rotating spindles or valve stems, typically used to prevent fluid leakage in mechanical valve assemblies. It is overridden by the more specific subclass F16K41/125, which provides a finer definition.
[F16K 41/12] with approximately flat diaphragm -- F16K 41/12 relates to spindle sealings that employ an approximately flat diaphragm as the sealing element, providing a barrier between a rotating shaft and the surrounding fluid or pressure environment. This arrangement is used in mechanical-engineering valve assemblies to improve sealing reliability while accommodating axial movement of the spindle.
[F16K 41/125] the part of the spindle traversing the diaphragm being rotatable or pivotable -- In spindle sealing arrangements, the element that passes through the sealing diaphragm and can rotate or pivot is covered by subclass F16K 41/125. It refers to the rotating or swinging portion of the spindle that penetrates the flexible diaphragm, enabling motion while maintaining pressure containment.
[F16K 41/14] with conical flange on the spindle which co-operates with a conical surface in the housing -- This subclass covers spindle sealing arrangements where the spindle carries a conical flange that mates with a matching conical surface on the housing, providing a sealing interface. The design ensures pressure-tight sealing while allowing axial movement of the spindle. It falls under CPC F16K 41/14 within valve spindle sealings.
[F16K 41/16] with a flange on the spindle which rests on a sealing ring -- Spindle sealing in which a flange formed on the rotating spindle presses against a stationary sealing ring, creating a contact line that prevents fluid or gas leakage along the shaft. The flange transfers load to the ring and is common in pumps, gearboxes and hydraulic machines.
[F16K 41/18] sealing only when the closure member is in the opened position -- F16K 41/18 covers spindle sealing designs where the seal engages only while the valve closure element is in the opened position, preventing leakage when the valve is open but allowing free movement when closed.
[F16K 43/00] Auxiliary closure means in valves, which in case of repair, e.g. rewashering, of the valve, can take over the function of the normal closure means -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[F16K 43/001] an auxiliary valve being actuated independently of the main valve -- F16K 43/001 relates to auxiliary closure devices for valves that can assume the sealing function of the main valve, especially during repair actions such as re-washering, and are operated independently of the primary valve. This subclass falls under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, and valves.
[F16K 43/003] the auxiliary valve being a rotary valve -- F16K 43/003 covers rotary auxiliary valves that can replace the main closure member of a valve during maintenance or repair, such as re-washering. The auxiliary valve provides the same sealing function as the primary valve but is installed temporarily to allow work on the main valve without interrupting service. It belongs to the mechanical engineering class for valve elements and units.
[F16K 43/005] an auxiliary valve closing automatically when the main valve is being disassembled -- The code F16K 43/005 covers auxiliary valve-closing devices that automatically engage when the main valve is taken apart for repair or re-washering, temporarily assuming the normal shut-off function. It applies to mechanisms that ensure continued closure of the flow path during disassembly of the primary valve.
[F16K 43/006] the auxiliary valve being held open by the main valve -- F16K 43/006 covers auxiliary closure devices for valves that are kept open by the main valve, allowing the auxiliary component to assume the sealing function during maintenance such as re-washering. The auxiliary valve releases its closure role when the primary valve closes, ensuring continuous shut-off capability.
[F16K 43/008] the main valve having a back-seat position, e.g. to service the spindle sealing -- F16K 43/008 covers main valves that are placed in a back-seat position to serve as auxiliary closure means during repair operations such as rewashering, enabling the spindle sealing to be serviced while still providing a sealing function.
[F16K 47/00] Means in valves for absorbing fluid energy -- Group F16K47/00 is impacted by reclassification into groups F16K47/01 , F16K47/011 , F16K47/0111 , F16K47/0112 and F16K47/012 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[F16K 47/01] Damping of valve members -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Feature that slows, cushions movement or reduces oscillation of a valve member.
[F16K 47/011] by means of a dashpot -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Valves provided with a dashpot to prevent or reduce chattering or hunting of the valve member or to dampen the opening and/or closing of the valve member.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[F16K 47/0111] the valve members comprising a plunger sliding within a fixed dashpot -- **Definition:** This place covers:  A movable plunger (comprising the valve member) slides within a fixed dashpot.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[F16K 47/0112] the valve members comprising a dashpot sliding over a fixed plunger -- **Definition:** This place covers:  A movable dashpot (comprising the valve member) slides over a fixed plunger.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[F16K 47/012] by means of a resilient damping element -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Valves provided with a resilient damping means to prevent or reduce chattering or hunting of the valve or to dampen the opening and/or closing of the valve member.  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this group.
[F16K 47/02] for preventing water-hammer or noise -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Valves with arrangements for the specific purpose of reducing water-hammer or noise.
[F16K 47/023] for preventing water-hammer, e.g. damping of the valve movement -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Damping for the specific purpose of reducing water-hammer.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[F16K 47/026] preventing noise in a single handle mixing valve -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Arrangements for preventing noise in single handle mixing valves.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[F16K 47/04] for decreasing pressure , the throttle being incorporated in the closure member -- The CPC subclass F16K 47/04 covers valve arrangements in which a throttling restriction is built into the moving closure element itself to reduce pressure as fluid passes. It relates to mechanical-engineering elements that absorb fluid energy by integrating the throttle in the valve's shut-off member.
[F16K 47/045] and the closure member being rotatable -- F16K 47/045 covers valve constructions that use a rotatable closure element-such as a rotating disc, plug or gate-to absorb fluid energy. The rotating member dissipates kinetic energy of the fluid, providing damping or flow-control functions within hydraulic, pneumatic or steam valves.
[F16K 47/06] with a throttle in the form of a helical channel -- The subclass F16K 47/06 covers valve arrangements that absorb fluid energy by incorporating a throttle formed as a helical channel within the flow path. Such designs use the spiral geometry to create a controlled pressure drop, providing damping or flow regulation in mechanical valve systems.
[F16K 47/08] for decreasing pressure and having a throttling member separate from the closure member -- F16K 47/08 relates to valves that reduce pressure by using a throttling component that is separate from the main closure member. Such devices allow controlled pressure drop while the shut-off function is performed by a different element.
[F16K 47/10] in which the medium in one direction must flow through the throttling channel, and in the other direction may flow through a much wider channel parallel to the throttling channel -- F16K 47/10 covers valves that provide a throttling passage for fluid flow in one direction while allowing the same fluid to return in the opposite direction through a substantially larger, parallel channel. The design uses the dual-path arrangement to create a pressure drop when forward flow occurs but offers low resistance on the reverse flow.
[F16K 47/12] the throttling channel being of helical form -- F16K 47/12 relates to valves that incorporate a helical throttling channel as the means for absorbing fluid energy. The helical passage forces the fluid to follow a spiral path, creating a pressure drop and dissipating energy within the valve.
[F16K 47/14] the throttling member being a perforated membrane -- The subclass F16K 47/14 covers valves that employ a perforated membrane as the throttling member to regulate fluid flow and absorb fluid energy. It pertains to designs where the membrane creates a controlled pressure drop, providing resistance and energy dissipation within the valve.
[F16K 47/16] the throttling member being a cone -- F16K 47/16 covers valve designs in which the throttling element that dissipates fluid energy is shaped as a cone. It falls under mechanical engineering, engineering elements and units, valves, and means in valves for absorbing fluid energy. The classification is used by the EPO and USPTO for patents involving conical throttling members.
[F16K 49/00] Means in or on valves for heating or cooling -- F16K 49/00 covers devices incorporated into or attached to valves that provide heating or cooling functions, such as heaters, thermostats, or cooling jackets, to control the temperature of the fluid or the valve itself. These arrangements are used to prevent freezing, maintain fluid properties, or improve valve performance in various mechanical engineering applications.
[F16K 49/002] Electric heating means -- F16K 49/002 covers electric heating devices that are integrated into or attached to valves for heating or cooling fluids. It includes resistive heating elements, heating coils or other electrically powered means placed in or on valves to raise the temperature of the fluid or the valve body.
[F16K 49/005] Circulation means for a separate heat transfer fluid -- F16K 49/005 covers components that pump or circulate a separate heat-transfer fluid through or around a valve to provide heating or cooling, such as built-in circulators, fluid channels or auxiliary pumps designed to regulate valve temperature and prevent freezing or overheating.
[F16K 49/007] located within the obturating element -- F16K 49/007 designates features that are positioned inside the obturating element of a valve used for heating or cooling, such as integrated springs, actuators or sealing components built within the valve plug, disc or closure. It covers any means that form part of the moving or sealing portion itself rather than being attached externally.

This design belongs to the valve subgroup dealing with purely rotary shut-off mechanisms within mechanical engineering.

The plug remains stationary while a surrounding sleeve rotates to align or block the flow passage, providing shut-off without axial movement.

[F16K 7/00] Diaphragm cut-off apparatus, e.g. with a member deformed, but not moved bodily, to close the passage -- F16K 7/00 relates to diaphragm cut-off apparatus in which a flexible member is deformed to close a flow passage while remaining essentially stationary. Such devices are used in hydraulic or pneumatic valves to achieve rapid sealing without moving the whole valve body.
[F16K 7/02] with tubular diaphragm -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.

[F16K 7/06] by means of a screw-spindle, cam, or other mechanical means -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[F16K 7/061] Screw clamps -- A screw clamp is a mechanical device that secures a deformed diaphragm in a cut-off valve, locking the passage closed without moving the diaphragm bodily. It is used in diaphragm-type shut-off apparatus to provide a reliable, adjustable seal for fluid control.
[F16K 7/063] Lever clamps -- F16K 7/063 covers lever-operated clamps that secure a deformable member in diaphragm cut-off devices, enabling the passage to be sealed without the member moving bodily. The clamps apply force via a lever mechanism to press the diaphragm into a closed position, forming part of valve assemblies in mechanical engineering.
[F16K 7/065] Cam clamps -- Cam clamps are devices that employ a cam mechanism to generate clamping pressure, securing a diaphragm or valve element in a cut-off apparatus so that the member is deformed to seal a passage without being moved bodily. They are used to control fluid flow by providing a reliable, adjustable closure within mechanical valve systems.
[F16K 7/066] Wedge clamps -- Wedge clamps are valve components that use a deformable element to press a diaphragm against a seat, sealing a passage without moving the element as a whole. They are employed in diaphragm cut-off apparatus where the closure is achieved by deformation rather than translation.
