[F17C 2209/234] of closing end pieces, e.g. caps -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Details in relation with the assembling of end pieces.  In this positive example from WO03031860, the mounting steps of closing end pieces is illustrated.  In this positive example from US2011253727, the form of the edge of the end piece is detailed.
[F17C 2209/236] Apparatus therefore -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatus related to the assembling of end pieces and to the inserting of valves.  In this positive example from EP0780191, the focus is on the mounting apparatus, not on the vessel.
[F17C 2209/238] Filling of insulants -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Details in relation with the filling of insulant into a wall, e.g. insertion and compression of perlite.  In this positive example from WO2010039198, Aerogel is added to an insulating layer.
[F17C 2221/00] Handled fluid, in particular type of fluid -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fluid stored in the pressure vessel or handled during charging or discharging into or from a pressure vessel.  In this positive example from WO2010039198, the fluid stored in the tank is hydrogen.
[F17C 2221/01] Pure fluids -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fluid represented by a single sort of molecules, or mixture containing at least one fluid represented by a single sort of molecules.
[F17C 2221/011] Oxygen -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fluid comprising oxygen (O 2 ).
[F17C 2221/012] Hydrogen -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fluid comprising hydrogen (H 2 ).
[F17C 2221/013] Carbon dioxide -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fluid comprising carbon dioxide (CO 2 ).
[F17C 2221/014] Nitrogen -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fluid comprising nitrogen (N 2 ).
[F17C 2221/015] Carbon monoxide -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fluid comprising carbon monoxide (CO).
[F17C 2221/016] Noble gases (Ar, Kr, Xe) -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fluid comprising mono-atomic fluids with very low chemical reactivity, like), neon (Ne), argon (Ar), krypton (Kr), xenon (Xe) or radon (Rn).
[F17C 2221/017] Helium -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fluid comprising Helium (He).
[F17C 2221/018] Acetylene -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fluid comprising acethylene (C 2 H 2 ).
[F17C 2221/03] Mixtures -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fluid represented by a mixture of molecules or mixture containing at least one fluid represented by a mixture of molecules.
[F17C 2221/031] Air -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fluid comprising air (4/5 nitrogen, 1/5 oxygen, argon and traces of carbon dioxide).
[F17C 2221/032] Hydrocarbons -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Hydrocarbon, composition of carbon and hydrogen atoms, of the type CmHn.
[F17C 2221/033] Methane, e.g. natural gas, CNG, LNG, GNL, GNC, PLNG -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Hydrocarbon entirely or essentially made of methane CH 4 , but generally comprising also ethane and higher hydrocarbons.
[F17C 2221/035] Propane butane, e.g. LPG, GPL -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Hydrocarbon entirely or essentially made of propane C 3 H 8 or butane C 4 H 10 , but generally comprising also some higher or lower hydrocarbons.
[F17C 2221/036] Hydrates -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fluid comprising hydrates, a mixture of hydrocarbon and water.
[F17C 2221/037] Containing pollutant, e.g. H -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fluid comprising toxic or pollutant substances, like chlorine.
[F17C 2221/038] Refrigerants -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fluid stored or transferred is a refrigerant.  In this positive example from EP1333223, Chlorodifluoromethane or ,1,1,2-Tetrafluoroethane is stored in the tank.  In this negative example from US2003159800, the refrigerant is not the fluid stored in the vessel, but in an attached refrigerating system.
[F17C 2221/05] Ultrapure fluid -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fluid cleaned of bacteria, particles. organic and inorganic substances, often used in the semiconductor manufacturing process.
[F17C 2221/07] Hyperpolarised gases -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Gas in an hyperpolarized state (ionised, having a negative potential).
[F17C 2221/08] Ergols, e.g. hydrazine -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Combustion fluid or propellant for rockets or space vehicles, e.g.N 2 H 4 .
[F17C 2223/00] Fluid contained in the vessel -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Particular characteristics of the fluid stored in the pressure vessel or before transfer into the pressure vessel.
[F17C 2223/01] characterised by the phase -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Phase of the fluid stored in the pressure vessel or before transfer from the pressure vessel.
[F17C 2223/0107] Single phase -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fluid stored in the pressure vessel or before transfer from the pressure vessel is in a single phase.
[F17C 2223/0115] dense or supercritical, i.e. at high pressure and high density -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The single phase of the fluid stored in the pressure vessel or before transfer from the pressure vessel is dense or supercritical, i.e. at a temperature or pressure above the critical point, where no distinction between liquid or gas exists.  In this positive example from US2003115889, natural gas is in dense phase.
[F17C 2223/0123] gaseous, e.g. CNG, GNC -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The single phase of the fluid stored in the pressure vessel or before transfer from the pressure vessel is gaseous.  In this positive example from US201007229, compressed hydrogen gas is stored.
[F17C 2223/013] liquid -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The single phase of the fluid stored in the pressure vessel or before transfer from the pressure vessel is liquid.
[F17C 2223/0138] solid -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The single phase of the fluid stored in the pressure vessel or before transfer from the pressure vessel is solid.  In this positive example from WO2011093786A1, a solid block of CO 2 is placed into the vessel during manufacturing.
[F17C 2223/0146] Two-phase -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fluid stored in the pressure vessel or before transfer from the pressure vessel is in a two-phase state.
[F17C 2223/0153] Liquefied gas, e.g. LPG, GPL -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The two-phase state is liquid and gaseous, and the fluid concerned can be liquefied at high pressure under normal temperatures, like butane or propane.  In this positive example from DE102004044259, liquefied propane is stored.
[F17C 2223/0161] cryogenic, e.g. LNG, GNL, PLNG -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The two-phase state is liquid and gaseous, and the fluid concerned is in liquid state at cryogenic temperature, like oxygen, hydrogen or natural gas.  In this positive example from WO2010039198, liquefied hydrogen is stored.
[F17C 2223/0169] subcooled -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The liquid and gaseous two-phase state is additionally sub-cooled, i.e. at a temperature lower than saturation temperature.  In this positive example from EP1353112, liquid CO 2 is subcooled.
[F17C 2223/0176] Solids and gas -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The two-phase state is solid and gaseous, like CO 2 dry ice.  In this positive example from WO9403288, solid carbon dioxide sublimates.
[F17C 2223/0184] Liquids and solids -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The two-phase state is solid and liquid, like hydrates.
[F17C 2223/0192] Three-phase, e.g. CO -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fluid stored in the pressure vessel or before transfer from the pressure vessel is in a three-phase state, i.e. at the triple point.  In this phase diagram extracted from Wikipedia, the triple point is visualised.
[F17C 2223/03] characterised by the pressure level -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Pressure of the fluid stored in the pressure vessel or before transfer from the pressure vessel.
[F17C 2223/031] Not under pressure, i.e. containing liquids or solids only -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The pressure is very low, around 1 bar absolute, or the vessel is not under pressure, open and in direct contact with the environment.  In this positive example from EP2103863, liquid oxygen is stored under lowest pressure.
[F17C 2223/033] Small pressure, e.g. for liquefied gas -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The pressure is low, from 1 to 10 bars absolute, or the pressure vessel contains liquefied gas and is submitted to its vapor pressure.
[F17C 2223/035] High pressure (>10 bar) -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The pressure is high, from 10 bars to 80 bars absolute.
[F17C 2223/036] Very high pressure (>80 bar) -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The pressure is very high, above 80 bars absolute.
[F17C 2223/038] Subatmospheric pressure -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The pressure is sub-atmospheric, e.g. when vacuum is intentionally maintained in the pressure vessel.
[F17C 2223/04] characterised by other properties of handled fluid before transfer -- The subclass F17C 2223/04 applies to vessels that store compressed, liquefied or solidified gases or liquids and are distinguished by particular properties of the fluid-such as temperature, purity, pressure range or phase-before it is transferred. It is used when those fluid characteristics, rather than the vessel's shape or material, define the technical features of the storage equipment.
[F17C 2223/041] Stratification -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fluid within one phase discloses different layers e.g. liquefied gas which is sub-cooled in the lower layers of the liquid phase, and at saturation temperature in the higher layers of the liquid phase.  In this positive example from WO9941164, stratification is obtained in a gaseous fluid.  In this positive example from JP59019800, liquefied gas stratifies in two layers.  In this negative example from WO0134235, a liquid phase is topped by the vapor phase. Each phase is homogeneous.
[F17C 2223/042] Localisation of the removal point -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fluid removal point with a particular location or position.  In this positive example from CH239535, the upper removal tube is bent upwards in a detailed drawing.  In this positive, more schematic example from WO0134235, a tube is prolonged inside the liquid in the vessel.
[F17C 2223/043] in the gas -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fluid removed is gas only or removal point is situated in the gas phase.  In this positive example from FR2607575 the removal tube's end is located on top of the vessel.
[F17C 2223/045] with a dip tube -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Gas is removed through a tube that extends into the vessel through the liquid phase.  In this positive example from CH239535, the upper tube reaches the gas phase through the liquid phase.
[F17C 2223/046] in the liquid -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fluid removed is liquid only or removal point is situated in the liquid phase.  In this positive example from DE102005002976, liquid carbon dioxide is removed from the bottom of the vessel.
[F17C 2223/047] with a dip tube -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Liquid is removed through a tube that extends into the vessel through the gas phase.  In this positive example from WO2007107509, liquefied natural gas is removed with a dip tube.
[F17C 2223/048] in the solid -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fluid removed is solid only or removal point is situated in the solid phase.
[F17C 2225/00] Handled fluid after transfer, i.e. state of fluid after transfer from the vessel -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Particular characteristics of the fluid after being transferred.  In this positive example from EP1846691, the handled fluid after transfer is gaseous.
[F17C 2225/01] characterised by the phase -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Phase of the fluid after being transferred.
[F17C 2225/0107] Single phase -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fluid after being transferred is in a single phase.
[F17C 2225/0115] dense or supercritical, i.e. at high pressure and high density -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The single phase of the fluid after being transferred is dense or supercritical, i.e. at a temperature or pressure above the critical point, where no distinction between liquid or gas exists.  In this positive example from US2003115889, natural gas is in dense phase.
[F17C 2225/0123] gaseous, e.g. CNG, GNC -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The single phase of the fluid after being transferred is gaseous.  In this positive example from CH209041, gas is transferred.
[F17C 2225/013] liquid -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The single phase of the fluid after being transferred is liquid.
[F17C 2225/0138] solid -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The single phase of the fluid after being transferred is solid.
[F17C 2225/0146] Two-phase -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fluid after being transferred is in a two-phase state.
[F17C 2225/0153] Liquefied gas, e.g. LPG, GPL -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The two-phase state is liquid and gaseous, and the fluid concerned can be liquefied at high pressure under normal temperatures, like butane or propane.  In this positive example from WO2005058684, liquefied gas is transferred without phase change.
[F17C 2225/0161] cryogenic, e.g. LNG, GNL, PLNG -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The two-phase state is liquid and gaseous, and the fluid is liquefied under cryogenic temperatures, like oxygen, hydrogen or natural gas.
[F17C 2225/0169] subcooled -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The two-phase state is liquefied gas, and the fluid phase is additionally sub-cooled, i.e. at a temperature lower than saturation temperature.
[F17C 2225/0176] Solids and gas -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The two-phase state is solid and gaseous, like CO 2 dry ice.
[F17C 2225/0184] Liquids and solids -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The two-phase state is solid and liquid, like hydrates.
[F17C 2225/0192] Three-phase, e.g. CO -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fluid after being transferred is in a three-phase state, i.e. at the triple point.
[F17C 2225/03] characterised by the pressure level -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Pressure of the fluid after being transferred.
[F17C 2225/031] Not under pressure, i.e. containing liquids or solids only -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The pressure is very low, around 1 bar absolute, or the vessel is not under pressure, open and in direct contact with the environment.
[F17C 2225/033] Small pressure, e.g. for liquefied gas -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The pressure is low, from 1 to 10 bars absolute, or the pressure vessel contains liquefied gas and is submitted to its vapor pressure.
[F17C 2225/035] High pressure, i.e. between 10 and 80 bars -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The pressure is high, from 10 bars to 80 bars absolute.
[F17C 2225/036] Very high pressure, i.e. above 80 bars -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The pressure is very high, above 80 bars absolute.
[F17C 2225/038] Subatmospheric pressure -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The pressure is sub-atmospheric, e.g. when vacuum is intentionally maintained in the pressure vessel.
[F17C 2225/04] characterised by other properties of handled fluid after transfer -- This subclass concerns vessels for storing compressed, liquefied or solidified gases where the defining feature is the condition of the fluid after it leaves the vessel, such as its temperature, pressure, phase or purity. It applies to systems that monitor, control or utilize these post-transfer properties for safety, efficiency or product quality.
[F17C 2225/041] Stratification -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fluid within one phase discloses different layers e.g. liquefied gas which is sub-cooled in the lower layers of the liquid phase, and at saturation temperature in the higher layers of the liquid phase.
[F17C 2225/042] Localisation of the filling point -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fluid filling point with a particular location or position.
[F17C 2225/043] in the gas -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fluid filling point is situated in the gas phase.  In this positive example from US2008141684, the filling tube's is located in the gas phase.
[F17C 2225/044] at several points, e.g. with a device for recondensing gas -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fluid is filled at several filling points, and condenses at the same time surrounding gas vapor.  In this positive example from WO2008099977, filling takes place at several points in the gas phase, where recondensation takes place.
[F17C 2225/045] with a dip tube -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Gas is filled through a tube that extends into the vessel through the liquid phase.  In this positive example from US2008141684, the upper tube reaches the gas phase through the liquid phase.
[F17C 2225/046] in the liquid -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fluid filling point is situated in the liquid phase.  In this positive example from US5404918 the filling tube's end is located on bottom of the vessel.
[F17C 2225/047] with a dip tube -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Liquid is filled through a tube that extends into the vessel through the gas phase.  In this positive example from FR2607575 the filling tube's end is located on the bottom of the vessel.
[F17C 2225/048] in the solid -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fluid filling point is situated in the solid phase.
[F17C 2227/00] Transfer of fluids, i.e. method or means for transferring the fluid -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or means for transferring fluid out of or into a pressure vessel. Method or means for achieving thermal exchange with fluid stored in or transferred out of or into a pressure vessel.
[F17C 2227/01] Propulsion of the fluid -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or means for transferring fluid out of or into a pressure vessel.  In this positive example from US2836963, the pressure vessel contains a piston.  In this negative example from BE471532, fluid is discharged after opening the valve.
[F17C 2227/0107] by pressurising the ullage -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods of transferring liquefied gas out of a pressure vessel by raising the pressure of the vapor phase (the ullage), so that the vapor phase pushes on the liquid surface. The pressure raise is often operated by vaporizing part of the liquefied gas with a heater.  In this positive example from US2007186925, a heating element evaporates liquefied gas in the vessel.  In this negative example from US2007017596, an external purge gas replaces the vapor phase. Though gas is added in the ullage, the purpose is not to specifically to discharge the vessel.
[F17C 2227/0114] with vacuum injectors, e.g. venturi -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods of transferring fluid out of or into a pressure vessel by using vacuum created by a vacuum injector, e.g. venturi type.  In this positive example from US2007261756, extraction takes place by a venturi pump.
[F17C 2227/0121] by gravity -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods of transferring fluid by gravity out of a pressure vessel, the weight of the fluid itself discharging the vessel.  In this positive example from EP1770326, an exit is simply mounted on the bottom of the vessel.
[F17C 2227/0128] with pumps or compressors -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods of transferring out of or into a pressure vessel fluid by using pumps or compressors.
[F17C 2227/0135] Pumps -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods of transferring liquid fluid out of or into a pressure vessel, by using pumps.  In this positive example from WO9709561, a pump is immersed in the vessel.
[F17C 2227/0142] with specified pump type, e.g. piston or impulsive type -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods of transferring liquid fluid out of or into a pressure vessel by using a pump, the type of which is specified, e.g. of piston type.  In this positive example from US3426545, a piston type pump is used.
[F17C 2227/015] with cooling of the pump -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods of transferring liquid fluid out of or into a pressure vessel using pumps, wherein the pump is cooled.
[F17C 2227/0157] Compressors -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods of transferring gas out of or into a pressure vessel using a compressor.  In this positive example from DE102009039645, a compressor compresses gas from the tank.
[F17C 2227/0164] with specified compressor type, e.g. piston or impulsive type -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods of transferring gas out of or into a pressure vessel using a compressor, the type of which being specified, e.g. piston type.  In this positive example from US2003021743, a high pressure piston compressor is used.
[F17C 2227/0171] Arrangement -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Special localization or configuration of pumps or compressors used to transfer fluid out of or into a pressure vessel.
[F17C 2227/0178] in the vessel -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Localization of pumps or compressors used to transfer fluid out of or into a pressure vessel inside a vessel.  In this positive example from FR2722760, the pump is immersed.
[F17C 2227/0185] comprising several pumps or compressors -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Configuration of pumps or compressors used to transfer fluid out of or into a pressure vessel, in which several pumps or compressors are used or usable.  In this positive example from US2006150640, three pumps transfer cryogen.
[F17C 2227/0192] by using a working fluid -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods of transferring fluid out of or into a pressure vessel using a working fluid. This working fluid replaces and pushes the fluid to be transferred. The working fluid may be in direct contact with the fluid to be transferred or separated by an element like a membrane or a piston. The working fluid is different from the transferred fluid.  In this positive example from DE29816811U, a piston separates the working fluid from the gas.  In this negative example from US2007186925, the vaporized fluid itself pressurises the ullage and pushes the liquefied gas.
[F17C 2227/03] Heat exchange with the fluid -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method or means for achieving thermal exchange with fluid transferred, the thermal exchange being heating or cooling, direct or indirect or via intermediate fluids, and taking place anywhere.  In this positive example from US20070186564, the part of the scheme related to the actual process gas is restricted to one heat exchanger 114.
[F17C 2227/0302] by heating -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method or means for heating fluid transferred out of or into a pressure vessel.  In this positive example from US2007186925, a heating element heats and evaporates liquefied gas in the vessel.
[F17C 2227/0304] using an electric heater -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method or means for heating fluid by an electrical heater.  In this positive example from DE102005049252, an electrical heater is mounted on a pipe inside the vessel.
[F17C 2227/0306] using the same fluid -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method or means for heating fluid by using the same fluid as the fluid transferred.  In this positive example from WO2005045337, the gaseous and the liquid phase of the fluid exchange heat in an external heat exchanger, the one being heated, the other cooled.
[F17C 2227/0309] using another fluid -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method or means for heating fluid, the heat being obtained from another fluid than the fluid transferred.  In this positive example from US4995234, liquefied carbon dioxide exchanges heat with the LNG.
[F17C 2227/0311] Air heating -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method or means for heating fluid , the heat being obtained from air.  In this positive example from US2007132116, air is used to vaporize LNG.
[F17C 2227/0313] by forced circulation, e.g. using a fan -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method or means for heating fluid, the heat being obtained from air in a forced circulation, e.g. by using a ventilator.  In this positive example from EP1724514, air is ventilated onto LNG.
[F17C 2227/0316] Water heating -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method or means for heating fluid, the heat being obtained from water.  In this positive example from US2006288711, water vaporizes the LNG.
[F17C 2227/0318] using seawater -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method or means for heating fluid, the heat being obtained from seawater.  In this positive example from US2006288711, seawater vaporizes the LNG.
[F17C 2227/032] using geothermal water -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method or means for heating fluid, the heat being obtained from geothermal water.  In this positive example from US2007079617, geothermal water vaporizes the LNG.
[F17C 2227/0323] in a closed loop -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method or means for heating fluid, the heat being obtained from another fluid that circulates in a closed loop. This other fluid is often an intermediate heat carrying fluid, like propane or an alcohol, chosen so as not to freeze in contact with the cryogen.  In this positive example from WO2009068731, an intermediate fluid is used to vaporize LNG.
[F17C 2227/0325] by expansion using "Joule-Thompson" effect -- F17C 2227/0325 covers methods or apparatus that transfer fluids by employing the Joule-Thomson expansion effect, using the temperature drop that occurs when a compressed gas expands through a restriction. It is applied in vessels for storing or distributing gases where cooling or pressure reduction is achieved as part of the fluid transfer process.
[F17C 2227/0327] with recovery of heat -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method or means for heating fluid, the heat being recovered for later reuse.  In this positive example from WO2007019946, LNG is vaporized, and the cold energy used to liquefy oxygen.
[F17C 2227/033] using solar energy -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method or means for heating fluid, the heat being generated by solar energy.
[F17C 2227/0332] by burning a combustible -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method or means for heating fluid, the heat being generated by burning a combustible.  In this positive example from WO2009032683, vaporizing of LNG occurs by using a submerged combustion vaporizer.
[F17C 2227/0334] by radiation means -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method or means for heating fluid, the heat being generated by radiation from e.g. chemical processes.
[F17C 2227/0337] by cooling -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method or means for cooling fluid transferred.
[F17C 2227/0339] using the same fluid -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method or means for cooling fluid, the cooling effect being obtained by a using the same fluid as the fluid transferred out of or into a pressure vessel.  In this positive example from WO2005045337, the gaseous and the liquid phase of the fluid exchange heat in an external heat exchanger, the one being heated, the other cooled.
[F17C 2227/0341] using another fluid -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method or means for cooling fluid, the cooling effect being obtained from another fluid than the fluid transferred out of or into a pressure.  In this positive example from EP1790904, hydrogen is cooled by nitrogen.
[F17C 2227/0344] Air cooling -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method or means for cooling fluid, the cooling effect being obtained from air.  In this positive example from US2005284154, hot nitrogen gas is cooled by air.
[F17C 2227/0346] by forced circulation, e.g. using a fan -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method or means for cooling fluid, the cooling effect being obtained from air in a forced circulation, e.g. by using a ventilator.  In this positive example from EP2110596, the hydrogen storage tank is cooled by a fan.
[F17C 2227/0348] Water cooling -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method or means for cooling fluid, the cooling effect being obtained from water  In this positive example from US2009114290, gaseous CO 2 is condensed und thus cooled by using water.
[F17C 2227/0351] using seawater -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method or means for cooling fluid, the cooling effect being obtained from seawater.  In this positive example from US2720082, seawater is used to cool the liquefied gas of the storage tanks.
[F17C 2227/0353] using cryocooler -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method or means for cooling fluid, the cooling effect being obtained from a cryocooler, which uses e.g. helium or another fluid to generate cryogenic temperature.  In this positive example from US2005204752, a helium cryocooler is disclosed.
[F17C 2227/0355] in a closed loop -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method or means for cooling fluid, the cooing effect being obtained from another fluid that circulates in a closed loop.  In this positive example from WO2005071333, LNG boil-off gas is cooled in a closed-loop refrigeration system.
[F17C 2227/0358] by expansion -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method or means for cooling fluid, the cooling effect being obtained from expansion of the fluid.
[F17C 2227/036] "Joule-Thompson" effect -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method or means for cooling fluid, the cooling effect being obtained from expansion of the fluid, the Joule-Thompson effect being mentioned or clearly implicit like when using a throttle.  Joule-Thompson formula:
[F17C 2227/0362] in a turbine -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method or means for cooling fluid, the cooling effect being obtained from expansion in a turbine.  In this positive example from WO2007028221, nitrogen is expanded in a turbine.
[F17C 2227/0365] with recovery of heat -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method or means for cooling fluid, the cooling potential being recovered for later reuse.
[F17C 2227/0367] Localisation of heat exchange -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Thermal exchange with the fluid, including heating and cooling, the thermal exchange taking place at particular locations.
[F17C 2227/0369] in or on a vessel -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Thermal exchange with the fluid, the exchange taking place in or on the pressure vessel.  In this positive example form EP1167862, the heating device is mounted in or on the vessel.
[F17C 2227/0372] in the gas -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Thermal exchange located in the gas phase of the fluid in the pressure vessel.  In this positive example from DE19720451, a heater is mounted in the gas phase.
[F17C 2227/0374] in the liquid -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Thermal exchange located in the liquid phase of the fluid in the pressure vessel.  In this positive example from DE19544593, a heating exchange device is located in the liquid phase.
[F17C 2227/0376] in wall contact -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Thermal exchange with the fluid in the pressure vessel, the thermal exchange device being in contact with the vessel wall.
[F17C 2227/0379] inside the vessel -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Thermal exchange with the fluid in the pressure vessel, the thermal exchange device being located inside the vessel.  In this positive example from EP2103863, the heat exchange device is distributed all over the spherical inner wall surface.
[F17C 2227/0381] integrated in the wall -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Thermal exchange with the fluid in the pressure vessel, the thermal exchange device being integrated in the wall of the vessel.  In this positive example from WO9612521, the heat exchange device is mounted inside the wall of the vessel.
[F17C 2227/0383] outside the vessel -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Thermal exchange with the fluid in the pressure vessel, the thermal exchange device not being in contact with the vessel.  In this positive example from US2002124575, the heat source is located below the vessel.
[F17C 2227/0386] with a jacket -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Thermal exchange with the fluid in the pressure vessel, the thermal exchange device being in form of a dismountable jacket.  In this positive example from FR2897413, the jacket covers the upper part of the vessel.
[F17C 2227/0388] separate -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Thermal exchange with the fluid, including heating or cooling, the thermal exchange taking place at a location remote from the pressure vessel.
[F17C 2227/039] on the pipes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Thermal exchange with the fluid, the thermal exchange taking place on pipes.  In this positive example from DE102005049252, the pipe is heated.
[F17C 2227/0393] using a vaporiser -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Thermal exchange with the fluid, the thermal exchange being vaporization and taking place in a vaporizer.  In this positive example from US6598408, LNG is vaporized in a vaporizer.  In this positive example from WO03012277, the vaporizer is a compact unit.
[F17C 2227/0395] using a submerged heat exchanger -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Thermal exchange with the fluid, the thermal exchange taking place in a submerged a heat exchanger.  In this positive example from US2006288711, seawater vaporizes the LNG.  In this negative example from WO2004081441, there is no heat exchanger, but direct heat exchange between seawater and vessel.
[F17C 2227/0397] characterised by fins -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Thermal exchange with the fluid, the thermal exchange process being supported by fins or rips.  In this positive example from US4598554, fins surround an vaporizer.
[F17C 2227/04] Methods for emptying or filling -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method for filling or emptying pressure vessels.  In this negative example from DE748096, a particular alignment of vessels is disclosed (arrangement in series). This does not allow a conclusion how the filling or emptying takes place.
[F17C 2227/041] vessel by vessel -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method for filling or emptying pressure vessels, the method comprising the filling or emptying of one vessel after another, instead of several vessels at the same time.  In this positive example from FR2874247, vessels are filled one by one.  In this negative example from US2006150640, salt caverns empty simultaneously.
[F17C 2227/042] with change-over from one vessel to another -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method for filling or emptying pressure vessels, the method comprising details about a switch-over from a first vessel or group of vessels, to a second vessel or group of vessels, once the filling or emptying of the first vessel or group of vessels is achieved.  In this positive example from FR2857728, a change-over from an emptied vessel to a spare vessel is described.
[F17C 2227/043] by pressure cascade -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method for filling a pressure vessel with gas by pressure cascade, i.e. from a group of pressure vessels at a different pressure levels each, the filling starting from the vessel at the lowest pressure and ending with the vessel at the highest pressure, so as to systematically obtain gas transfer by pressure difference, with the lowest pressure drop possible.  In this positive example from US2009229701, the filling is of cascade type.
[F17C 2227/044] by purging -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method for emptying a pressure vessel or pipes, that includes a step of purging, i.e. washing out residual gas by an inert gas  In this positive example from EP0916891, inert gas replaces residual gas.
[F17C 2227/045] by vacuum -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method for emptying a pressure vessel or pipes, that includes a step of evacuating at vacuum level, e.g. with a venturi or vacuum pump, so as to get rid of residual gas.  In this positive example from US2005204752, evacuation and inert gas purging are used in cycles.
[F17C 2227/046] by even emptying or filling -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method for filling or emptying pressure vessels, the method allowing to fill or empty several vessels in parallel.  In this positive example from WO03016765, liquefied gas vessels are evenly emptied.
[F17C 2227/047] by repeating a process cycle -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method for filling or emptying pressure vessels, the method comprising a step that is sequentially repeated in a process cycle until the filling or emptying is achieved.  In this positive example from DE10247505, the transfer takes sequentially place by small quantities.
[F17C 2227/048] by maintaining residual pressure -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Method for emptying a pressure vessel that includes the intentional remainder of a residual pressure in the vessel, which usually prevents the introduction of impurities into the vessel.  In this positive example from EP1400742, a residual pressure valve (RPV) is mounted on the vessel.
[F17C 2250/00] Accessories -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Means and accessories supporting the gas storage or the handling of gas in and out of a pressure vessel, or pressure vessels as such, in particular means to control, to indicate and to measure and monitor parameters like temperature, pressure etc...
[F17C 2250/01] Intermediate tanks -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Intermediate tank situated between a source storage tank and distribution means to a user, the intermediate tank allowing e.g. to individually process smaller buffer quantities of fluid from the storage tank fluid before distributing it.  In this positive example from EP1353112, the intermediate tank is used for the distribution of carbon dioxide  In this negative example from DE10021681, the fluid is directly processed from the storage tank.
[F17C 2250/03] Control means -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Means for controlling, the gas storage or handling in and out of a pressure vessel, or the pressure vessel.
[F17C 2250/032] using computers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Means for controlling, the means comprising a computer.  In this positive example from GB2440692, a PC is displayed.  In this positive example from US2002148502, the words CPU, ROM and RAM are visible.
[F17C 2250/034] using wireless transmissions -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Means for controlling, the means comprising a wireless transmission or data.  In this positive example from US2009050630, a networks manages fuel cell vehicles.
[F17C 2250/036] using alarms -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Means for controlling, the means triggering an alarm at predefined events. The alarm can be any of any type: visual, acoustic, by odor etc...The triggering event can be of any type as well.  In this positive example from WO9323117, an alarms system is mounted on a pipe.
[F17C 2250/038] using cameras -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Means for controlling, the means comprising a camera.  In this positive example from WO2011104736, an control camera is used.
[F17C 2250/04] Indicating or measuring of parameters as input values -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Means and accessories supporting the gas storage or handling in and out of a pressure vessel, or the pressure vessel, the means dedicated to indicate, measure and monitor parameters like temperature, pressure etc.
[F17C 2250/0404] Parameters indicated or measured -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Parameters indicated or measured by means supporting the gas storage and handling in and out of a pressure vessel.  The parameters do not need to be measured directly in or on the pressure vessel.
[F17C 2250/0408] Level of content in the vessel -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Indication or measurement of the level of content in the vessel, like e.g. by using transparent vessel wall, or a spyhole...
[F17C 2250/0413] with floats -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Indication or measurement of the level of content in the vessel, by using a float.  In this positive example from EP1318347, the float is mounted in a portable vessel.
[F17C 2250/0417] with electrical means -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Indication or measurement of the level of content in the vessel, by using an electrical device.  In this positive example from WO9940553, a sensor is mounted in the vessel.
[F17C 2250/0421] Mass or weight of the content of the vessel -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Indication or measurement of mass of fluid.  In this positive example from FR2865018, the whole vessel is weighted.
[F17C 2250/0426] Volume -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Indication or measurement of volume of fluid.
[F17C 2250/043] Pressure -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Indication or measurement of pressure, e.g. by a pressure gauge  In this positive example from WO9323117, the pressure is measured by a sensor.
[F17C 2250/0434] Pressure difference -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Indication or measurement of a pressure difference, i.e. the difference of pressure between two points of measure.  In this positive example from WO9118266, pressure is measured in the liquid and the gas.
[F17C 2250/0439] Temperature -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Indication or measurement of temperature.  In this positive example from US2002124575, a temperature sensor is shown.
[F17C 2250/0443] Flow or movement of content -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Indication or measurement of flow or movement of fluid.  In this positive example from US5333465, a flow meter is used.
[F17C 2250/0447] Composition -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Indication or measurement of composition.
[F17C 2250/0452] Concentration of a product -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Indication or measurement of a concentration within the fluid, like in % vol.
[F17C 2250/0456] Calorific or heating value -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Indication or measurement of calorific or heating value, like indicated by the Wobbe index.
[F17C 2250/046] Humidity -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Indication or measurement of moisture.  In this positive example from EP0271483, a moisture indicator is attached to the closing cap of the gas vessel.
[F17C 2250/0465] Vibrations, e.g. of acoustic type -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Indication or measurement of vibrations.  In this positive example from US2008104969, an acoustic sensors allows to monitor the fluid level.

[F17C 2250/0478] Position or presence -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Indication or measurement of the position or of the presence of items, like e.g. the correct attachment of a dispensing pistol.  In this positive example from US2010192695, the presence of vessels is monitored.
[F17C 2250/0482] Acceleration -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Indication or measurement of an acceleration.  In this positive example from US2009050630, an acceleration sensor is connected to a controller.
[F17C 2250/0486] Indicating or measuring characterised by the location -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Particular locations for measuring parameters.
[F17C 2250/0491] Parameters measured at or inside the vessel -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Particular location for measuring parameters, the location being at or inside the vessel.
[F17C 2250/0495] the indicated parameter is a converted measured parameter -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Particular situation when the measured parameter is converted into another parameter that is then displayed.
[F17C 2250/06] Controlling or regulating of parameters as output values -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Means and accessories supporting the gas storage or handling in and out of a pressure vessel, or a pressure vessel, in particular to regulate a parameter, in the sense of reaching and maintaining a set point.

[F17C 2250/0694] with calculations -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods for regulating for which the algorithm or formulae on which the regulation is based, are disclosed.
[F17C 2250/07] Actions triggered by measured parameters -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Particular actions triggered by parameters.
[F17C 2250/072] Action when predefined value is reached -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Particular actions triggered when a particular value of a parameter is reached, e.g. an alarms starts, a connection is established etc...
[F17C 2250/075] when full -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Particular actions triggered in the moment the pressure vessel is full.  In this positive example from EP1318347, the valve closes when the vessel is filled.
[F17C 2250/077] when empty -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Particular actions triggered in the moment the pressure vessel is empty.
[F17C 2260/00] Purposes of gas storage and gas handling -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Purposes in relation with gas storage and gas handling, that are intended to be achieved by the technical features disclosed.
[F17C 2260/01] Improving mechanical properties or manufacturing -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Purposes to be achieved by the technical features disclosed, these purposes being in relation with mechanical properties or with the manufacturing of pressure vessels
[F17C 2260/011] Improving strength -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Purpose consisting in improving strength, stability, mechanical resistance of the device.  In this positive example from US4925044, an additional layer allows to "improve the mechanical strength of the tank"" (in the text).
[F17C 2260/012] Reducing weight -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Purpose consisting in reducing the weight of the device.  In this positive example from US2280501, "It is an object ...to provide a... container...in which the ...weight ...is a minimum" (in the text).
[F17C 2260/013] Reducing manufacturing time or effort -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Purpose consisting in facilitating the manufacturing of the device so as to reduce the manufacturing time or effort.  In this positive example from EP1520683, the objective " shortening the manufacturing time" (in the text) is achieved.
[F17C 2260/015] Facilitating maintenance -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Purpose consisting in facilitating or simplifying the maintenance of the device.
[F17C 2260/016] Preventing slosh -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Purpose consisting in reducing or preventing slosh in the pressure vessel.  In this positive example from US2922287, baffles are mounted "to prevent sloshing" (in the text).  **Glossary:** - Slosh: Movement of a liquid, in particular of the free surface of a liquid inside a vessel undergoing motion, like on a ship or in a plane. The liquid, also in motion, can damage or alter the vessel.
[F17C 2260/017] by calculation -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Purposes achieved in relation with mechanical properties or with manufacturing by applying formulae or calculations.  In this positive example from GB1008317, a formula defines the wall thickness.  In this negative example from XP000486893, a chemical formula is disclosed.
[F17C 2260/018] Adapting dimensions -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Purpose consisting in being able to insert the pressure vessel in an restricted location with a specific geometry, like a car backseat, or a spare wheel compartment.  In this positive example from EP1584859, the vessel is mounted on a car, the shape is for a "better integration" (in the text).
[F17C 2260/02] Improving properties related to fluid or fluid transfer -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Purposes to be achieved by the technical features disclosed, these purposes being in relation with the fluid or the fluid transfer.
[F17C 2260/021] Avoiding over pressurising -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Purpose consisting in avoiding a too high pressure inside the pressure vessel.  In this positive example from GB1167296, a space for swelling is foreseen in the bottom "in the case of accidental overpressure" (in the text).
[F17C 2260/022] Avoiding overfilling -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Purpose consisting in avoiding overfilling of the pressure vessel.  In this positive example from EP1318347, a device foreseen "to prevent it from being filled beyond a given limit" (in the text).
[F17C 2260/023] Avoiding overheating -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Purpose consisting in avoiding overheating.  In this positive example from US20070000561, surplus heat is evacuated from the vessel "to avoid heat build up " (in the text).
[F17C 2260/024] Improving metering -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Purpose consisting in improving the metering, i.e. the transfer of a precise dosage of fluid.  In this positive example from US5131440, nitrogen is dispensed on food, with the requirement "to provide a predetermined volume of liquid gas" (in the text).
[F17C 2260/025] Reducing transfer time -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Purpose consisting in shortening duration for transferring fluid from or to a vessel, e.g. when refuelling vehicles with liquefied hydrogen.  In this positive example from EP1726869, a heat evacuation system is foreseen to reduce "the time required to refill onboard tanks" (in the text).
[F17C 2260/026] by calculation -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Purpose achieved in relation with the fluid or fluid transfer by applying formulae or calculations.  In this positive example from XP002302219, heat transfer is calculated  In this negative example from XP000486893, a chemical formula is disclosed.
[F17C 2260/027] Making transfer independent of vessel orientation -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Purpose consisting in making the fluid transfer independent from the changing orientation of the vessel, e.g. in planes or satellites.  In this positive example from DE19910894, liquefied gas can be discharged even if the vessel is upside down, thus overcoming the prior art disadvantage that liquid can only be discharged with a "particular orientation of the vessel" (in the text).  In this positive example from US5279323, traps are foreseen to withdraw liquefied gas so as to be "operable under low-gravity conditions" (in the text)
[F17C 2260/028] Avoiding unauthorised transfer -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Purpose consisting in making fluid transfer only possible for authorised persons.  In this positive example from WO03044426, a connector between a gas control valve and a gas meter is designed with the requirement of "preventing illegal use of public utility gas" (in the text).
[F17C 2260/03] Dealing with losses -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Purpose to be achieved by the technical features disclosed, the purpose being in relation with losses.
[F17C 2260/031] due to heat transfer -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Purpose in relation with losses due to heat transfer.  In this positive example from WO03018344, heat transfer into the tank "generally unwanted" (in the text).
[F17C 2260/032] Avoiding freezing or defrosting -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Purpose consisting in avoiding ice formation on or removing ice from pipes, valves or vessels.
[F17C 2260/033] by enhancing insulation -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Purpose consisting in avoiding heat loss by enhancing thermal insulation.  In this positive example from US4835975, thermal insulation reduces heat transfer on a trailer.
[F17C 2260/035] of fluid -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Purpose in relation with losses of transferred or stored fluid.
[F17C 2260/036] Avoiding leaks -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Purposes in relation with losses of transferred or stored fluid, the solution being to avoid fluid leaks, e.g. by O-rings.  In this positive example from EP2325543, an oxide layer is formed on the inner face of a resin layer to "ensure gas barrier properties of the tank" (in the text).
[F17C 2260/037] Handling leaked fluid -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Purposes in relation with losses of transferred or stored fluid, the solution being to handle the leaked fluid.  In this positive example from GB830171, an conduit extracts leaked fluid, "the loss of fluid being much less great" (in the text).
[F17C 2260/038] Detecting leaked fluid -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Purposes in relation with losses of transferred or stored fluid, the solution detecting leaks.  In this positive example from EP1037269, a leak detector is mounted inside the box.
[F17C 2260/04] Reducing risks and environmental impact -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Purposes to be achieved by the technical features disclosed, these purposes consisting in protecting the environment.
[F17C 2260/042] Reducing risk of explosion -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Purpose consisting in reducing the risks of explosion or limiting the effects of bursting of vessels.  In this positive example from GB2399052, air pressurizes low pressure oxygen on demand "for its safe storage" (in the text).
[F17C 2260/044] Avoiding pollution or contamination -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Purpose consisting in reducing the risks of explosion or limiting the effects of bursting of vessels.  In this positive example from GB2399052, air pressurizes low pressure oxygen on demand "for its safe storage" (in the text).
[F17C 2260/046] Enhancing energy recovery -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Purpose consisting in increasing the energy recovery.
[F17C 2260/048] Refurbishing -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Purpose consisting in simplifying the refurbishing of the pressure vessel, e.g. washing it, repairing it, so as to reuse it.
[F17C 2260/05] Improving chemical properties -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Purpose consisting in improving chemical properties of the handled fluid.  In the positive example from DE102006045031, methanol or/and ethanol is added to natural gas to reduce its freezing point.
[F17C 2260/053] Reducing corrosion -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Purpose consisting in reducing corrosion.  In this positive example from EP0565444, the risk of corrosion - "corrosion of the vessel would take place" (in the text) - is reduced by a sacrificial anode.
[F17C 2260/056] Improving fluid characteristics -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Purpose consisting in improving fluid characteristics, like viscosity, calorific value, etc.
[F17C 2265/00] Purposes or effects -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Effects achieved in relation with gas storage and gas handling.
[F17C 2265/01] Purifying the fluid -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Effects achieved, the effect being purification of the handled fluid.
[F17C 2265/012] by filtering -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Purification of the handled fluid by filtering, e.g. by using a filter.  In this positive example from US2424879, a coarse filter is used.
[F17C 2265/015] by separating -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Purification of the handled fluid by separation.  In this positive example from FR2165729, nitrogen on top is separated from re-liquefied natural gas at the bottom.
[F17C 2265/017] different phases of a same fluid -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Purification of the handled fluid by separation of different phases of the same fluid.  In this positive example from FR2165729, liquid and gaseous phases are separated.
[F17C 2265/02] Mixing fluids -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Effects achieved, the effect being the mixing of the handled fluid.
[F17C 2265/022] identical fluid -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Mixing of the handled fluid, by mixing two or more streams of the same fluid, in the same or different phases.  In this positive example from DE4342210, fluid from the bottom of the tank is added to the top.
[F17C 2265/025] different fluids -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Mixing of two different fluids.  In this positive example from EP2236202, oxygen, helium and/or argon are mixed.
[F17C 2265/027] with odorizing -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Mixing of two different fluids, one of the fluids being the handled fluid, the other an odorising additive, allowing to identify scentless fluids.  In this positive example from EP1712831, THT is added to natural gas.
[F17C 2265/03] Treating the boil-off -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Effects achieved, the effect being to treat boil-off of liquefied gas.  In this positive example from DE3245865, boil-off gas is recycled.  **Glossary:** - Boil-off: Vapour of liquefied gas, evaporating from the free surface of the liquid, due to unintentional heat input into the liquefied gas storage device, and considered as a by-product.
[F17C 2265/031] by discharge -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Treating boil-off by discharge into the environment, e.g. by flaring.  In this positive example from WO9716678, the BOG is flared.
[F17C 2265/032] by recovery -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Treating boil-off so as to recover or recycle it.  In this positive example from US6382240, the boil-off is reused.
[F17C 2265/033] with cooling -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Treating boil-off so as to recover or recycle it, the boil-off being cooled.
[F17C 2265/034] with condensing the gas phase -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Treating boil-off so as to recover or recycle it, the boil-off being cooled and condensed.  In this positive example from DE102010000946, boil-off is cooled and condensed.
[F17C 2265/035] with subcooling the liquid phase -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Treating boil-off so as to recover or recycle it, the boil-off being cooled, re-condensed and sub-cooled.
[F17C 2265/036] with heating -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Treating boil-off so as to recover or recycle it, the boil-off being heated  In this positive example from WO2011061169, the boil-off is heated.
[F17C 2265/037] with pressurising -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Treating boil-off so as to recover or recycle it, the boil-off being pressurized.  In this positive example from WO2006031634, the boil-off is pressurized.
[F17C 2265/038] with expanding -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Treating boil-off so as to recover or recycle it, the boil-off being expanded.  In this positive example from us2007009369, the boil-off is expanded.
[F17C 2265/04] using an independent energy source, e.g. battery -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Effects achieved, the effect being independency by using an autonomous energy source, like a battery or a fuel cell.  In this positive example from WO2010038069, an energy source is mounted on a trailer.
[F17C 2265/05] Regasification -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Effects achieved, the effect being regasification, i.e. industrial scale vaporisation of liquefied gas, mostly LNG.  In this positive example from US2937504, LNG is vaporized after discharge from a ship.
[F17C 2265/06] Fluid distribution -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Effects achieved, the effect being the fluid distribution, the transport from the production site to the user, or intermediate steps thereof.
[F17C 2265/061] for supply of supplying vehicles -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fluid distribution phase from a production or regasification site, starting from very large volumes, e.g. filling of LNG carrier ships at a production site.  In this positive example from US2009115190, liquefied gas is charged on ship at a refinery.
[F17C 2265/063] for supply of refuelling stations -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fluid distribution phase to a fluid supply station, with large volumes, e.g. from a trailer to a fuel supply station.  In this positive example from FR2896302, the fluid is discharged in a refuelling tank above ground.  In this positive example from WO2006008299, the fluid is discharged into an underground refuelling tank.
[F17C 2265/065] for refuelling vehicle fuel tanks -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fluid distribution phase from a refuelling station to a vehicle fuel tank, with small volumes.  In this positive example from WO03006874, the fluid is discharged into a car.
[F17C 2265/066] for feeding engines for propulsion -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fluid distribution phase with details of the transfer from the vehicle fuel tank to the propulsion unit, e.g. in cars or on ships.  In this positive example from US6390075, the fluid distribution inside the car is disclosed.  In this negative example US2003150218, only a standard principle scheme is disclosed.
[F17C 2265/068] Distribution pipeline networks -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Fluid distribution with details of the transfer from vessels into a pipeline or within a pipeline network.  In this positive example from WO9932837, fluid is loaded into a pipeline network.  In this negative example from US3092063, fluid is discharged into a local pipe network on a ship.
[F17C 2265/07] Generating electrical power as side effect -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Effects achieved, the effect being the generation of electrical power as a by-product of gas storage and handling.  In this positive example from EP2405176, electrical power is generated as side effect.
[F17C 2270/00] Applications -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Applications and tasks in relation with pressure vessels for compressed, liquefied or solidified gas.
[F17C 2270/01] for fluid transport or storage -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Applications in relation with storage or transport of compressed, liquefied or solidified gas.
[F17C 2270/0102] on or in the water -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Storage or transport of compressed, liquefied or solidified gas on or in the water.  In this positive example from WO2005032942, LNG is stored/transported in a tanker and then transferred on a floating structure.
[F17C 2270/0105] Ships -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Storage or transport on ships.  In this positive example from FR2116193, liquefied gas is stored and transported in a tanker.
[F17C 2270/0107] Wall panels -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Storage or transport on ships, the ships comprising wall panels.  In this positive example from GB1332820, wall panels are used for insulation.
[F17C 2270/011] Barges -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Storage or transport on barges, i.e. units pulled by ships.  In this positive example from CA2283008, the gas storage system is on top of the deck of the barge.
[F17C 2270/0113] floating -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Storage or transport on barges, the barges floating on the water surface.  In this positive example from WO2004000636, the floating barges can be pulled into a floating haven.
[F17C 2270/0115] immerged -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Storage or transport on barges, the barges being located below water surface.  In this positive example from US2732687, the barge is immersed in the water.
[F17C 2270/0118] Offshore -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Storage offshore, often in flat sea near the coast.  In this positive example from US2009293506, the storage tanks are located in a semi-submersible offshore structure near the coast.
[F17C 2270/0121] Platforms -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Offshore storage on immobile platforms, usually for processing.  In this positive example from US6517286, the offshore gas production platform is situated above the gas source.
[F17C 2270/0123] Terminals -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Offshore storage using terminals, usually for temporary storage.  In this positive example from WO2006039485, LNG is offloaded to a temporary offshore tank.
[F17C 2270/0126] Buoys -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Offshore device using buoys, small floating devices attached to the sea floor for fluid distribution.  In this positive example from US2005042035, LNG is transferred to buoys anchored to the seabed.
[F17C 2270/0128] Storage in depth -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Offshore storage in depth, using vessels or deep sea water pressure and temperature for containment.  In this positive example from EP0728537, liquefied CO 2 is stored in bags at high depth.  In this positive example from DE743163, the vessel is located on the sea floor.
[F17C 2270/0131] Submarines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Storage or transport in submarines.  In this positive example from US3640237, fluid is stored in a large submarine tank.
[F17C 2270/0134] placed above the ground -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Storage of compressed, liquefied or solidified gas on or above the ground.  In this positive example from US3438532, the tank is placed on the ground and supported by pillars.
[F17C 2270/0136] Terminals -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Storage above ground using terminals, e.g. for temporary storage.  In this positive example from US2005061395, fluid is stored in an onshore terminal.
[F17C 2270/0139] Fuel stations -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Storage in refuelling stations for vehicles.  In this positive example from WO02064395, a fuel station for cars is disclosed.  Additionally, gaseous hydrogen is stored above the ground and cryogenic hydrogen is stored underground.
[F17C 2270/0142] placed underground -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Storage of compressed, liquefied or solidified gas at least partially below the ground.  In this positive example from US2117314, the lower tank part is in the ground.  In this positive example from WO02064395, cryogenic hydrogen is stored underground (and gaseous hydrogen above the ground).
[F17C 2270/0144] Type of cavity -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Particular ways of storing below the ground.
[F17C 2270/0147] by burying vessels -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Storing below the ground by at least partially burying an existing vessel.  In this positive example from DE9310032U, the whole vessel is buried in the ground.
[F17C 2270/0149] by digging cavities -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Storing below the ground by using a man-made cavity, e.g. a mine.  In this positive example from GB2435505, an vessel is placed into a mine.
[F17C 2270/0152] Salt caverns -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Storing below the ground by using a man-made cavity, the man-made cavern having previously been a salt cavern which has been purged from salt.  In this positive example from US2003115889, LNG is stored in an uncompensated salt cavern.
[F17C 2270/0155] by using natural cavities -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Storing below the ground by using a natural cavity.  In this positive example from US2005061395, the natural cavity is a former oil bed.
[F17C 2270/0157] Location of cavity -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Location of the cavity.

[F17C 2270/0163] offshore -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Location of the cavity, the location being under sea surface.  In this positive example from US2005061395, the cavity is located under the bottom of the sea.

[F17C 2270/05] for industrial use -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Applications in relation with storage or transport or transfer of compressed, liquefied or solidified gas in industrial fields.
[F17C 2270/0509] "Dewar" vessels -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Storage of liquefied gas in a dewar vessel.  In this positive example from DE102005003284, the Dewar vessel contains nitrogen.
[F17C 2270/0518] Semiconductors -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Storage or transport or transfer in the semiconductor industry.  In this positive example from EP1910733, ammonia vapor is used in a semiconductor manufacturing application.

In this positive example from US2009032133, containers are filled at industrial scale with liquefied carbonic gas.
